summary: Full-codebase architecture audit of Neutron Open: map subsystems, critique cross-cutting dimensions, merge, adversarially verify
logs: Map complete: 17/17 subsystems mapped, 146 debt items collected
Merged to 24 findings; verifying each adversarially...

subsystems.purpose: The repo's written self-knowledge: public product pitch (README), community contracts (CONTRIBUTING/SECURITY), architecture map (docs/SYSTEM-OVERVIEW.md), three shipped-change changelogs (AS-BUILT variants), and the design pipeline (docs/specs -> docs/plans -> docs/research). Critically, large parts are machine-written AND machine-read: trident/Ralph agent prompts hardcode reading/updating root AS-BUILT.md and SPEC.md, and the CI leak-gate structurally polices the docs tree, so doc conventions are effectively an agent API, not passive prose.
debt_titles: [P1] Changelog fragmentation: 3 AS-BUILT files, inconsistent per-PR writes, no authoritative log; root file 493KB and order-violating yet agent-read every Ralph iteration
[P1] Roadmap + key design docs exist only as untracked working-tree files (both 2026-06-30 plans, trident-v2 rearchitecture, 4 parity audits, m1 rounds 3/4/7)

[P1] SYSTEM-OVERVIEW contradicts shipped code (append-mostly rot): boot-path section claims Open boots a /healthz-only shell; web-chat section claims React client is behind a flag with vanilla as default — both false
[P1] Master SPEC.md is external/private; plans cite rotting private line numbers; Ralph prompt expects a root SPEC.md that does not exist in this repo

[P2] Plan/spec lifecycle has no status transitions: credential plan says 'PLAN-ONLY. No code written' after substantially shipping (project-credentials/, tabs/registry.ts:131, SettingsTab.tsx, migration 0092); trident-v2 doc says 'design-ready' after being superseded by the shipped exec-model (which is essentially the Option B-prime the doc REJECTED)
[P2] README stale: lists removed notes Core, omits google-workspace + scraping Cores, claims GBrain 'degrades fail-soft' after install was changed to abort-on-failure
[P2] CONTRIBUTING contradicts testing reality: 'bun test' whole-suite advice OOMs (~775 files >1.2GB RSS per docs/testing-runner.md); root 'bunx tsc --noEmit' misses trident errors (leaf tsconfig)

[P3] Dead doc artifacts: abandoned docs/AS-BUILT.md; prototype .mjs parked in docs/research; stale full repo copy under .claude/worktrees/ (live evidence of the trident doc's worktree-GC coverage hole)
load_bearing: docs/AS_BUILT.md leak-gate exemptions are keyed to the LITERAL path (leak-gate-allowlist.txt:75-79) — merging/renaming the changelog re-arms zero-tolerance tenant-vocab rules against its content and fails CI unless allowlist entries move with it
leak-gate.sh:200 FORBIDDEN_EXACT bans root STATUS.md/ISSUES.md/CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md; .gitignore ignores STATUS.md at ANY depth, so even a tracked docs/STATUS.md would be silently unaddable

trident/Ralph prompts hardcode root AS-BUILT.md and SPEC.md (trident/prompts.ts:113,116,163,199; inner-workflow.mjs:361) — deleting/renaming without prompt updates makes self-builds recreate the file or fail their update step

AS-BUILT prose is the only anchored record of behavioral invariants (one-reply-per-turn, false-completion discipline, provenance-gated APPROVE, paused-not-finished) that the no-functionality-change refactor must verify against — do not discard content when consolidating
docs/AS_BUILT.md deliberately NAMES retired 'tenant' vocabulary as rename evidence — a well-meaning vocabulary scrub destroys the very content the leak-gate exemption exists for
Plans encode Ryan-locked decisions with verbatim quotes (e.g. all_messages default, no-feature-flags locks) that code reviews cite as authority for non-obvious code shapes

subsystems.purpose: The composition root of Neutron Open: resolves single-owner config, opens SQLite + migrations, composes the ~14-module GatewayModuleGraph and the ordered HTTP/WS route chain, binds one Bun.serve listener with systemd watchdog + graceful shutdown, and wires every product surface (onboarding, landing chat, app-ws, ~20 /api/app/* surfaces, uploads, cores, memory, trident, reminders) into one CompositionInput. It is a 4-stage pipeline (entrypoint → GraphComposer → composeProductionGraph → composeHttpHandler → boot()) with TWO stage-1 composers: the in-repo Open composer and an out-of-repo Managed composer injected via the NEUTRON_GRAPH_COMPOSER_MODULE env seam, both built on the shared gateway/realmode-composer/ builder library (~13k LOC).

[P1] boot-helpers.ts grab-bag with an invisible external contract: 8 exports have zero in-repo consumers, live only for the out-of-repo Managed composer
[P1] Adding one HTTP surface requires hand-edits in 4-5 bookkeeping sites (interface field, field-by-field mapping, 31-term hasAnyChainedSurface, ladder step, LANDING_PATHS/gate lists)
[P1] Config resolution smeared across ~25 env vars; open/server.ts mutates process.env as its DI mechanism because boot() reads process.env directly
[P1] Three stacked hand-rolled route ladders on the Open request path with duplicated cookie-mint logic
[P2] Duplicated LLM credential resolvers kept in sync by comment (Open resolveOpenLlmPool mirrors Managed resolve-llm-credentials; 'ambient' kind exists only in one)
[P2] Layering inversion: gateway/cores/mount-open-cores.ts imports up into open/, creating a directory-level cycle with open/composer.ts
[P2] CompositionInput is a ~50-field all-optional god bag: forgetting a field compiles clean and 404s silently (a repeatedly re-fixed regression class)
[P2] 'realmode-composer' (Managed vocabulary) names the shared builder library both modes use; internal_handle/tenant vocabulary aliased at the composer top
[P3] Dual entrypoints duplicate ignition; bun start:gateway on a fresh Open box silently boots healthz-only with the wrong default DB path

composition.ts ordering: replToolBridge registered AFTER mcp (:338); cron.scheduler.start() only AFTER graph.compose() (:349-377, jobs register during module init); then wireCoresSurfaces -> wireConnectOverlay -> buildComposedHttpFromComposition (overlays mutate the composition the HTTP chain is built from)
module-graph topo order is alphabetical-deterministic (module-graph.ts:177-180); get() readiness sentinel allows mid-compose dep reads (:129-142)

shutdown order is fixed: listener stop -> STOPPING=1 -> watchdog clear -> graph.shutdown -> shutdownAllPersistentRepls (orphan-claude fix) -> realmode_cleanups -> db.close (index.ts:385-458); stop() auto-forces under NODE_ENV=test (:357) or bun test hangs
error paths release resources: composeProductionGraph tears down the started graph if HTTP composition throws (composition.ts:413-426); boot() closes db on composer throw (index.ts:236-255)

compose.ts ladder: authGate first (Set-Cookie stitched onto downstream response :894-948); chunked upload BEFORE single-shot import upload; landing path-SET match before connect API; every landing route MUST be in LANDING_PATHS (:722-752) or it 404s — the recurring ISSUES #59 bug class

Open ?start= tokens are single-use (JTI claim, open/composer.ts:1638-1653); cookie minted only on first claim; valid-cookie-but-no-resumable-state cold-starts (:1686-1690, stale-cookie-over-wiped-DB wedge); React bootstrap injected by string-replace on the /chat-react.js script tag (:1616-1626)
prewarm promise NEVER rejects and is not awaited at boot (open/composer.ts:3661-3684); prewarmSettled flag elevates timeouts for every cold-window turn (:508-521); awaitPrewarmReady capped at 35s
substrate instance-id prefixes are pool keys: trident fire substrate must be WARM per-repo-cwd or the detached Workflow aborts on settle (:590-633); only cc-agent- gets enableToolBridge (:535-541)
late-bound holders (dispatchBoardHolder :654, importWatchHolder :1329, onboardingMsgHolder :2321, appWsHolder :2689) rely on fill-before-first-dispatch timing

chained fetch selected per-request inside the Bun.serve arrow so the live server ref reaches WS upgrades (index.ts:309-323); maxRequestBodySize = import cap + 64MB (:302) prevents silent pre-handler 413 on large exports
composeProductionGraph mutates and returns the SAME CompositionInput object reference (composition.ts:81-86); project_slug is frozen at boot (index.ts:184), slug rename = .url_slug file + restart

subsystems.purpose: The per-instance gateway's HTTP/WS surface layer: compose.ts assembles ~30 optional route handlers plus an auth gate into the single { fetch, websocket } pair Bun.serve runs; ~20 app-*-surface.ts modules serve the Expo/web app API over a uniform disclaim-with-null handler contract; chat-bridge.ts is the production ChatBridge wiring the web chat WS to the onboarding engine, live-agent turns, engagement gating, and the process-wide topic-to-socket sender registry. It also (mis)hosts storage primitives like the filesystem DocStore.

subsystems.purpose: The gateway process's boot shell and composition root: opens the per-owner SQLite DB, composes a topo-sorted module graph (~14 modules), binds the single Bun.serve listener, integrates with systemd, and orchestrates ordered shutdown. In practice it has also become the landing zone for ~8 product-domain services (git doc-versioning/backup, inline comments + agent watcher, binary store, resumable uploads, Expo push, proactive briefs/nudges, P6 nudge engine, project settings) plus the Cores install/OAuth/integrations wiring — none of which are gateway concerns.

subsystems.purpose: The onboarding state machine for a fresh install: walks the owner from signup through history import, personality/name capture, project proposal, persona synthesis, credential setup, wow moment, and final handoff. Owns the 18-phase transition table, the InterviewEngine turn driver, the durable onboarding_state store (phase_state JSON), two LLM subsystems (freeform-intent router + phase-prompt copy generator), and the newer "Path 1" live-session onboarding pieces (preamble, post-turn extractor, button-backed answer) that bypass the phase machine for conversation.

subsystems.purpose: Nominally the substrate layer: the locked Substrate.start(spec) -> SessionHandle contract (substrate.ts/session-handle.ts/events.ts) plus the credential pool and model registry every LLM call flows through. In practice it has accreted three more subsystems — the Open/Managed PlatformAdapter seam (~2.5k LOC of split shrapnel), the entity-writer persistence gate + auto-link, and subagent supervision (registry/spawn-guard/watchdog) — plus a util junk drawer (doc-links, system-prompt, atomic-write, constant-time-equal, env-flag parsers).

subsystems.purpose: The three concrete implementations of the locked Substrate seam (start(spec)→SessionHandle). The Claude Code adapter is THE production substrate: one warm interactive `claude` REPL per (instance,user,project,credential) pool key over a PTY, each turn driven through a per-session loopback MCP dev-channel and settled into exactly one token+completion via the enforce-reply Stop hook, plus the full self-healing stack (persisted registry, respawn-is-always-resume, 7-member watchdog family, PTY output-scan recovery detectors, pending-respawn replay/redelivery). The OpenAI Responses and Codex CLI adapters are plan-mandated risk-mitigation siblings with zero production consumers today.

subsystems.purpose: The web chat product surface for a Neutron Open install: a React 19 + assistant-ui SPA (landing/chat-react/) served at GET /chat over the gateway's /ws/app/chat socket via the shared @neutron/chat-core sync engine, plus the landing HTTP server (server.ts) that serves the shell/assets and runtime-bundles the client. The package also doubles as an auth library (session-cookie.ts, auth-gate.ts) consumed by the gateway, and — critically — as the de-facto home of the chat wire-protocol types (ChatOutbound/ChatBridge) consumed across the whole codebase. The legacy vanilla chat client AND the NEUTRON_WEB_CHAT_CLIENT flag were deleted 2026-06-26; React is the only web client (the audit brief's 'behind flag' claim is stale).

subsystems.purpose: The data + memory layer of Neutron Open: SQLite access (ProjectDb wrapper with WAL/mutex/busy-retry over one instance project.db), a forward-only migration runner with a byte-identical schema snapshot test, the GBrain long-term-memory adapter (stdio MCP to the external gbrain binary, sync hook fanning entity-writer pages into a typed-edge graph), the scribe chat-time extraction pipeline, file-based reflection stores, an FTS5 doc-search sidecar, and the canonical tasks/reminders stores with projections and a fire-time tick loop.
debt_titles: [P1] reminders package layer-inverted: imports gateway composition root + landing + channels
[P1] package.json dependency graph is fiction — undeclared cross-workspace relative imports throughout the layer
[P1] Four-plus persistence idioms with no decision rule; ProjectDb.raw() escape hatch bypasses the serialization invariant at ~15 sites including a write
[P2] scribe re-implements entity-writer's private on-disk page format (parsers admit they mirror unexported functions)
[P2] GBrain failure model: silent memory divergence by design; deferred-edge retry queue is RAM-only and lost on restart
[P2] tenant vocabulary baked into schema: project_slug column = INSTANCE slug, project_id = actual project
[P2] Four near-duplicate app-chat stores (~930 LOC) + ~50 hand-rolled store classes repo-wide
[P3] Migration numbering gaps are permanent + stale docs (runner cites nonexistent 0067; AGENTS.md says runner is ~80 LOC)
[P3] Dead/vestigial code and non-workspace module
load_bearing: ProjectDb mutex + AsyncLocalStorage re-entry: tx.run/tx.exec inside transaction callbacks bypass the lock (db.ts:216-226) — naive locking deadlocks; mutex chain rebuilds with swallowing .catch

isBusyError rejects BusyRetryExhaustedError (retry.ts:49-56) — prevents 15x15 outer/inner retry amplification; busy-retry is async (Bun.sleep) so the systemd WATCHDOG=1 tick keeps firing during contention

Migration runner: PRAGMA preamble hoisted outside the per-migration transaction; foreign_keys=ON re-asserted in finally even on throw (runner.ts:89-126); NEVER renumber/backfill gap versions — live _migrations tables would re-apply

GBrainSyncHook: remove_link (predicate-blind) MUST run before add_link so surviving predicates are re-asserted (GBrainSyncHook.ts:199-256); deferred queue drains only when pageLanded; binary-missing latch logs exactly once then short-circuits forever

Scribe append-only merge: never replace a richer existing compiled-truth; frontmatter MERGED not replaced (writer replaces wholesale — losing category:'inferred_interest' is data loss); deterministic timeline body makes repeated turns no-ops (write-to-gbrain.ts:19-41)
Scribe is fire-and-forget off the chat hot path: handleUserTurn never blocks/throws into chat; budget released in finally (scribe/index.ts:236-268)

Reminders tick: single-flight (skip not stack); claim-then-dispatch with revert paths (store.ts:234-293); recurring rows advanceRecurrence INSTEAD of markFired; dispatcher degrades to literalFallback on ANY LLM failure so a reminder always delivers
Reminders outbound persist-before-send: durable button_prompts row first, live push best-effort with swallowed throws (outbound.ts:7-18); deliver via app: registry not web: (PR#105)

Task-reminder link writes in the SAME transaction as the task mutation (reminder-link.ts:11-14); focus-score cron deliberately does NOT trigger projection rewrites (projection/write.ts:17-19); STATUS.md marked-block rewrite preserves owner narrative; tmp+rename atomic
Task list orderings are client-bound contracts: 'default' bound by Expo; 'focus_score' interleaves fresh unranked rows into the last LLM ranking (store.ts:69-89)
doc-search: collapse to best-chunk-per-file BEFORE limit (store.ts:279-291); malformed FTS MATCH returns [] not throw

GBrain OpenAI key resolved LAZILY at each gbrain serve spawn (stdio client resolver thunk) so a key pasted after boot activates embeddings on the next memory op; ensure-brain-init always creates the 3072-dim OpenAI-ready column even keyless (upgrade-in-place; --no-embedding would brick later activation)
GBrainMemoryStore routes empty/blank query to list_pages (search returns nothing for '') — admin Memory tab recent-listing depends on it
Diary appends stay under PIPE_BUF single write(2) — the no-torn-lines guarantee
Boot order: ProjectDb.open -> applyMigrations(db.raw()) -> composer, with init-failure closing DB + shutting down half-composed graph (gateway/index.ts:181-255)

subsystems.purpose: Three concerns: (1) connect/ — dormant cross-instance federation (Slack-Connect model: /connect/v1 API, guest/trusted joins, memory mirror, unified project list); (2) auth/ — the LIVE owner-credential vault (AES-256-GCM SecretsStore + Claude Max paste-token, ChatGPT/Codex device-code OAuth, BYO API key clients); (3) jwt-validator/ — EdDSA JWT validation with JWKS cache, built for federation and only instantiated as an inert placeholder in Open production. Gateway glue (gateway/connect, gateway/http/app-connect-*, gateway/projects/shared-projects-resolver) is complete and tested but almost entirely unwired in Open boot.
debt_titles: [P1] Dormant federation entangled through 4 live modules (STATUS.md blocker #1 confirmed)

[P1] Two parallel HTTP auth gates; the tested one (landing/auth-gate.ts) is unmounted, the mounted one is a bespoke closure inside the 3.7k-line open/composer; documented fail-OPEN branch on mint failure

[P2] Shipped-but-dead client surfaces: connect-relay.ts has zero importers; Expo connect-members UI drives guaranteed-501 routes; app-connect-auth surface + FederatedTokenStore never constructed in production
[P2] runtime/connect-handlers.ts is a 185-line hand-synced shadow copy of connect/api types with deliberate `any` params + `as any` casts at the consumption seam
[P2] Undeclared cross-workspace imports in connect/ (gbrain-memory, channels, runtime not in package.json)
[P2] SecretsStore identity-key rename trap enforced by convention only (SQL column project_slug holds frozen internal_handle; passing url_slug silently loses credentials)
[P3] shared-projects-resolver interleaves unreachable Managed and Open modes via inline ternaries
load_bearing: Engagement gate fail-soft: DB/read error → DEFAULT all_messages, never a dropped turn; tag_gated+no-mention still PERSISTS the user turn and sends a no-render agent_ack to clear typing dots (chat-bridge.ts:2749-2791)
Mention detector quote-guard: fenced/inline code + blockquotes blanked before @neutron match; 'claude' courtesy alias (agent-engagement.ts:70+)
SecretsStore: callers MUST pass frozen internal_handle (column misnamed project_slug); ensureKey REUSES the existing keyfile — regenerating bricks all stored credentials
Max paste-token persists as TWO rows (refresh+access, same value) so the resolver read path short-circuits; probe treats 401-rate-limit/403-quota as VALID token (max-oauth.ts:23-38)

FederatedTokenStore: secret row expires_at=refresh_expires_at → blob self-deletes without a sweeper; refresh-401 → disconnect(); getMemberships decodes WITHOUT signature verification by design (receiver re-verifies)

Open-mode origin slug derives from the token's kind:'user' membership, NOT the local box slug (shared-projects-resolver.ts:164-179, Argus BLOCKER #2); 10s aggregate + 30s per-instance caches; source_errors must never blank the list

Compose-chain null-fallthrough: connect API and app-connect-auth return null for unowned paths; app-connect-auth claims exactly 4 (path,method) pairs so tokenless sibling requests are never 401-shadowed; cookie-not-bearer auth because /callback is a SameSite=Lax OAuth 302; safeRelativePath open-redirect guard

connect/api/server.ts must NEVER be statically imported by composition (Open boots must not load it); HTTP-composition failure after graph.compose() must graph.shutdown() before rethrow (gateway/composition.ts:400-426)
wireConnectOverlay ordering: after cores wiring, before HTTP composition; overlays on_inbound_message only when caller left it undefined AND supplied the factory
auth-gate Accept-header carve-out: non-HTML requests never 302'd to signin (would break Expo bearer calls)
open/composer.ts:1246 inert JwksCache('https://invalid.local/...') is safe only because the HMAC verifier never resolves JWKS keys — an eager-fetching JwksCache breaks Open boot
Timing-safe slug compares (auth-helpers.ts:31) + internal_handle canonicalization before compare (app-connect-auth.ts:160-173)
member-join local_slug allocation must stay inside the member-insert transaction (#108 race fix); stampOriginInstance throws on grammar-violating slugs
Route-code semantics the Expo client branches on: 501 connect_not_configured (app routes) vs 404 (absent federation handlers) vs 429 (edge limiter)

subsystems.purpose: The build/test/CI/install layer of Neutron Open: a partitioned bounded-memory bash test runner (scripts/run-tests.sh, replacing single-process `bun test` which OOMs at ~886 files) with a content-derived PGLite-WASM quarantine lane and a fatal coverage audit; a 4-step CI gate chain (install → root tsc → runner → leak-gate purity subset); the tsconfig topology; the self-host lifecycle scripts (install/uninstall/service/backup/CLI); and the @neutronai/prompts workspace (live disk-sourced agent prompt bodies + strict template resolver).
debt_titles: [P1] CI typecheck coverage is accidental: root tsc include list omits trident/app/agent-dispatch/jwt-validator/project-credentials/work-board and most of landing; leaf tsconfigs never run in CI; test files in those dirs never typechecked
[P1] dependency-cruiser is a devDependency with NO config and NO invocation — zero import/layer rules enforced; the only architecture fences are one grep-walk test and the shell leak-gate

[P2] The gate scripts are themselves untested and the 'never silent' coverage audit has fail-open branches (empty BUN_DISC skips the cross-check; PGLite lane ran-count falls back to NPGLITE); the discovery probe reloads all 886 files in one process
[P2] Root deploy-gate tsconfig grants DOM lib to all server code, and leaf configs fragment across two extends-roots
[P2] Three test-placement conventions; tests/integration is a 17k-line grab-bag mixing package-owned tests (migration roundtrips, installer tests) with genuine cross-package flows
[P2] install.sh is a 1,499-line five-cluster monolith; minimal dotenv reader duplicated 3x across install.sh/neutron-service.sh/neutron-backup.sh

[P3] Doc/contract drift: CONTRIBUTING tells contributors to run bare `bun test` (the documented OOM path) and never mentions run-tests.sh or the leak-gate; hardcoded suite counts stale in 3 places; prompts/AGENTS.md claims the dir is empty

[P3] Hygiene gaps vs world-class baseline: committed .pyc, no repo-wide lint/format config, no bun version pin for devs, single Linux-only workflow (launchd paths untested in CI), floating non-workspace source dirs (open/tabs/work-board/project-credentials) consumed via relative imports
load_bearing: grep -a + LC_ALL=C in run-tests.sh log parsing (run-tests.sh:132-133,175-179): chunk logs contain NUL bytes; without -a the coverage audit false-fatals on green runs

PGLite lane runs AFTER general chunks, serially (--max-concurrency=1), membership = literal substring 'pglite' in file content; whole-lane retry x2 while general chunks get NO retry (asymmetric by design)
bunfig [test].preload env scrub must run in every spawned bun test process — ~48 onboarding tests depend on a clean CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN
CI ordering: bun install BEFORE tsc (workspace symlinks) and concurrency keyed on PR number not ref (#321, pinned by scripts/ci/ci-workflow.test.ts)

bun pinned 1.3.9 because discover-test-files.sh mirrors bun's dot-dir/node_modules discovery exclusions (keeps ~6810 .claude/worktrees clone tests out); drift is fatal by design — fix the mirror, don't loosen the check

prompts/*.md are live contract bodies with SILENT fail-soft: trident/prompts.ts:80-98 degrades to a terse inline contract if forge.md/argus.md fail to load — moving prompts/ breaks nothing loudly except prompts-disk-source.test.ts; loadPrompt resolves via import.meta.url never cwd
leak-gate has no skip flag/env bypass and refuses an empty scan; committed allowlist is the only exception mechanism
bin/neutron resolves CODE_DIR through its own symlink chain (resolve_self); parallel run_chunk appends rely on short atomic O_APPEND writes to one results file

findings.title: The declared five-layer architecture does not exist in the import graph and nothing enforces it: 28-module SCC, 11 removable back-edges, dependency-cruiser is a dead devDependency
findings.diagnosis: Measured over all 1,745 source files, Tarjan SCC puts 28 of ~44 modules (gateway, open, runtime, onboarding, landing, connect, migrations, cores, trident, scribe, ...) in ONE cycle; the tangle hangs on exactly 11 removable edges, mostly a single stranded constant/type each (runtime imports onboarding's ALL_PHASES; migrations imports open's resolveOpenDbPath; landing re-exports onboarding's MOBILE_APP_URL; gateway/cores/mount-open-cores value-imports open/agent-profile-backend while open/composer imports it back; http<->realmode-composer cycle). Simulation confirms cutting the 11 edges yields a zero-cycle 8-level DAG. Meanwhile dependency-cruiser@17.4.3 sits configless and never invoked, and the repo's one completed god-file split (EngineInternals: a 300-line friend interface with 165 `self.` refs) proves splits silently re-tangle without a ratchet — the failure mode the whole refactor must not repeat.
findings.proposal: (a) Add .dependency-cruiser.cjs: 5 layer bands (contracts→platform→services→product→composition), no-cycles, cores-use-sdk-only, nobody-imports-composition; grandfather today's violations via a baseline and fail CI only on NEW edges, burning the baseline down. (b) Land the 11-edge cut list as ~6 small PRs (constant/type moves + one parameter injection for mount-open-cores, re-export shims for one release). (c) Adopt split done-criteria as refactor policy: extracted modules never receive the host object (max ~4 narrow capability interfaces), friend-interface entries + delegators deleted in the same PR, invariant comments travel verbatim, tests move with code, plus a rule forbidding extracted modules from importing their former host.
findings.evidence: SCC + cut simulation (layering report §2); runtime/onboarding-conversational-flag.ts:24; migrations/runner.ts:6; landing/server.ts:41; gateway/cores/mount-open-cores.ts:48 vs open/composer.ts:110; gateway/http/chat-bridge.ts:116 vs realmode-composer/build-landing-stack.ts:44; package.json:57 (configless dependency-cruiser); onboarding/interview/engine-internals.ts:2020-2035

verification.notes: Independently reproduced the core claims with a fresh directory-level import scan + Tarjan SCC (script at scratchpad/audit/scc.ts, scc-prod.ts, scc-full.ts). (1) SCC: 1,740 .ts files scanned (critic said 1,745 — match within skip-set differences); ONE SCC of exactly 28 modules whose membership matches the critic's list verbatim (agent-dispatch, app, auth, channels, connect, cores, cron, doc-search, gateway, gbrain-memory, landing, mcp, message-search, migrations, onboarding, open, persistence, project-credentials, reflection, reminders, runtime, scribe, skill-forge, tasks, tools, trident, watchdog, work-board). (2) Cut claim: weighted local-search feedback-arc-set finds a 7-edge / 11-import-statement cut that makes the PRODUCTION graph a DAG (migrations→open, landing→onboarding, tasks→onboarding, runtime→onboarding, reminders→gateway, onboarding→gateway, gateway→open); full graph incl. tests needs 12 edges / 22 imports. Critic's "exactly 11" is within heuristic noise. (3) Every cited edge verified at exact lines: runtime/onboarding-conversational-flag.ts:24 imports ALL_PHASES from onboarding/interview/phase.ts; migrations/runner.ts:6 imports resolveOpenDbPath from open/owner-identity.ts; landing/server.ts:41 re-exports MOBILE_APP_URL from onboarding/interview/final-handoff-config.ts; gateway/cores/mount-open-cores.ts:48 value-imports open/agent-profile-backend.ts while open/composer.ts:110 imports mountOpenCores back; gateway/http/chat-bridge.ts:116 static-imports realmode-composer/build-onboarding-handoff.ts while realmode-composer/build-landing-stack.ts:38-44 imports chat-bridge.ts. (4) dependency-cruiser 17.4.3 at package.json:57 (devDependencies); no .dependency-cruiser.* config exists; zero invocations in scripts/, .github/, or package.json scripts — dead dep confirmed. (5) EngineInternals: 303-line interface at onboarding/interview/engine-internals.ts:2027; engine-import-routing.ts has 167 `self.` refs (critic said 165) plus engine-persona.ts has 72 more — the re-tangle claim is if anything understated. engine.ts is 10,078 lines. (6) Bonus evidence the critic missed: gateway/http/chat-bridge.ts:294-301 contains a comment declaring the invariant "the http layer never takes a static realmode-composer import edge" — violated 180 lines earlier in the same file at :116. The layering invariant already exists as prose and is already broken; only a ratchet fixes that class. (7) Feasibility: mountOpenCores has exactly one production caller (open/composer.ts) and uses buildOpenAgentProfileBackend lazily inside the function body (mount-open-cores.ts:300), so parameter injection is mechanical; gateway/cores/__tests__/mount-open-cores.test.ts exists. landing/__tests__/mobile-app-url-constant.test.ts greps for the URL literal and asserts single definition — a constant move MUST update this test (it fails loudly, catching drift). Severity: P0 is honest for a refactor-mission audit — this is the enforcement precondition for every other finding (proven by EngineInternals re-tangle + the chat-bridge comment-vs-code contradiction); it is a maintainability P0, not a runtime P0.
verification.corrected_diagnosis: Directionally accurate with two factual tightenings. First, the 28-module SCC is measured WITH test files; production-code-only the SCC is 19 modules (still catastrophic — gateway, open, runtime, onboarding, landing, connect, migrations, cores, trident, scribe all inside). The two numbers describe different graphs and the finding conflates them: on the full 1,745-file graph the minimal cut is ~12 edges / ~22 import statements, and roughly HALF of those cut edges are cross-package __tests__ imports (scribe/__tests__→gateway/http/chat-bridge.ts, gbrain-memory/__tests__↔mcp/server.ts, runtime/__tests__→scribe, runtime/persistent/__tests__→mcp/server.ts) which constant/type moves do NOT fix — they need test relocation or an explicit test-edge policy in the ruleset. Production-only, the cut is 7 edges totaling 11 single-file imports, matching the "single stranded constant/type each" characterization exactly. Second, the strongest evidence for "nothing enforces it" is one the critic missed: gateway/http/chat-bridge.ts:294-301 documents the invariant "the http layer never takes a static realmode-composer import edge" while line 116 of the same file takes exactly that edge — the layering rule already exists as a comment and is already violated. Also minor: 165 self. refs undercounts — it is 167 in engine-import-routing.ts plus 72 in engine-persona.ts (239 total across two friend-interface consumers of the 303-line EngineInternals at engine-internals.ts:2027).
verification.corrected_proposal: Proposal is sound; three amendments. (1) The dependency-cruiser ruleset must include an explicit test-file policy (either exempt __tests__ edges from layer bands while still forbidding NEW prod cycles, or schedule test moves as separate PRs) — otherwise the baseline never burns down because ~half the full-graph cycle edges are test imports that the 6 constant-move PRs won't touch. (2) Behavior-preservation guards for the edge-cut PRs: (a) MOBILE_APP_URL move must update landing/__tests__/mobile-app-url-constant.test.ts (it greps all .ts for the literal and asserts a single definition — it will fail loudly on a naive move; keep the re-export shim at landing/server.ts:41 so importers are unbroken); (b) migrations/runner.ts:6 resolveOpenDbPath must move VERBATIM — any drift silently migrates a different/empty SQLite file, and no test pins the resolved path; (c) mount-open-cores injection is low-risk (single caller open/composer.ts:110, lazy use at mount-open-cores.ts:300) but shifts backend construction from inside mountOpenCores to the composer — verify buildOpenAgentProfileBackend has no order-sensitive construction side effects and keep gateway/cores/__tests__/mount-open-cores.test.ts green; (d) do NOT convert any static import in the cycle files to dynamic import as a shortcut — ESM eval order in these cycles is load-bearing (chat-bridge deliberately uses structural types + runtime injection to avoid exactly this, per its :294-301 comment) and a boot smoke test (bun run open/server.ts) should gate each PR. (3) Simpler alternative worth considering before adopting dependency-cruiser: a ~100-line in-repo bun test that scans imports, computes the module DAG, and diffs against a committed edge baseline — zero new tooling/config, runs in `bun test` with everything else, and matches the repo's existing invariant-guard-test culture (mobile-app-url-constant.test.ts is precisely this pattern). Since dependency-cruiser is already a (dead) dep, either works, but the bun-test ratchet has no config-drift risk and can encode the test-edge policy trivially.
verification.behavior_risk: Riskiest change: the migrations/runner.ts resolveOpenDbPath extraction — a path-resolution drift makes `bun run migrations/runner.ts` create/migrate a different SQLite file than the gateway opens, a silent data-level failure no existing test pins (tests boot in-memory/temp DBs). Second: the mount-open-cores parameter injection shifts agent-profile-backend construction timing into the composer; covered by gateway/cores/__tests__/mount-open-cores.test.ts plus composer boot, so likely caught. Third: any static→dynamic import conversion in cycle members could reorder ESM module evaluation (TDZ at boot — loud, caught by a server-boot smoke test, but only if one gates the PR). The dependency-cruiser/CI part of the proposal is zero-runtime-risk.

findings.title: No golden composed-surface manifest and a 67-field all-optional CompositionInput: adding one HTTP surface costs 8-10 hand edits, forgotten fields 404 silently, and the mapping/gate tables have already diverged
findings.diagnosis: One new surface requires coordinated edits across compose.ts (interface, destructure, hand-placed ladder branch), composition.ts (mapping AND hasAnyChainedSurface), the input split file, open/composer.ts's 190-line return literal, and the Nth copy of resolveBearer — empirically paid 3x in 3 weeks (#127/#149/#167-169). All fields optional means a dropped slice compiles clean and 404s in production — a documented, repeatedly re-fixed regression class (three in-code incident comments). The mapping and gate are ALREADY out of sync (chat_history/chat_topics/import_resume mapped but ungated), nine surfaces have zero production setters with nothing saying whether that is scoping or drift, and today's only safety net is ~69 per-outage boot tests each pinning one route. Compounding: 19 byte-identical resolveBearer copies, 15 jsonError, 12 readJsonBody, 21 structurally identical handler-slot interfaces (~1,000 duplicated LOC) — a bearer-parse security fix is 19 synchronized edits.
findings.proposal: Before any composer split: (1) golden composed-surface manifest test — boot the Open composer once, probe every route (method × path × auth-state), snapshot {route→status-class,gated?} like migrations/expected-schema.txt; (2) reflection test pinning mapping ↔ gate field equivalence, fixing the divergence as an explicit tested commit; (3) data-driven RouteSlot registry ({key, match, handler, gated, order}) generating the mapping, gate, and ladder, with the current ladder order encoded verbatim and a transition test asserting generated == today's literals; (4) OpenComposition = Base & Required<Pick<...>> on the composer return so a dropped field fails tsc, plus an explicit owner decision on the nine unwired surfaces; (5) gateway/http/surface-kit.ts (resolveBearer with ownerIdentityMismatch folded in, jsonError/jsonOk, readJsonBody, one HandlerSlot type) with byte-identical {ok:false,code,message} wire responses.
findings.evidence: gateway/composition.ts:137-296 (mapping vs gate divergence); gateway/http/compose.ts:722-752 (LANDING_PATHS + 3 incident comments), :950-1320; open/composer.ts:1757-1764,3426-3613; app-tasks-surface.ts:415-435 vs app-docs-surface.ts:1638 vs work-board-surface.ts:322 (byte-identical resolveBearer, 19 files); 45+24 per-outage boot tests

verification.notes: Checked every evidence pointer at HEAD. (1) Mapping/gate divergence CONFIRMED: gateway/composition.ts maps chat_history_surface (:155-157), chat_topics_surface (:158-160), import_resume_handler (:173-175) into composeInput, but hasAnyChainedSurface (:264-295) omits all three; no test anywhere references hasAnyChainedSurface (grep: only composition.ts). Caveat: the divergence is LATENT, not production-reachable — the sole production caller is gateway/index.ts:228 fed by open/composer.ts, whose return literal (open/composer.ts:3426-3613, verified span) always sets landing_server, so the gate always passes. (2) Field counts CONFIRMED with a nuance: CompositionInput is 67 fields across 11 per-concern slices (gateway/composition/input/*: 59 optional + 8 required — project_slug, db, notifier fields, platform). "All-optional" is overstated for the interface but true for every HTTP/app surface field, and the composer's declared return type is Promise<CompositionInput> (open/composer.ts:401), so a dropped surface field compiles clean — the 404 class is real and documented in-code: open/composer.ts:1757-1764 and 1772-1780 ("THE BUG (Ryan, dogfooding)" x2, topics then history), plus compose.ts:731-751 LANDING_PATHS entries citing the ISSUES #59/#208 bug class. (3) Per-surface hand-edit count CONFIRMED: compose.ts has ~20 structurally identical one-field handler interfaces (:37-261, e.g. AppLauncherHandler/AppUploadHandler/AppTasksHandler/AppRemindersHandler/AppBackupsHandler all exactly `{handler:(req)=>Promise<Response|null>}`), a 38-name destructure (:834-872), and a hand-ordered ladder (:950-1320+); plus composition.ts mapping+gate, input slice, and the composer return literal. PR recurrence verified in git log: #127 (work-board), #149 (project-credentials), #167/#169 (codex-credential) each added a surface. (4) Duplication CONFIRMED with correction: resolveBearer in exactly 19 gateway/http surface files; md5 of bodies shows 16/19 byte-identical and 3 formatting-only variants (app-tabs var rename, app-backups line-wrapping, codex-credential one-line signature) — all semantically identical, so "19 byte-identical" → "19 semantically identical (16 byte-identical)". jsonError 15 copies (bodies identical {ok:false,code,message}), readJsonBody 12 copies. Note the proposal's ownerIdentityMismatch already lives centralized in gateway/http/auth-helpers.ts:91 (imported by chat-history/chat-topics surfaces) — a shared-helper seam already exists and works; surface-kit should extend it, not re-fold it. (5) "Nine unwired surfaces" CONFIRMED exactly: app_focus, app_focus_current, app_admin, app_persona, app_devices, app_backups, app_launcher, app_reminders, app_connect_auth have zero setters in any production path in this repo (open/, gateway/index.ts, realmode-composer/, landing/) — plausibly Managed-only scoping, and nothing in code says so, so the ambiguity claim is fair. (6) Test-count claims are the sloppiest part: I count 16 *production-composer*.test.ts files in gateway/__tests__ (~122 it() cases) and 8 open/__tests__ *wiring* tests (~18 cases); the critic's "45+24"/"~69" doesn't match my file counts, but the characterization (per-outage, one-route-each, no golden manifest) is accurate, and migrations/expected-schema.txt exists as the cited precedent. Severity: P0 is honest AS A REFACTOR PREREQUISITE (recurring regression class + planned composer split + no manifest safety net); the mapping/gate divergence itself would only be P2 as a standing defect. Behavior-preservation risks in the proposal: step 2's divergence fix IS a functionality change in an unreachable corner (chain would build where it previously returned null) — must stay an explicit flagged commit; step 3 (RouteSlot registry) is the risky piece because the ladder is heterogeneous, with load-bearing subtleties a uniform registry flattens: auth-gate pre-pass + Set-Cookie stitching wrapping dispatchRequest (compose.ts:894-948), appWs handler taking (req, server) for WS upgrade (:1078-1081), exact-match vs disclaiming-null slot semantics, handleCacheInvalidate wrapper (:969), LANDING_PATHS + root-with-?invite special case + isSpaClientRoute (:754-766, :806-818), and orderings: devMintSession first (:959), chunkedUpload before importResume before importUpload (:1047-1072), appTabs/appWorkBoard/appProjectCredentials/appCodexCredential BEFORE appProjects (:1125-1168), appFocusCurrent before appFocus (:1184-1195). "Transition test asserting generated == today's literals" cannot literally compare closures — it must be dispatch-equivalence, which is exactly the step-1 golden manifest, so step 1 MUST land before step 3.
verification.corrected_diagnosis: Directionally and mostly factually accurate; four corrections. (a) CompositionInput is 67 fields but 8 are required (project_slug, db, notifiers, platform) — the accurate claim is that every HTTP/app SURFACE field is optional and the Open composer's return type is the fully-optional CompositionInput (open/composer.ts:401), so a dropped surface slice compiles clean and 404s. (b) resolveBearer: 16 of 19 copies are byte-identical; the other 3 differ only in formatting/variable names — semantically 19 identical copies, and an ownerIdentityMismatch helper ALREADY exists centralized in gateway/http/auth-helpers.ts:91 (used by chat-history/chat-topics), proving the consolidation seam works. (c) The mapping/gate divergence (chat_history/chat_topics/import_resume mapped at composition.ts:155-175 but absent from hasAnyChainedSurface :264-295) is real but LATENT: the only production caller (gateway/index.ts:228 via open/composer.ts) always sets landing_server, so the gate always passes today — it is a refactor tripwire, not an active bug. (d) Test counts: 16 production-composer test files (~122 cases) + 8 open wiring tests, not "45+24"; the characterization (per-outage, one-route-each, no golden manifest, zero tests touching hasAnyChainedSurface) is correct. The nine zero-setter app surfaces (focus, focus_current, admin, persona, devices, backups, launcher, reminders, connect_auth) verify exactly. The 3x-in-3-weeks recurrence verifies against git log (#127, #149, #167/#169 each added one surface through the full 8-file edit chain).
verification.corrected_proposal: Keep steps 1, 2, 4, 5 as written — they are cheap, test/type-only, and behavior-preserving: (1) golden manifest test first (open/__tests__ already boot the real composer via buildOpenGraphComposer, so this is feasible today); (2) mapping↔gate reflection test, with the divergence fix isolated as an explicitly-flagged commit since adding the three fields to hasAnyChainedSurface changes null-vs-chain semantics in a currently-unreachable configuration; (4) OpenComposition = CompositionInput & Required<Pick<...>> on the composer return type is zero-runtime-risk and kills the dropped-slice 404 class at compile time — arguably the single highest-value/lowest-cost item; (5) surface-kit should EXTEND the existing gateway/http/auth-helpers.ts (ownerIdentityMismatch already lives there) and must keep resolveBearer returning failure OBJECTS (call sites choose status codes) with the already-uniform {ok:false,code,message} jsonError body. Downgrade step 3 (RouteSlot registry) from prerequisite to optional follow-up: the ladder's slot semantics are heterogeneous (auth-gate pre-pass + Set-Cookie stitch wrapping dispatch, appWs (req,server) upgrade signature, exact-match vs disclaiming-null contracts, LANDING_PATHS/?invite/SPA-route special cases, five load-bearing precedence pairs) and a uniform registry is where behavior actually breaks. A simpler 80/20 alternative: keep the hand ladder, and add a reflection test asserting every field of the http-surfaces/app-surfaces input slices appears in BOTH the composition.ts mapping and hasAnyChainedSurface, plus the step-1 manifest — that closes the forgotten-field and gate-drift classes without regenerating the ladder. If the registry is still wanted, it must land strictly AFTER the golden manifest, and its transition test should be dispatch-equivalence against the manifest snapshot, not literal-code comparison. Also fold in an explicit owner decision comment (or removal) for the nine never-set app surface fields.
verification.behavior_risk: Riskiest: step 3's generated route ladder silently changing precedence or slot semantics — e.g. appProjects matching /api/app/projects/<id>/work-board before the work-board slot, importUpload swallowing /api/import/<id>/resume, losing the auth-gate Set-Cookie stitch (compose.ts:935-947), or breaking the appWs (req,server) upgrade path. Existing per-outage composer tests would catch some orderings (~122 cases) but nothing tests the gate stitch ordering or hasAnyChainedSurface; only the step-1 golden manifest (probing method x path x auth-state) would catch the full class, which is why it must land first. Secondary: the gate-divergence fix changes null-vs-chain boot semantics for compositions with only chat_history/chat_topics/import_resume set — unreachable via the shipped Open composer but a functionality change under the audit's no-change mandate, so it needs its own flagged commit.

findings.title: The composition root is a 3,220-line closure with four silent-no-op mutable holders, per-request security code inlined, and no home for product features — every feature PR appends to the god closure
findings.diagnosis: buildOpenGraphComposer (open/composer.ts:396-3615) wires ~15 clusters (5 substrate pools, memory, uploads, cookie/start-token gate + HTML string-replace, ~20 app surfaces, a bespoke app-ws receiver) in one closure; openFetch — the hottest security path in Open — is an anonymous closure testable only by booting everything. Four late-bound holders encode invisible fill-before-first-dispatch timing and silently no-op if dereferenced early. Measured composer growth is +19 to +38 lines per feature PR; product logic lands in gateway/realmode-composer (agentic kickoff, 459 lines, importing upward into onboarding) or floating root dirs because features have no home. The good DI container (module-graph, topo order, reverse shutdown) hosts only 14 infra modules; buildLandingStack is a second 34-field god sub-root; cross-cutting seams (cron jobs, telemetry sinks, prompt fragments) each require hand edits to central files.
findings.proposal: Behind the finding-2 golden manifest: carve along existing section anchors into ~12 wireX(ctx) modules each returning a typed Partial<CompositionInput> slice, registered via a defineFeature({name, deps, services, surfaces, cron?, cleanup?}) convention on the existing module graph so product features get topo-ordered init/shutdown; composer becomes a ~150-line order-preserving orchestrator. Convert holders to a late<T>(name) seam that THROWS before bind(). Extract openFetch verbatim into a named, unit-tested OpenOwnerGate. Split buildLandingStack's input bag into sub-builders. Re-home build-project-kickoff and project-credentials/ as the pattern-setting first moves. Then apply the same extract-verbatim recipe to the second-tier god files: project-backup-store (all five mutex maps stay in the facade; share git-exec with doc-version-store), email backend (barrel split, MIME/header-injection isolated), admin.tsx (free pane moves — the template PR), docs.tsx (per-cluster hooks + a structural RequestGate primitive).
findings.evidence: open/composer.ts:396-3615, holders :654,1329,2321,2689, openFetch :1655-1755, prewarm :3661-3693; gateway/module-graph.ts:177-180 vs build-core-modules.ts:153-727; gateway/realmode-composer/build-landing-stack.ts:93-440; build-project-kickoff.ts:1-64; composer diffs in ca78517/dbb2901/6140b07/3c252c6

verification.notes: Checked every citation at HEAD. (1) God closure: buildOpenGraphComposer spans open/composer.ts:396 to ~3616 of a 3,732-line file (~3,220 lines) — confirmed via grep of the export at :396 and the closing return block ending at :3616; prewarmSubstrate at :3661 and awaitPrewarmReady at :3693 as cited. (2) All four holder line numbers are exactly right: dispatchBoardHolder :654, importWatchHolder :1329, onboardingMsgHolder :2321, appWsHolder :2689. Silent-no-op confirmed at call sites: `dispatchBoardHolder.store?.get(...) ?? null` (:657-664), `importWatchHolder.watch?.(input.user_id)` (:1333), `onboardingMsgHolder.emit?.(input) ?? Promise.resolve()` (:2347, :2882), `void appWsHolder.adapter?.send(msg)` (:2731). BUT the composer's own comments document this as deliberate fail-soft with a fill-before-first-dispatch argument (:648-653, :2324-2330, :2685-2688) — see corrected_proposal. (3) openFetch is an anonymous closure at :1655-1755 doing cookie gate + single-use start-token claim + HTML string-replace injection (withReactBootstrap :1610-1631 literally string-replaces the <script> tag). "Testable only by booting everything" confirmed: open/__tests__/open-start-token-single-use.test.ts:91 exercises it via full buildOpenGraphComposer + composeProductionGraph boot (as do ~30 other open/__tests__ boot tests) — note it IS tested, just only at boot granularity, which conversely means a verbatim extraction has a real safety net. (4) DI container: gateway/module-graph.ts has cycle detection (:163-166) and deterministic alphabetical-seeded topo (:177-180); build-core-modules.ts hosts exactly 14 modules (:153,228,233,239,249,266,281,326,434,463,515,661,676,727) — but the file lives at gateway/composition/build-core-modules.ts, NOT gateway/realmode-composer/ (minor citation error; :153-727 range correct). (5) buildLandingStack input bag: the critic UNDERSTATED — BuildLandingStackInput has 64 fields, not 34 (interface starting build-landing-stack.ts:93; file is 1,529 lines). (6) Upward import confirmed and broader than claimed: build-project-kickoff.ts (459 lines, matches) imports onboarding/history-import + onboarding/wow-moment (:56-61), and 12 realmode-composer files carry ~60 `from '../../onboarding` import lines (build-onboarding-finalize.ts has 11). (7) Growth measurement confirmed: git show on ca78517 (+27), dbb2901 (+25), 6140b07 (19 changed/+12 net), 3c252c6 (38 changed/+26 net) — the "+19 to +38" range is honest gross-lines-touched. (8) One overstatement: "~20 app surfaces" — the return block registers 9 app_*_surface entries plus ~6 other HTTP surfaces (chat_topics :1766, chat_history :1782, etc.); call it ~15. (9) Proposal gap: "carve along existing section anchors" — the `── section ──` anchors exist only from :440 to :2249 (19 anchors); the last ~1,370 lines (app-ws receiver, work board, credential surfaces, return assembly) have NO anchors, and that tail is the most entangled region (the on_session_open/receiver closures capture dozens of locals, :3280-3345). Severity P0 is defensible for a maintainability-mission audit: the composition root is the measured chokepoint every feature PR appends to, and the appWsHolder cycle (adapter↔receiver, :2685-2731) proves the module graph as-is cannot host these clusters without a late-bind seam.
verification.corrected_diagnosis: Accurate as written with four corrections: (a) build-core-modules.ts is at gateway/composition/, not gateway/realmode-composer/; (b) buildLandingStack's input bag is 64 fields, not 34 (worse than claimed); (c) the upward gateway→onboarding coupling is not just build-project-kickoff — ~60 import lines across 12 realmode-composer files reach into onboarding/*, so re-homing one file does not fix the layer inversion; (d) "~20 app surfaces" is really 9 app_*_surface entries plus ~6 other surfaces. Also a framing correction: openFetch is not untested — it has boot-level coverage (open-start-token-single-use.test.ts, open-doc-deeplink-serve.test.ts, open-boot-shell.test.ts), which is exactly what makes verbatim extraction safe. And the four holders are not merely "invisible" debt: each carries an explicit comment arguing why the silent no-op is safe (fill happens during synchronous-ish composition, dereference happens post-boot), i.e. the silent-no-op is a documented fail-soft contract, not an accident.
verification.corrected_proposal: Three material corrections. (1) DROP the "late<T> THROWS before bind()" conversion or soften it to log-and-no-op: throwing is a behavior CHANGE that contradicts the no-functionality-change mandate. The holders sit inside fire-and-forget runtime paths (onboardingMsgHolder.emit?.() at :2347/:2882 is called from the import-completion watcher and finalize, which can run detached; importWatchHolder.watch?.() fires on every upload). Today a pre-bind dereference degrades silently (opening message lost, later recovered by ensureProjectOpeningOnEntry :3329); a throw becomes an unhandled rejection in a detached async path — potentially a boot crash if any resume/watcher path ever fires early. If a loud seam is wanted, make it log-once, and add throw-before-bind only under a test-only flag. (2) The carve cannot rely on declared-deps topo order alone: appWsHolder exists because adapter↔receiver is a genuine cycle that module-graph's cycle detector (module-graph.ts:163-166) would reject, and the graph's alphabetical-seeded order (:177-180) differs from the composer's textual order, which encodes undeclared constraints (realmodeCleanups registered before buildLandingStack per :1320-1322; prewarm promise awaited via awaitPrewarmReady before the first conversational dispatch per :3644-3660). The orchestrator must preserve explicit call order and keep a late-bind seam for the true cycle; "order-preserving" must be a hard rule, not a nicety. (3) Cut scope: the trailing sentence bolting on project-backup-store, email backend, admin.tsx, and docs.tsx is a separate finding wearing this one's severity — splitting it keeps this PR-able. Simpler first moves that de-risk the XL: extract openFetch + withReactBootstrap + claimStartToken verbatim into a named module (already covered by boot tests, zero ordering risk), and adopt defineFeature for NEW features only to stop the +19..+38/PR bleed, migrating existing clusters opportunistically instead of a big-bang 12-module carve — especially since the last 1,370 lines have no section anchors to carve along and would need boundary invention inside the most closure-entangled code.
verification.behavior_risk: Riskiest: converting silent-no-op holders to throw-before-bind. A pre-bind dereference in a detached path (import-completion watcher finalizing while wiring is mid-flight, or an early upload calling importWatchHolder.watch) turns a documented silent degrade into an unhandled rejection/boot crash. No existing test exercises pre-bind dereference (all ~30 open/__tests__ boot the full composer first), so this regression would ship undetected. Second: re-ordering construction via graph topo order — the prewarm-awaited-before-first-dispatch invariant (composer.ts:3644-3660) and realmodeCleanups-before-buildLandingStack ordering (:1320) are timing contracts no unit test asserts; breaking them re-introduces the cold-spawn-inside-conversational-timeout failure the comment says was deliberately fixed 2026-06-18. openFetch extraction itself is low-risk (verbatim + covered by open-start-token-single-use.test.ts and open-doc-deeplink-serve.test.ts, which assert single-use token claim and cookie-mint semantics end-to-end).

findings.title: onboarding/interview/engine.ts: 10k-line god class behind a failed friend-interface split, with ~1.6k dead lines and its shared phase_state JSON contract unpinned by any test
findings.diagnosis: InterviewEngine has ~102 methods including a 615-line spec resolver, an 887-line consumeChoice dispatcher, and a 772-line start(); the prior extraction moved 4.5k lines into satellites taking `self: EngineInternals`, so every signature change needs 3 coordinated edits. ~1.6k lines are unreachable in Open (acceptChoice has zero production callers; the slug flow is Managed-only). phase_state — the JSON grab-bag every extracted module will touch — is written via THREE mechanisms (store JS shallow-merge, resume-cron SQL json_remove, engine LLM-output whitelist merge) and raw-read by composer/upload/cores; 58 test files mention it but none pins the key registry or merge semantics, and no test covers the Path-1 app-ws ↔ chat-bridge-engine shared-store interplay. The split can fracture crash-resume watermarks while staying green.
findings.proposal: Sequence: (0) write down the Path-1 fork decision (which architecture the engine serves); (1) delete acceptChoice + the slug flow, migrating tests to advance; (2) BEFORE splitting, land a typed phase_state codec with a grep-derived key registry, a round-trip golden over a representative corpus, and one integration test driving both architectures against the same sqlite store; (3) move ImportJobRunnerHook/LlmCallFn/profile-pic hooks to the contracts leaf; (4) carve flow modules along the verified cluster map (SpecResolution, FinalHandoff, Wow, GapFill, Resume, then the advance/router core), each taking 2-4 narrow capability interfaces with EngineInternals shrinking monotonically; (5) collapse the six parallel per-phase tables into one exhaustiveness-checked descriptor. Positional invariants (buttonStore.resolve was_new barrier, PENDING_INBOUND_WINDOW_MS ordering, last_advanced_at dual semantics) travel verbatim, pinned by the 83 fixture files, one flow per PR.
findings.evidence: onboarding/interview/engine.ts:540,688,1721,4085,7808, :372-392 (whitelist merge); engine-internals.ts:2027; engine-import-routing.ts (165 self. refs); gateway/http/chat-bridge.ts:1942; open/composer.ts:2954-3010 (Path-1 bypass, raw read :2975); sqlite-state-store.ts:10-14,98-102; resume-cron.ts:266-276

verification.notes: Verified every load-bearing claim at HEAD. (1) Size/structure: engine.ts = 10,078 lines (wc -l) with exactly 102 method declarations; start() = engine.ts:688-1460 (~772 lines), consumeChoice = :4085-4971 (~887), resolvePhasePromptSpecUncached = :7808-8423 (~615). All cited anchors land on the claimed constructs (class at :540, whitelist ROUTER_AMEND_ALLOWED_KEYS at :373-392). (2) Failed split: satellites total exactly 4,475 lines (engine-import-routing.ts 2,234 + engine-slug.ts 1,086 + engine-persona.ts 1,155), each a bag of free functions taking `self: EngineInternals` (interface at engine-internals.ts:2027; 165 `self.` refs in engine-import-routing.ts) — so signature changes do require coordinated edits across engine.ts + engine-internals.ts + satellite. (3) Dead code: acceptChoice (engine.ts:1721) has ZERO production callers repo-wide (only tests: tests/integration/button-primitive-cross-channel.test.ts etc.); the slug flow is cut in Open mode via OPEN_MODE_EXTRA_TRANSITIONS (phase.ts:129-134). CAVEAT: managed mode is not gone from this repo — it is env-selectable (NEUTRON_ROLE=managed, gateway/deployment-mode.ts:31-44), wired at build-landing-stack.ts:1343, and the engine constructor DEFAULTS to 'managed' (engine.ts:573: `this.deploymentMode = deps.deploymentMode ?? 'managed'`). (4) phase_state triple-writer confirmed: JS shallow-merge sqlite-state-store.ts:98-102, raw SQL json_remove resume-cron.ts:266-276, engine whitelist merge :373-392/:3191; raw read in the Path-1 bypass at open/composer.ts:2975; chat-bridge drives the other architecture via engine.advance at chat-bridge.ts:1942. (5) Tests: 113 *.test.ts files mention phase_state (critic said 58 — undercount; direction unchanged); no test pins the store shallow-merge contract or a key registry (state-store-composite-key.test.ts:74-87 pins only cross-user isolation); found no integration test driving both the app-ws Path-1 composer path and the chat-bridge engine path against one sqlite store — matches the standing memory note that app-ws chat_log is an unresolved M1 design decision. (6) "Six parallel per-phase tables" is approximately right: PHASE_KNOWLEDGE (phase-spec-resolver.ts:1116), LEGAL_TRANSITIONS (phase.ts:73), OPEN_MODE_EXTRA_TRANSITIONS (phase.ts:130), INTERACTION_MODE_BY_PHASE (interaction-mode.ts:73) plus two Partial records (interaction-mode.ts:177,239) and STATIC_PHASE_SPECS. Severity P0 is honest for a maintainability-refactor audit: this is the largest file in the repo sharing an unpinned JSON contract with three writers across two live conversation architectures. Riskiest behavior change in the proposal: step-1 slug-flow deletion (managed-mode functionality change, see corrected_proposal) and the typed codec silently dropping unknown phase_state keys — the proposed grep-derived registry + round-trip golden is the right mitigation and nothing existing would catch it today.
verification.corrected_diagnosis: Diagnosis stands as written with two factual corrections: (a) the count of test files mentioning phase_state is ~113, not 58 (undercount; the point — broad incidental coverage, zero contract-pinning — is unchanged); (b) "~1.6k dead lines" is only accurate under an Open-only product ruling: acceptChoice (~268 lines, engine.ts:1721-1988) is genuinely dead (zero production callers), but the slug flow (engine-slug.ts 1,086 lines + engine wrappers ~8728-8844) is live code for managed mode, which remains env-selectable in this repo (NEUTRON_ROLE=managed, gateway/deployment-mode.ts:31-44; wired at build-landing-stack.ts:1343) — and the engine's constructor default is 'managed' (engine.ts:573), a trap any split must carry verbatim.
verification.corrected_proposal: Keep the sequence, but split step 1 in two and gate the slug deletion on step 0: (1a) delete acceptChoice now — zero production callers, tests migrate to advance, no behavior at risk; (1b) delete the slug flow ONLY IF step 0's written decision also rules managed mode out of scope for neutron-open — otherwise the slug flow is reachable production behavior (NEUTRON_ROLE=managed) and deleting it violates the no-functionality-change mandate; the safe alternative is to leave engine-slug.ts as an already-extracted satellite (it is the one extraction that mostly worked) and exclude it from the split's critical path. Add to step 2: the phase_state codec must be lossy-proof for UNKNOWN keys (pass-through, never drop) because the store shallow-merge (sqlite-state-store.ts:98-102) and the resume-cron raw SQL json_remove/json_extract (resume-cron.ts:266-276) both operate on keys the TypeScript layer never enumerates; and pin the engine constructor's deploymentMode-defaults-to-'managed' quirk (engine.ts:573) in the golden. Rest of the plan (contracts leaf, cluster-map flow carving with monotonically shrinking EngineInternals, per-phase table collapse, invariants traveling verbatim) is sound as proposed.
verification.behavior_risk: Highest: deleting the slug flow silently changes managed-mode onboarding (NEUTRON_ROLE=managed routes agent_name_chosen→slug_chosen today; no Open-mode test would fail). Second: a typed phase_state codec that whitelists known keys would drop unknown/legacy keys on rewrite, breaking crash-resume watermarks (resume_active_prompt_id json_remove rollback, import_failed raw read in composer.ts:2975) — no existing test pins merge semantics, so it would stay green. Third: any split that changes the ordering of the store shallow-merge vs the router whitelist merge within a turn changes which writer wins on key collision.

findings.title: No durable-job primitive: a restart silently discards completed paid imports, strands mid-run jobs, and orphans every dispatched agent — while the repo already contains the correct pattern
findings.diagnosis: The live synthesis import runner stamps import_jobs status='completed' durably but keeps the actual ImportResult only in an in-process RAM Map: restart between completion and consumption discards the whole paid synthesis (status() returns result=undefined, engine degrades to gap-fill); restart mid-run strands pass1-running rows with no boot sweep (rescued only by a 15-min hard timeout); the completion watcher is a setTimeout chain never re-armed at boot. runtime/subagent/registry.ts explicitly promises SQLite persistence ('S4') that never landed, so a restart orphans every dispatched Atlas/Sentinel run with no reaping or surfaced completion; the GBrain deferred-edge queue is RAM-only. Meanwhile profile-pic pending-call-store + restart-resume and the REPL pending-respawns queue already implement durable-row + boot-sweep correctly — four maturity tiers for one problem.
findings.proposal: One durable job_runs table + JobHost (enqueue writes a row before work starts; claim/heartbeat/complete/fail; boot resume sweep with profile-pic-style fresh/retry/hard-fail windows). Migrate synthesis import first: persist ImportResult in the same write that flips status='completed', fall back to the row on Map miss, boot-sweep orphaned non-terminal rows to failed/resumable converging with the existing hard timeout. Then persist SubagentRecords with boot reaping that fires the report sink (without double-surfacing pre-crash completions); persist a tiny gbrain_sync_state row. Explicitly do NOT persist trident's fired/redispatched sets or the recovered-reply buffer — RAM-by-design orphan detection; write the durability-tier decision rule (instance table / sidecar / file / RAM-by-design) into persistence/AGENTS.md and annotate the deliberate RAM stores.
findings.evidence: gateway/realmode-composer/build-synthesis-import-runner.ts:128-132,240-248,267-274,305-333; runtime/subagent/registry.ts:1-12, control.ts:118-121; gbrain-memory/GBrainSyncHook.ts:130-148; contrast onboarding/profile-pic/pending-call-store.ts + restart-resume.ts, pending-respawns-queue.ts:13-19; trident/orchestrator.ts:198-206 (RAM-by-design)

verification.notes: Checked every cited file at HEAD. CONFIRMED core claims: (1) synthesis import runner keeps ImportResult in a RAM Map (build-synthesis-import-runner.ts:131) while flipping import_jobs status='completed' durably (242-248); status() only attaches result on Map hit (305-308) and synthesizeOnDemand is Map-only (327-333) — after a restart the boot-re-armed cron drives completed→advanceFromImportRunningOnComplete with `job.result ?? null` (engine-import-routing.ts:913-921), which stamps import_result=null and the user lands in gap-fill (1554-1563). The RAM choice is deliberate and documented (128-131). (2) runJob is fire-and-forget in-process (267-274); no boot sweep of non-terminal import_jobs rows exists anywhere. (3) SubagentRegistry is an in-memory Map with an explicit unfulfilled S4 SQLite promise (runtime/subagent/registry.ts:4-7,74-75; control.ts:117-119; AGENTS.md:7); the Open dispatch service builds on it (open/composer.ts:666) and its report sink is currently just console.log (composer.ts:674-681); board run-bindings are durable (agent-dispatch/service.ts:333) with terminal-only reconcile (service.ts:469) and no boot reconciliation — restart leaves phantom in_progress items. (4) GBrain deferred-edge queue is RAM-only (GBrainSyncHook.ts:138-139). (5) The durable pattern genuinely exists: profile_pic_pending + boot hook with 60s-fresh/one-auto-retry/hard-fail windows (pending-call-store.ts:14-25, restart-resume.ts:8-43) and pending-respawns-queue.ts:12-19 ("disk is the source of truth" boot drain). (6) trident fired/redispatched RAM-by-design confirmed (trident/orchestrator.ts:198-208) — the critic's exclusion is correct. REFUTED/CORRECTED details: (a) "rescued only by a 15-min hard timeout" is stale — 2026-06-18 replaced the flat cap with a progress-aware timeout: 30-min floor (env-tunable), 5-min read no-progress window, 30-min consolidate window, 4h ceiling (engine-internals.ts:98-163; engine-import-routing.ts:980-1052). Stranding is WORSE than claimed (~30 min minimum). (b) "completion watcher is a setTimeout chain never re-armed at boot" conflates two watchers: the terminal-status poller is a CRON registered at boot (import-running-cron.ts:262-296; gateway/composition/build-core-modules.ts:607-620; boot test tests/integration/import-running-cron-scheduler-boot.test.ts), and the in-memory setTimeout consumer of import_analysis_presented, while indeed not re-armed at boot, IS re-armed on session reconnect plus a finalize-recovery sweep (open/composer.ts:3236-3272, M1 E2E fixes) — no permanent wedge at HEAD. (c) "discards the whole paid synthesis" is overstated: project seeds are written to disk BEFORE the completed flip (build-synthesis-import-runner.ts:227-238) and survive; what's lost is the ImportResult presentation/primary_projects stamping. (d) The critic missed the closest prior art: the legacy chunked ImportJobRunner already persists results to an import_results table with upsert + read-back (onboarding/history-import/job-runner.ts:745,1977-1998) — the synthesis runner regressed this deliberately, so the fix is table reuse, not a new primitive. Riskiest behavior changes in the proposal: a generic boot sweep flipping non-terminal import_jobs rows to failed would destroy the legacy runner's cross-restart rate_limit_paused resume machinery (resumeRunner wiring open/composer.ts:1410; tests/integration/import-paused-auto-resume.test.ts, import-resume-button.test.ts would catch rate_limit_* but not a queued/pass1-running-scoped sweep shared with the legacy runner); and persisting SubagentRecords would break a load-bearing subtlety — the RAM registry auto-clearing at restart is what lets liveByKey/already_running guards (registry.ts:145-152) permit re-dispatch after a crash; durable 'running' rows loaded at boot would wedge every spawn until reaped, so reap-to-terminal must run strictly before any spawn. Also note: persisting the final ImportResult only rescues the seconds-wide completed→consumed window (5s cron cadence); mid-run restarts still lose the synthesis regardless — the boot sweep's real value is converting a ~30-min silent strand into an immediate graceful failure, a functionality change (an intended bug-fix) that must be carved out of the no-functionality-change refactor scope.
verification.corrected_diagnosis: Four maturity tiers for one restart-durability problem, confirmed: (1) the synthesis import runner stamps import_jobs status='completed' durably but keeps the paid ImportResult only in a RAM Map (build-synthesis-import-runner.ts:131,240-248,305-308) — a restart in the completed→consumed window (seconds wide; 5s cron cadence) or anywhere mid-run loses the analysis, and the engine degrades to gap-fill with import_result=null (engine-import-routing.ts:913-921,1554-1563); project seeds written to disk pre-completion survive (runner:227-238), so the loss is the presentation/user-model, not the whole synthesis. Mid-run stranded pass1-running rows have no boot sweep and are rescued only by the progress-aware timeout — 30-min floor + no-progress windows + 4h ceiling (engine-internals.ts:98-163), NOT the claimed 15 min. The terminal-status poller IS re-armed at boot (import-running-cron.ts:262-296, build-core-modules.ts:607-620) and the in-memory analysis-consumer setTimeout watcher is re-armed on reconnect (open/composer.ts:3248-3272), so nothing wedges permanently — the cost is a ~30-min silent wait then a degraded (paid-import-discarded) onboarding. (2) runtime/subagent/registry.ts promises S4 SQLite persistence that never landed (registry.ts:4-7, AGENTS.md:7); restart empties the registry, orphaning dispatched Atlas/Sentinel runs and leaving durable board items bound to phantom run_ids (agent-dispatch/service.ts:333,469) with no boot reconcile — though the report sink is currently only console.log (open/composer.ts:674-681). (3) GBrain deferred-edge queue is RAM-only (GBrainSyncHook.ts:138) — but best-effort/capped by design and gbrain is currently non-functional in prod. (4) profile-pic pending-call-store + restart-resume and pending-respawns-queue already implement durable-row + boot-sweep correctly. Severity: P1, not P0 — the worst user-visible outcome (once-per-install onboarding import degraded to gap-fill after a restart, ~30-min strand) has a designed graceful path, and the tiny completed→consumed window is the only case final-result persistence rescues.
verification.corrected_proposal: Skip the generic JobHost — targeted per-store fixes are safer and mostly reuse existing schema. (1) Synthesis import: reuse the EXISTING import_results table exactly as the legacy chunked runner does (write-back at completion mirroring job-runner.ts:1977-1998, read on Map miss in status()/synthesizeOnDemand mirroring job-runner.ts:745) — same write ordering as today (seeds → result persist → status flip → onSynthesisComplete) so no observable change while the process lives. Flag the restart path explicitly as a FUNCTIONALITY CHANGE (restart now presents analysis instead of gap-fill) and land it as a bug-fix commit outside the no-functionality-change refactor. (2) Boot sweep: scope strictly to statuses the synthesis runner owns (queued/pass1-running with no import_results row) and NEVER touch rate_limit_paused/cooling_off — the legacy runner's cross-restart resume machinery depends on those rows surviving (open/composer.ts:1410; import-paused-auto-resume.test.ts). Sweeping to 'failed' converges with the existing failed→import_analysis_presented(failed) route, replacing the 30-min strand. (3) Subagent registry: do NOT naively persist records — the RAM registry auto-clearing at restart is load-bearing for the liveByKey/already_running double-spawn guards (registry.ts:145-152; a durable 'running' row would wedge re-dispatch). Either keep the registry RAM and add a boot-time BOARD reconcile (clear linked_run_id/status for bound runs absent from the registry — one SQL pass, no registry persistence), or if S4 persistence is wanted, reap all non-terminal rows to crashed-at-boot BEFORE the first spawn and suppress report-sink re-fire for pre-crash completions. (4) GBrain deferred edges: leave RAM-by-design (capped best-effort queue; gbrain is dead in prod anyway) — just annotate. (5) Do keep the critic's good idea of writing the durability-tier decision rule + RAM-by-design annotations (trident/orchestrator.ts:198-208 style) into persistence/AGENTS.md.
verification.behavior_risk: Two concrete traps: (a) a generic boot sweep of non-terminal import_jobs rows would silently destroy the legacy chunked runner's cross-restart rate_limit_paused auto-resume (tests import-paused-auto-resume.test.ts / import-resume-button.test.ts cover the paused path but not a shared-table sweep scoped to queued/pass1-running, which both runners emit); (b) persisting SubagentRecords without reap-before-first-spawn inverts the crash semantics of the liveByKey/already_running guards — today a restart clears them (re-dispatch allowed), a durable registry would wedge every logical task whose pre-crash row is still 'running'. Also, persisting/serving the ImportResult after restart changes user-visible behavior (analysis presented vs gap-fill), so it cannot ship inside the behavior-preserving refactor umbrella without being declared.

findings.title: Capability gating and HITL approval are inert at the only tool-dispatch chokepoint, and ToolCallContext is dropped so per-project scoping shipped half-broken by architecture
findings.diagnosis: McpServer.dispatch is the sole path for every agent tool call, but its capability_gate defaults to always-allow and production passes none; dispatch never reads approval_policy; ApprovalManager.requestApproval has zero dispatch callers; ToolDefSchema cannot express approval and install hardcodes 'auto', so tools declaring 'prompt-user' (agent-dispatch, trident build, codex-credential) execute ungated. Enforcement exists only because each bundled Core voluntarily wraps its own handlers — a third-party Core that skips the wrapper gets un-gated, un-audited dispatch on a platform whose SDK advertises gating. Additionally install-bundled's wrapHandler drops ToolCallContext entirely, which is why per-project credential scoping (PR #154) needed an AsyncLocalStorage bolt-on and the primary CC-spawn MCP path still silently resolves global.
findings.proposal: Make dispatch the single enforcement chokepoint: consult installation-record capabilities + approval_policy there, preserving secret_audit_log row shapes; ship LOG-ONLY first to inventory every capability string currently dispatched, then flip to enforce, keeping bundled Cores' CapabilityGuard as belt-and-braces during transition. Thread a typed ToolCallContext{project_id, topic_id} through dispatch → tools-bridge POST → sink → Core handlers as a second argument and delete the ALS pattern (gate per-service via the existing SERVICE_SCOPE policy; gmail/calendar stay global). Whether 'prompt-user' tools start prompting is an explicit owner decision — a deliberate behavior change, not part of the silent refactor.
findings.evidence: mcp/server.ts:42,67-84; tools/approval.ts:97 (zero dispatch callers); cores/sdk/manifest.ts:103-110; gateway/cores/install-bundled.ts:936-975 (hardcoded 'auto', wrapHandler drops context); gateway/cores/active-project-context.ts:16-25; core-credential-resolver.ts:33,47-51

verification.notes: Every cited fact checks out at HEAD. (1) Gate inert: mcp/server.ts:42 `capability_gate ?? (() => true)`; the ONLY production construction (gateway/composition/build-core-modules.ts:248-254) passes no gate; the only caller ever passing one is mcp/server.test.ts:62. (2) dispatch (mcp/server.ts:67-84) reads capability_required but never approval_policy; repo-wide grep for requestApproval hits only tools/approval.ts + tools/approval.test.ts — ApprovalManager is instantiated into the module graph (build-core-modules.ts:232-236) but nothing consumes it: dormant machinery incl. tool_approvals table, TTL sweep, notifier. (3) prompt-user tools confirmed ungated: agent-dispatch/tool.ts:97, trident/work-board-build-tool.ts:117, trident/codex-credential-tool.ts:92, skill-forge/tool.ts:163 — registry stores approval_policy (tools/registry.ts:58) that no code path reads at dispatch. (4) ToolDefSchema (cores/sdk/manifest.ts:103-110) has no approval field; install-bundled.ts:936,953,975 hardcode DEFAULT_APPROVAL_POLICY='auto'; wrapHandler (install-bundled.ts:957-964) discards ToolCallContext (`_ctx` unused). (5) Bundled cores self-wrap via CapabilityGuard (e.g. cores/free/research/src/tools.ts:86, tasks/src/tools.ts:127) — and note the guard is WEAKER than the critic states: capability-guard.ts:90-113 only checks the core's OWN manifest self-consistency (tool declared, capability in its own capabilities[]), never the owner's installation-record grant. installations-store.ts persists manifest_capabilities_json but nothing consults it at dispatch. (6) CC-spawn MCP path resolves global: active-project-context.ts:16-19 says so verbatim; persistent-repl-substrate.ts:1022-1035 documents the topic_id:null dispatch (Codex r1 P2 known follow-up); replToolBridge.dispatch at persistent-repl-substrate.ts:1050 binds no TopicContext (resolveBound/withTopicContext have zero production callers outside mcp/).
