Two honest caveats the critic underplays: every gap is a DOCUMENTED deferral, not silent breakage (server.ts:20-25 'P1 S4 wires the real source', loader.ts:16-17 'marketplace fetch is a P3+ detail', active-project-context.ts:19 'safe, no regression'), and there is no live third-party Core install flow yet (loader v1 = local directory), so the un-gated-third-party scenario is a platform-contract risk, not a live exposure; the agent is anyway a full Claude Code process with Bash, so the MCP gate is not the actual security boundary of a single-owner install. That argues P1, not P0. Proposal gaps: ToolRegistration (tools/registry.ts:52-69) carries no core_slug provenance, so dispatch cannot know WHICH installation record to consult — enforcement needs provenance added at registration, and platform tools registered outside Cores (work_board, dispatch_agent, doc_search, neutron-tools surfaces) have no installation record to check at all. 'Delete the ALS pattern' contradicts the documented constraint that created it: Core clients consume zero-arg accessor closures built once at boot (active-project-context.ts:5-9, core-credential-resolver.ts:127-135), so explicit threading means changing the accessor contract in every @neutronai Core package PLUS per-turn topic threading through the warm-pool turn lifecycle (AgentSpec carries no per-turn topic) — effort L not M, and it touches one-reply-per-turn machinery. Test coverage would NOT catch an enforce-flip regression: mcp/server.test.ts only tests an injected gate; sprint-31-cores-runtime.test.ts:164 exercises CapabilityGuard directly, not the production chokepoint; custom capability strings like 'read:notes.db' (install-bundled.ts:926-935 comment) exist and would be the first denial casualties.
verification.corrected_diagnosis: Diagnosis is factually accurate in every checked particular, with two corrections. (a) Severity P0 is inflated: P1. All four gaps are explicitly documented deferrals (mcp/server.ts:20-25 promises 'P1 S4 wires the real per-instance capability source' — never kept; active-project-context.ts:16-19 documents the CC-spawn global fallback as 'safe, no regression'; persistent-repl-substrate.ts:1022-1035 documents topic_id:null as a known P0-1 follow-up), there is no live third-party Core loading path (cores/runtime/loader.ts:16-17: v1 reads a local directory, marketplace is 'a P3+ detail'), and the agent is a fully-privileged Claude Code process with Bash, so the MCP capability gate is not the operative security boundary of a single-owner install. It is a platform-contract integrity finding (SDK published on npm advertising gating that the platform never enforces), not a live vulnerability. (b) The finding UNDERSTATES one thing: even the bundled Cores' voluntary CapabilityGuard never checks owner grants — capability-guard.ts:90-113 only validates the core's own manifest self-consistency. No code anywhere consults the installation-record capabilities (installations-store.ts persists manifest_capabilities_json; nothing reads it at dispatch). The owner-grant check does not exist in any layer, voluntary or otherwise.
verification.corrected_proposal: Keep the LOG-ONLY-first dispatch-chokepoint direction but fix three feasibility gaps. (1) Dispatch cannot consult installation records today: ToolRegistration (tools/registry.ts:52-69) has no core_slug provenance, and platform tools (work_board, dispatch_agent, doc_search, skill_forge, neutron-tools surfaces) have no installation record at all — add provenance at registration and an explicit 'platform' class exempt from installation-record lookup, or boot-time enforcement denies everything. (2) Do NOT 'delete the ALS pattern' as part of this refactor: it exists precisely because Core credential accessors are zero-arg closures built once at boot (active-project-context.ts:5-9); explicit ToolCallContext threading requires changing the accessor contract in every @neutronai Core package plus per-turn topic threading through the warm-pool turn lifecycle (AgentSpec carries no per-turn topic — persistent-repl-substrate.ts:1024-1035), touching the one-reply-per-turn machinery. That is a separate L-sized project; the M-sized behavior-preserving step is binding the ALS frame (or an equivalent explicit ctx) at the /tool-call sink by carrying project_id in the tool-call POST, which fixes the primary CC-spawn global-fallback without touching Core packages. (3) Name the concrete flip risks: enforcing prompt-user would stall trident's autonomous pipeline (work_board_build/dispatch_agent/codex_credential block on a Telegram decision with a 5-minute TTL, approval.ts:63, then resolve 'expired'); and belt-and-braces double-enforcement double-writes secret_audit_log tool_call rows while bundled Cores keep self-wrapping — either move CapabilityGuard wrapping into install-bundled's registration loop (platform wraps, cores stop self-wrapping; same row shapes, one seam) or suppress dispatch-level audit rows for already-wrapped cores during transition. Simplest behavior-preserving alternative for the approval half: since ApprovalManager has zero callers and no UI, either wire it (a behavior change needing owner sign-off) or delete/mark-reserved the approval_policy field and the dormant machinery so the SDK stops advertising an inert contract.
verification.behavior_risk: Riskiest: flipping capability enforcement on would hard-fail tool dispatches whose capability strings aren't resolvable against any grant source — custom strings like 'read:notes.db'/'read:calendar_core.events' (install-bundled.ts:926-935) and all platform tools with no installation record — and no existing test covers the production composition path (build-core-modules.ts:253 constructs the gate-less server; mcp/server.test.ts:59-77 only tests an injected gate; sprint-31-cores-runtime.test.ts:164 tests CapabilityGuard directly), so breakage would surface only at runtime. Second: enforcing prompt-user stalls trident autonomous builds and dispatch_agent (5-min approval TTL then 'expired'). Third: ALS deletion risks changing gmail/calendar credential resolution (SERVICE_SCOPE global forcing at core-credential-resolver.ts:105 and the fail-soft null accessor at :127-135 must be preserved verbatim) and perturbing the one-reply-per-turn turn lifecycle if topic threading is done through the warm pool.

findings.title: Contracts-first extraction: shared wire/protocol types are stranded inside god files and edge packages, generating most inverted edges, all client hand-mirrors, and blocking every declared split
findings.diagnosis: The chat wire protocol (ChatOutbound/ChatBridge/PendingChatClaim — whose JSDoc is the ONLY written spec for jti-claim atomicity, identity-unregister, and seed-reemit semantics) lives in landing/server.ts with 7 wrong-direction importers across gateway/reminders/open. OnboardingPhase is value-imported by runtime; AgentEngagementMode (pure chat policy) lives in dormant connect/ and is value-imported by a bundled core and chat-bridge; McpToolResolver lives inside a dormant adapter; ImportJobRunnerHook inside the 2.3k engine-internals; ChatCommandFilter is defined in an HTTP surface file, imported sideways, and structurally re-cloned by three Cores. These homes generate 6 of the 11 SCC back-edges, force node-poisoned-package hand-mirrors in the Expo app, and make every god-file split cross-package.
findings.proposal: One dedicated 'contracts pass' PR series before the composer/chat-bridge/engine splits: extract zero-dep node-free leaves — @neutronai/chat-protocol (ChatOutbound/ChatInbound/ChatBridge + PendingChatClaim with JSDoc moved VERBATIM) and @neutronai/contracts (phases, engagement mode + its load-bearing fail-soft default values, LlmCallFn, ImportJobRunnerHook, ChatCommandFilter, handoff sentinels, tool-resolver) — old paths keep `export … from` shims. Move connect/agent-engagement.ts out (with its tests, verbatim) so connect/ becomes a quarantinable leaf. Runtime values (sentinels, DEFAULT_AGENT_ENGAGEMENT_MODE) move with re-exports, never duplicated; verify no cycles via the new depcruise config.
findings.evidence: landing/server.ts:170-699 + 7 consumers (chat-bridge.ts:45, reminders/outbound.ts:24, open/composer.ts:214, ...); runtime/platform-adapter.ts:53; connect/agent-engagement.ts ← cores/free/agent-settings/src/backend.ts:43-47 + chat-bridge.ts:2749; mcp/server.ts:15; engine-internals.ts:696; chat-bridge.ts:1009 (sideways import)

verification.notes: Checked every cited location at HEAD (d30280c). CONFIRMED: (1) The chat wire protocol really lives in landing/server.ts — ChatInbound:170, ChatOutbound:203, PendingChatClaim:530, ChatBridge:546-699, and the JSDoc there is the only spec-grade prose for jti-claim atomicity (524-528, 553-559), identity-aware unregister (648-674), and seed-reemit supersede semantics (627-646); no protocol spec exists in docs/ (only a passing mention at docs/SYSTEM-OVERVIEW.md:2005). Exactly 7 non-test files import landing/server.ts: reminders/outbound.ts:24, open/composer.ts:214, gateway/http/chat-bridge.ts:45, gateway/http/recovered-reply-store.ts:51, gateway/proactive/button-store-sink.ts:36, gateway/realmode-composer/build-live-agent-turn.ts:67, gateway/realmode-composer/build-landing-stack.ts:78. (2) connect/agent-engagement.ts is a self-described PURE module (header lines 18-20) with VALUE consumers outside connect: cores/free/agent-settings/src/backend.ts:43-47 (DEFAULT_AGENT_ENGAGEMENT_MODE, isAgentEngagementMode), gateway/http/chat-bridge.ts:121-126 (values; ~2749-2760 is the fail-soft usage site), plus type imports in gateway/projects/sqlite-store.ts:41, app-projects-surface.ts:48, build-landing-stack.ts:28, agent-settings/tools.ts:38. The Expo hand-mirror exists with an explicit "must stay in lockstep" warning at app/lib/projects-client.ts:22-28. (3) McpToolResolver is defined in runtime/adapters/gpt-5-5-api/mcp-shim.ts:34 and imported by mcp/server.ts:15; the gpt-5-5-api adapter is wired into no composer (only comment references elsewhere) — dormant confirmed. (4) ImportJobRunnerHook at onboarding/interview/engine-internals.ts:696 (file is 2,329 lines — "2.3k" accurate); ~10 integration tests import it. (5) ChatCommandFilter defined in gateway/http/app-ws-surface.ts:122; sideways inline import() at chat-bridge.ts:1009 and 1711, ~8 more in boot-helpers.ts:520-704, mount-open-cores.ts:49; structural re-clones in exactly three Cores: cores/free/email/src/chat-bridge.ts:39, cores/free/research/src/chat-bridge.ts:38-41, cores/free/scraping/src/chat-bridge.ts:38 (same match(input)→Promise<Result|null> shape). (6) The runtime↔onboarding cycle is real and bidirectional-VALUE: runtime/onboarding-conversational-flag.ts:24 value-imports ALL_PHASES from onboarding, while onboarding value-imports runtime (e.g. phase-spec-resolver.ts:48 OPTIN_TOKENS, synthesis-session.ts:29 getBestModel). CORRECTIONS: (a) the cited runtime/platform-adapter.ts:53 is `import type` — NOT a value import; the value edge is in onboarding-conversational-flag.ts:24; (b) 6 of the 7 landing/server.ts importers are TYPE-ONLY (erased at runtime), and the 7th (build-landing-stack.ts:78 createLandingServer) is a legitimate composition-root value import — so the landing-home edges are compile-time/type-graph coupling, not runtime cycles; (c) "all client hand-mirrors" is overbroad: app/lib/ws-envelope.ts mirrors channels/adapters/app-ws/envelope.ts and app/lib/composer-constants.ts mirrors a server cap — homes NOT in the critic's list; (d) "6 of the 11 SCC back-edges" is unverifiable in code — no dependency-cruiser config exists at HEAD (only the devDependency, package.json:57); treat the count as the audit's own graph analysis, not fact. SEVERITY: P1 is honest as refactor sequencing — the type homes genuinely make every declared split (chat-bridge 3,113 lines, composer 3,732, engine-internals 2,329) cross-package — but note the coupling is mostly type-level, so there is no runtime-correctness exposure today. BEHAVIOR PRESERVATION: type moves + `export…from` value shims are runtime-neutral; the riskiest seam is the fail-soft DEFAULT_AGENT_ENGAGEMENT_MODE fallback in chat-bridge.ts:~2759 (drift risk if the constant were ever duplicated instead of moved), and it is directly covered by gateway/http/__tests__/chat-bridge-engagement-mode.test.ts; seed-reemit/jti races are implementation behavior untouched by type moves and covered by chat-bridge-seed-reemit-race.test.ts and chat-bridge.test.ts; connect/__tests__/agent-engagement.test.ts moves with the module. Two feasibility caveats the critic missed: landing/server.ts:41 already re-exports MOBILE_APP_URL (a VALUE) from onboarding/interview/final-handoff-config.ts, so the "protocol" file contains value glue that needs the shim treatment too; and new Bun-workspace packages carry real setup cost here (leaf-tsconfig pattern, Expo metro monorepo config gotcha if the app ever imports them).
verification.corrected_diagnosis: Shared wire/protocol types are stranded inside implementation files and mis-homed packages, and this blocks every declared god-file split. The chat wire protocol (ChatInbound/ChatOutbound/PendingChatClaim/ChatBridge, landing/server.ts:170-699) — whose JSDoc is the only written spec for jti-claim atomicity, identity-aware unregister, and seed-reemit supersede semantics — is imported by 6 files across gateway/reminders/open as TYPE-ONLY imports (chat-bridge.ts:45, reminders/outbound.ts:24, open/composer.ts:214, recovered-reply-store.ts:51, button-store-sink.ts:36, build-live-agent-turn.ts:67); a 7th importer (build-landing-stack.ts:78) is the composition root legitimately constructing the server. These are compile-time coupling, not runtime cycles — the one true bidirectional VALUE cycle is runtime↔onboarding (runtime/onboarding-conversational-flag.ts:24 value-imports ALL_PHASES; onboarding value-imports runtime in phase-spec-resolver.ts:48 and synthesis-session.ts:29; the cited platform-adapter.ts:53 is type-only). AgentEngagementMode + its load-bearing DEFAULT_AGENT_ENGAGEMENT_MODE live in dormant connect/ and are value-imported by a bundled core (cores/free/agent-settings/src/backend.ts:43-47) and chat-bridge (import at :121-126, fail-soft usage at ~:2749-2760), forcing an explicit must-stay-in-lockstep hand-mirror in the Expo app (app/lib/projects-client.ts:22-28) — though other app mirrors (ws-envelope.ts, composer-constants.ts) mirror channels/gateway homes outside this finding's scope. McpToolResolver, consumed by mcp/server.ts:15 as the shape "every substrate adapter" uses, is defined inside the unwired gpt-5-5-api adapter (runtime/adapters/gpt-5-5-api/mcp-shim.ts:34). ImportJobRunnerHook sits at engine-internals.ts:696 inside the 2,329-line engine internals. ChatCommandFilter is defined in an HTTP surface file (gateway/http/app-ws-surface.ts:122), imported sideways via inline import() from chat-bridge.ts:1009/:1711 and ~8 sites in boot-helpers.ts, and structurally re-cloned by exactly three Cores (email/research/scraping src/chat-bridge.ts:38-39). The "6 of 11 SCC back-edges" count could not be verified — no dependency-cruiser config exists at HEAD.
verification.corrected_proposal: Keep the contracts-first sequencing, but stage it to minimize build-system risk: Step 1 (zero build churn) — extract leaf MODULES within existing packages, not new workspace packages: landing/chat-protocol.ts (ChatInbound/ChatOutbound/ChatBridge/PendingChatClaim with JSDoc moved verbatim; note landing/server.ts:41 also value re-exports MOBILE_APP_URL from onboarding — shim that too), gateway/http/chat-command-filter.ts (ChatCommandFilter + Result), onboarding/interview/import-job-runner-hook.ts, and move connect/agent-engagement.ts (pure, zero imports, header says so) plus connect/__tests__/agent-engagement.test.ts verbatim to a neutral home (e.g. chat-core or a new contracts/ dir), with `export … from` shims at every old path so no import site is forced to change in the same PR — values (DEFAULT_AGENT_ENGAGEMENT_MODE, isAgentEngagementMode, resolveEngagement, classifyTaggedIntent) MOVE with re-exports, never duplicate. Also break the one real value cycle: runtime/onboarding-conversational-flag.ts:24's ALL_PHASES import moves with the phase vocabulary. Step 2 (optional, later) — promote those leaves into @neutronai/chat-protocol / @neutronai/contracts workspace packages once the depcruise config exists to enforce direction; new packages here carry known costs (leaf-tsconfig pattern, Expo metro monorepo config if app/ ever consumes them), so don't couple them to the split-unblocking step. Deleting the three Core-side ChatCommandFilter structural clones and the Expo AgentEngagementMode mirror are behavior-neutral follow-ups, not prerequisites. Gate each PR on the existing seams' tests: chat-bridge-engagement-mode.test.ts (fail-soft default), chat-bridge-seed-reemit-race.test.ts, chat-bridge.test.ts, agent-engagement.test.ts, and the ~10 integration tests importing ImportJobRunnerHook.
verification.behavior_risk: Low. Type moves are erased at runtime; the six landing/server.ts protocol importers are type-only so no runtime edge changes. The real risks: (1) accidentally duplicating instead of moving DEFAULT_AGENT_ENGAGEMENT_MODE — later drift would silently flip the fail-soft engagement fallback at chat-bridge.ts:~2759 (covered by chat-bridge-engagement-mode.test.ts); (2) forgetting the MOBILE_APP_URL value re-export at landing/server.ts:41 when splitting the protocol out (a value, not a type — breaks importers at boot); (3) if new workspace packages are created in the same pass, tsconfig/metro wiring can break the Expo app build (known repo gotcha) — which is why package promotion should be a separate step from the module extraction.

findings.title: Test infrastructure cannot be trusted through a mass refactor: 280 files (both clients, all trident tests) never typechecked, live .env leaks into every test process, and chunk co-residency reshuffles on any file add
findings.diagnosis: CI runs only root tsc: all of app/ (181 files), landing/chat-react (51), 28 trident test files, and several packages' tests are never typechecked — bun test transpiles without checking, and the leaf tsconfigs pass TODAY so the fix is zero-cost now but the hand-maintained include list will silently drop more files during moves (it already includes a deleted file). Bun auto-loads the live .env (NEUTRON_HOME, port, cookie secret) into every test process with only ONE var scrubbed; 42 files hand-restore env, and one forgotten key reads/writes live dogfood state — the most plausible substrate for the intermittent ~53-failure episodes. run-tests.sh chunks by sorted index so adding a file reshuffles process co-residency, making leakage partition-dependent and unretried; two wiring tests burn 62s (15% of wall clock) on the REAL 30s reminder tick because CompositionInput exposes no timer seam; run-tests.sh/leak-gate.sh have zero tests with fail-open audit branches; CONTRIBUTING documents the wrong workflow.
findings.proposal: Land BEFORE file moves: leaf tsc CI steps (trident, app, landing/chat-react, agent-dispatch, jwt-validator) + full-include root tsconfig + a guard test asserting every *.test.ts is claimed by some tsconfig; extend the preload to scrub the whole NEUTRON_*/OWNER_HOME namespace with explicit opt-in, ship withScopedEnv/withTmpHome in a real test-support workspace, convert module-load env reads to lazy resolvers; partition chunks by package/directory (deterministic, bisectable) keeping the 100-file cap; add an optional timers block to CompositionInput (defaults pinned by snapshot); spawn-based runner self-tests via the existing OVERRIDE seam + make empty BUN_DISC loud + skip-budget test; fix CONTRIBUTING; disperse tests/integration into package __tests__ and promote cross-package fixtures (zip-writer, interview testkit) into the test-support package.

verification.notes:
Checked every cited claim at HEAD d30280c.

TYPECHECK HOLE — CONFIRMED, actually WORSE than claimed. ci.yml:51-52 runs only `bunx tsc --noEmit` (root config). tsconfig.json:12-44 include list omits: trident/ (28 test files, matches exactly), app/ (183 TS/TSX files; critic said 181), landing/chat-react (51 files exact; only landing/server.ts + landing/__tests__ are included at :41-43), and ALSO agent-dispatch/, jwt-validator/, work-board/, and project-credentials/ — the last two have NO tsconfig anywhere, so their tests are typechecked by nothing. Stale include confirmed: landing/chat.ts (tsconfig.json:42) does not exist on disk; tsc silently ignores missing include patterns, so the list rots invisibly. All these files DO run in CI via run-tests.sh (bun transpiles without checking), so they execute but never typecheck.

"Leaf tsconfigs pass TODAY / zero-cost" — REFUTED in part: I ran them. trident (1.6s), app (4.3s), landing/chat-react (3.7s), agent-dispatch (1.3s) pass; jwt-validator FAILS today: jwt-validator/jwt.test.ts:7 TS2305 "Module 'jose' has no exported member 'CryptoKey'". Rot has already happened — strengthens the diagnosis, falsifies the zero-cost claim.

ENV LEAK — CONFIRMED mechanism. bunfig.toml [test].preload → tests/support/scrub-substrate-env.ts:26 deletes ONLY CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN. The live .env carries NEUTRON_HOME=/Users/ryan/neutron/data, NEUTRON_PORT, NEUTRON_ONBOARDING_CHAT_COOKIE_SECRET. open/owner-identity.ts:41-45 makes env NEUTRON_HOME win verbatim; open/server.ts:55-59 mutates process.env.OWNER_HOME at boot. Count correction: ~39 test files hand-manage NEUTRON_*/OWNER_HOME/token env (critic said 42; 28 touch NEUTRON_HOME specifically). The "most plausible substrate for the ~53-failure episodes" attribution is speculation — no code evidence either way; treat as hypothesis, not fact.

CHUNK RESHUFFLE — CONFIRMED. scripts/lib/discover-test-files.sh:24-35 is `find | sort`; run-tests.sh:180-183 slices GENERAL_FILES by index, so one added file shifts every later file's chunk co-residency. General chunks have no retry (only the PGLite lane retries, run-tests.sh:205-225). Fail-open audit branches confirmed: run-tests.sh:140 skips the coverage cross-check entirely when BUN_DISC parses empty; :224 lane `ran` falls back to NPGLITE so a truncated lane can't trip the audit. No self-tests exist for run-tests.sh chunking or leak-gate.sh (scripts/ci/ci-workflow.test.ts guards only ci.yml text patterns). The NEUTRON_TEST_DISCOVER_OVERRIDE seam the proposal wants to use exists (discover-test-files.sh:15-23).

REAL 30s TICK — CONFIRMED. gateway/composition/build-core-modules.ts remindersModule (~:280-296) constructs ReminderTickLoop without tick_interval_ms even though reminders/tick.ts:52,90 already expose the knob (and :56 an injectable clock). Two tests wait on the REAL composition tick: open/__tests__/open-reminder-appws-live-delivery.test.ts:159-167 (40s waitFor, 60_000 test timeout) and open-project-reminder-appws-live-delivery.test.ts:179-186. The ~31s-each measurement is consistent with a 30s tick; the "15% of wall clock" fraction I could not verify.

FIXTURE REACH-IN — CONFIRMED: tests/fixtures/m2/build.ts:25 imports zip-writer from @neutronai/onboarding/history-import/__tests__/.

CONTRIBUTING — OVERSTATED: CONTRIBUTING.md:47-49,62 documents `bun test` + root tsc, which is incomplete/divergent from CI (no run-tests.sh, no leak-gate, no leaf tsc) rather than "wrong" — plain `bun test` still works locally, just unbounded-memory and env-leaky.

SEVERITY: P1 is honest for a pre-refactor gate. Mass file moves with a silently-rotting hand-include list, 280+ never-typechecked files (one already broken), and partition-dependent env leakage is exactly the failure mode that lets a NO-FUNCTIONALITY-CHANGE refactor merge broken code.

BEHAVIOR RISK OF PROPOSAL (see behavior_risk field): the lazy-resolver conversion and the full-include root tsconfig are the two pieces that need correction.
verification.behavior_risk: Riskiest piece: converting module-load env reads to lazy resolvers IS a behavior change, not a pure refactor. Example: onboarding/feedback/m2-week-4-collector.ts:41 computes DEFAULT_M2_FEEDBACK_PATH at import time; open/server.ts:55-59 mutates process.env.OWNER_HOME at boot. Making the read lazy changes WHEN env is sampled relative to that boot mutation and can change the resolved path on a real install (import-time cwd fallback vs post-boot value). No existing test pins this path — it needs a snapshot test first, or should be dropped from the bundle. Second risk: blanket NEUTRON_* preload scrub will change local test outcomes for any test implicitly relying on developer .env values (that is the point, and CI already runs env-less, so CI is the behavior anchor) — but the scrub must not eat vars the runner itself injects into child bun processes; audit NEUTRON_TEST_* readers before shipping. Third: the CompositionInput timers block touches production composition (gateway/composition/build-core-modules.ts); safe only if the 30_000/50 defaults are pinned by a test. Fourth: directory-partitioned chunks deliberately change process co-residency and may surface latent order-dependent failures as CI churn — run both partitionings in parallel for a few days before switching the gate. Product runtime behavior is otherwise untouched; this is test-infra work.
verification.corrected_diagnosis: CI's only typecheck is root `bunx tsc --noEmit` (ci.yml:51-52) against a hand-maintained include list (tsconfig.json:12-44) that omits trident/ (28 test files), app/ (183 files), landing/chat-react (51 files), agent-dispatch/, jwt-validator/, AND work-board/ + project-credentials/ (which have no tsconfig at all) — and already carries a deleted file (landing/chat.ts, :42) that tsc silently ignores, proving the list rots invisibly during file moves. The rot is not hypothetical: jwt-validator's leaf tsc FAILS today (jwt-validator/jwt.test.ts:7 TS2305, 'jose' CryptoKey) — so the fix is cheap but not zero-cost. Bun auto-loads the live .env (NEUTRON_HOME, NEUTRON_PORT, cookie secret) into every `bun test` process and the preload scrubs exactly one var (tests/support/scrub-substrate-env.ts:26); ~39 test files hand-manage NEUTRON_*/OWNER_HOME env, and owner-identity.ts:41-45 makes a forgotten NEUTRON_HOME resolve to the live dogfood dir. (Attributing the intermittent ~53-failure episodes to this is plausible but unproven.) run-tests.sh chunks the sorted file list by index (:180-183) so any file add reshuffles process co-residency, general chunks are unretried, and two audit branches fail open (:140 empty BUN_DISC skips the coverage cross-check; :224 lane count falls back to NPGLITE). Two wiring tests wait ~31s each on the REAL 30s reminder tick because build-core-modules.ts hardcodes ReminderTickLoop defaults even though tick.ts:52,56 already expose tick_interval_ms/now. run-tests.sh and leak-gate.sh have no self-tests (scripts/ci/ci-workflow.test.ts only pattern-matches ci.yml). CONTRIBUTING.md:47-62 documents a workflow that is incomplete/divergent from CI (bun test + root tsc only), not flatly wrong. tests/fixtures/m2/build.ts:25 reaches into another package's __tests__ for its zip-writer fixture.
verification.corrected_proposal: Land before file moves, in this order. (1) CI leaf-tsc steps: instead of a "full-include root tsconfig" — which is infeasible as stated because app/ (Expo/React-Native JSX) and landing/chat-react (DOM+JSX) need compiler options incompatible with the root config — add a CI step that GLOBS every */tsconfig.json and runs `tsc -p` on each, plus minimal tsconfigs for work-board/ and project-credentials/; first fix the pre-existing jwt-validator/jwt.test.ts:7 jose type error (test-only change). (2) Guard test asserting every file from scripts/lib/discover-test-files.sh discovery is claimed by some tsconfig's include — this is what makes the hole impossible to reopen during moves; also delete the stale landing/chat.ts entry. (3) Extend the bunfig preload to scrub NEUTRON_*/OWNER_HOME with an explicit keep-list, after auditing which vars the runner intentionally passes into child test processes; ship withScopedEnv/withTmpHome helpers in tests/support and migrate the ~39 hand-restoring files opportunistically, not en masse. (4) Fix the fail-open branches in run-tests.sh (:140 empty BUN_DISC must be FATAL, :224 fallback must warn loudly) and add spawn-based self-tests via the existing NEUTRON_TEST_DISCOVER_OVERRIDE seam — these are pure hardening. (5) Directory-partitioned chunking: run it as a parallel CI lane for a burn-in period before making it the gate, since changed co-residency can surface latent order-dependent tests. (6) Optional timers/interval overrides on CompositionInput with current defaults pinned by test; the ReminderTickLoop knobs already exist so this is plumbing only. (7) DEFER the lazy-resolver conversion of module-load env reads (e.g. m2-week-4-collector.ts:41) — it changes when env is sampled relative to open/server.ts:55-59's boot-time env mutation and is a behavior change; snapshot the resolved values first or leave it out of a no-functionality-change bundle. (8) Update CONTRIBUTING to document run-tests.sh + leak-gate + leaf tsc. The fixture promotion (zip-writer) is fine but low priority.

findings.title: Reify the invisible Managed-composer ABI (barrel + snapshot test), then execute the verified kill-list: ~20 dead modules, ~10k src + ~20k green-but-meaningless test LOC, and the Connect quarantine
findings.diagnosis: NEUTRON_GRAPH_COMPOSER_MODULE dynamic-imports a private-repo composer that reaches boot-helpers exports (8 with zero in-repo consumers), gateway/realmode-composer/* paths, and internal_handle option-bag keys — symbols dead-by-grep but live-by-contract, so every deletion/rename in this audit carries invisible cross-repo blast radius. Behind that gate sits a grep-verified kill-list: landing/connect-relay (zero refs), chat-bridge slug-picker (~490 LOC), the per-chunk import pipeline (~4k src + ~10k test LOC, unreachable — importUseSynthesis hardcoded true at the sole call site), the launcher surface, core-sdk's 1,400-line hand validator, dead prompt files, and assorted zombies. ≥10,400 lines of green tests pin this dead code — including a 'production composer' test that hand-mounts the surface no production composer mounts — inflating the green signal and taxing every split.
findings.proposal: FIRST: create gateway/composer-abi.ts as the ONLY sanctioned import surface for injected composers, pinned by an export-name + option-bag-key snapshot test, plus a one-time Managed-repo import audit; then rename gateway/realmode-composer → gateway/wiring behind path shims (Managed-era vocabulary, ~97 referencing files) before splits mint new modules under the wrong name. THEN deletion PRs by stratum: (1) zero-reference files; (2) barrel zombies; (3) slug-picker/acceptChoice/launcher, migrating still-valuable assertions to live seams; (4) per-chunk pipeline AFTER extracting its live leaves (extractJsonObject, history-import/types.ts, the 429 matcher — golden-tested); (5) dead prompts paired with KNOWN_PROMPTS edits. Fold in the Connect quarantine: once agent-engagement moves (finding 7), consolidate dormant gateway/connect + app-connect-* + landing/connect-* behind the dynamic-import seam and delete the dead files.
findings.evidence: gateway/index.ts:32-63,540-587; boot-helpers.ts:63,420,1259,... (zero non-test consumers); open/composer.ts:1288 (importUseSynthesis:true); chat-bridge.ts:2027-2511; launcher-production-composer.test.ts:110; core-sdk/package.json:5 (private:true) + validator.ts; synthesis-session.ts:30 (live import from dead module); wc -l: history-import tests 5,539 / code-gen 2,835 / validator 622

verification.notes: Core thesis verified at HEAD d30280c. (1) ABI seam: gateway/index.ts:540-587 dynamic-imports NEUTRON_GRAPH_COMPOSER_MODULE validating only buildGraphComposer() shape; open/server.ts:48-52 defers to it too; index.ts:20-24 documents the injected composer imports boot-helpers.ts directly (non-entry, TLA-cycle avoidance). STRONGER than claimed: index.ts:534-538 cites gateway/__tests__/graph-composer-env-seam.test.ts as the seam's pin test but that file does not exist anywhere in the repo — the seam is unpinned at HEAD. (2) boot-helpers zero-consumer exports confirmed: 7 with zero in-repo refs (createTasksCoreOwnerRegistry:63, defaultListProjects:420, loadAnthropicOAuthConfigFromEnv:1259, resolveIdentityPublicBaseUrl:1350, resolveBaseDomain:1358, buildMaxOAuthGateHandler:1389, buildGateLandingServer:1642) + 2-3 test-only; "8" is fair. (3) realmode-composer: 97 referencing files confirmed exactly. (4) importUseSynthesis:true at open/composer.ts:1288 confirmed as sole in-repo buildLandingStack production call — BUT build-landing-stack.ts:1111-1124 explicitly documents the MANAGED hosted import stays on the per-chunk buildImportJobRunnerHook path (ephemeral substrate, 2026-06 recovered-reply decision) until its substrate is reworked; buildImportSubstrate (build-import-substrate.ts) has zero in-repo production callers, i.e. Managed-only. "Unreachable" is Open-only; per-chunk is live-by-contract. (5) Kill-list spot checks: landing/connect-relay.ts zero refs confirmed (browser client, consumers chat.ts/invite.ts gone); slug-picker chat-bridge.ts ~2009-2503 (~490 LOC) DI hook no Open composer threads (build-landing-stack.ts:958 defaults null, open/composer.ts never passes slugPicker); acceptChoice (engine.ts:1721) zero production callers (live path = consumeChoice via advance, engine.ts:2784+); synthesis-session.ts:30 imports extractJsonObject from substrate-callers.ts and :35 VoiceSignals from history-import/types.ts — live-leaf claim exact. (6) LOC evidence: history-import tests 5,623 (claimed 5,539, close), code-gen tests 2,835 (exact), core-sdk/validator.test.ts 622 (exact). ERRORS: validator.ts is 650 lines not "1,400" (validateNeutronManifest has zero in-repo production consumers — only comment refs at cores/sdk/manifest.ts:175,298 — but core-sdk is the intended published SDK, package.json:5 private:true today, so deletion is a public-API decision); launcher surface is not purely dead — app/lib/launcher-client.ts + reducers + 4 app test files call /api/app/projects/<id>/launcher while no composer mounts createAppLauncherSurface (only composition.ts:185 maps it): unmounted-feature vs dead-code is a product decision; launcher-production-composer.test.ts:104-122 is not "meaningless" — per ISSUE #32 it pins the composition.app_xxx_surface→composeInput mapping (deleting a mapping line provably breaks it); code-gen skeleton is an explicit design keep (boot-helpers.ts:1128-1145 "The skeleton STAYS", install_ok must stay TRUE), so its 2,835 test lines are not all dead-code pins. Severity P1 is honest: the unpinned cross-repo ABI genuinely gates every other deletion/rename in the audit, and the dead-by-grep volume is real.
verification.corrected_diagnosis: As stated, with three corrections. (a) The per-chunk import pipeline is unreachable ONLY from the Open composer: build-landing-stack.ts:1111-1124 documents that the Managed hosted import deliberately stays on buildImportJobRunnerHook (its substrate is ephemeral by the 2026-06 recovered-reply decision), and buildImportSubstrate has zero in-repo callers because it exists for the Managed composer — the pipeline is dead-by-grep but live-by-the-very-contract this finding identifies, so it belongs in the ABI-frozen set, not the kill-list, until Managed migrates. Its ~5.6k test LOC are correspondingly not "meaningless" — they are the only regression guard on a Managed-live path and the de facto cross-repo contract tests. (b) core-sdk's hand validator is 650 lines (not 1,400) and core-sdk is the intended published plugin SDK (private:true today); validateNeutronManifest has zero in-repo production consumers, but removing it shrinks the public SDK surface — a product/API decision, not mechanical dead-code removal. (c) The launcher surface is an unmounted feature, not a zombie: the shipped Expo app contains a live launcher client (app/lib/launcher-client.ts + state reducers) targeting /api/app/projects/<id>/launcher that no composer mounts; and launcher-production-composer.test.ts exists to pin the composition-mapping lines (ISSUE #32), not to fake production coverage. ADDITIONAL evidence strengthening the core claim: gateway/index.ts:534-538 cites gateway/__tests__/graph-composer-env-seam.test.ts as the pin for the composer boot seam, but that test does not exist anywhere at HEAD — the invisible ABI is currently entirely unpinned, including its documented guard.
verification.corrected_proposal: Keep the ordering (ABI barrel + snapshot test + one-time Managed-repo import audit FIRST, then rename realmode-composer→wiring behind path shims, then stratified deletions) with these amendments. (1) The composer-abi.ts barrel MUST be a non-entry module that never imports gateway/index.ts — the PR #440 r2 TLA-cycle constraint (gateway/index.ts:20-24) is load-bearing; also (re)create the missing graph-composer-env-seam subprocess test the code already claims exists (gateway/index.ts:534-538), since a barrel alone cannot stop a dynamic-import composer from deep-importing any file — the Managed audit plus a CI check in the Managed repo is the real enforcement. (2) Split the kill-list into two buckets driven by the audit's output: (A) provably Open-dead AND Managed-unused → delete (connect-relay, acceptChoice, dead prompts+KNOWN_PROMPTS pairs — note prompts/template.test.ts:159 pins KNOWN_PROMPTS to the exact on-disk .md set, so edits must be paired); (B) Managed-live-by-contract → move behind the ABI barrel and freeze, do NOT delete: the per-chunk pipeline (explicitly documented Managed-live at build-landing-stack.ts:1117-1122; deletion is gated on Managed's substrate rework, out of this repo's control), the slug-picker hook, buildImportSubstrate, and the zero-consumer boot-helpers exports. (3) Launcher surface: decide mount-vs-remove as a product question (the Expo app client expects it); if removed, delete the app-side client code in the same PR, and re-home launcher-production-composer.test.ts's composition-mapping assertions (ISSUE #32) onto a surface every composer mounts rather than deleting them. (4) core-sdk validator: treat as SDK-surface decision, not dead code; if kept, its 622-line test stays. (5) Still extract the live leaves (extractJsonObject, history-import/types.ts, default-source-parser/SourceParser used by build-synthesis-import-runner.ts:49-52) into a neutral module first regardless of bucket, so the synthesis path stops importing from the per-chunk module.
verification.behavior_risk: Riskiest change: deleting the per-chunk import pipeline (proposal stratum 4) would break the Managed hosted history-import at the invisible ABI — build-landing-stack.ts:1117-1122 documents Managed still runs buildImportJobRunnerHook live; no test in THIS repo would catch it (the pinning tests would be deleted along with the code) and the failure surfaces only as a Managed boot/dynamic-import failure or broken import jobs in production. Second: any deep-import path the Managed composer uses that the one-time audit misses — the ABI barrel is advisory, not enforced, and the documented seam pin test (graph-composer-env-seam.test.ts) is already missing at HEAD. Third: the realmode-composer→wiring rename is behavior-safe only while path shims persist; removing shims before Managed is migrated breaks Managed module resolution at boot (fail-fast exit 1, so at least loud). Fourth: deleting the launcher surface silently orphans the shipped Expo app's launcher client (fails as 404s at runtime; only app-side unit tests exist, which don't exercise the HTTP mount).

findings.title: Config and auth fail open: ~150 env vars with no schema, process.env mutation as DI, divergent entrypoint defaults, no bind/auth coupling, and a WS upgrade any web page can hijack via a constant bearer
findings.diagnosis: ~150 NEUTRON_* vars are read across 71 files with defaults inlined everywhere and only the port validated; open/server.ts must mutate process.env before boot() because boot() re-reads env independently (the docblock admits they 'would silently desync'); the two entrypoints resolve different default DB paths, so a bare `bun start:gateway` boots healthz-only against the wrong database. Security: nothing couples bind mode to auth mode — NEUTRON_HOST=0.0.0.0 with the unconditional bypass:true auth exposes full agent control to anyone sending the compile-time constant `Bearer dev:owner`; the /ws/app/chat upgrade performs no Origin validation, so even on loopback any web page the owner visits can open the socket with the public constant token and drive the agent. .env.example documents ~15 of ~150 vars (the bypass toggles undocumented); four secrets models coexist with no rule, including a process-wide dev-channel SINK_TOKEN shared across all warm REPL sessions.
findings.proposal: config/ leaf exporting zod-validated resolveBootConfig(env,argv): every var read once, defaults only here, loud boot validation, redacted dump, threaded into boot() and the composer (transitional write-back shim, then delete reads incrementally); one DB-path/slug resolver preserving each precedence chain verbatim under characterization tests; generate .env.example from the schema with a CI equality check. Flagged security hardening (not silent refactor): refuse non-loopback bind under bypass auth (NEUTRON_ALLOW_INSECURE_BIND escape hatch); Origin allowlist on the WS upgrade (null accepted for native), per-boot random token replacing dev:owner injected into the served HTML; per-session sink token checked on /tool-call; document the secrets convention (SecretsStore for third-party secrets, the Anthropic env cred as the single explicit exception, codex plaintext registered for uninstall reaping).
findings.evidence: open/server.ts:39-73; gateway/index.ts:118-157,272,303-308; open/owner-identity.ts vs gateway/index.ts resolvers; gateway/http/app-ws-surface.ts:204-261 (no Origin); open/composer.ts:1977-1993 (bypass:true); landing/chat-react/config.ts:15-18; persistent-repl-substrate.ts:965,1003-1004 + tools-bridge.ts:52,173; .env.example vs 150-var census

verification.notes:
Every load-bearing claim checks out against HEAD.

CONFIG SPRAWL / process.env-as-DI:
- open/server.ts:47-73 mutates process.env (OWNER_HOME, NEUTRON_DB_PATH, NEUTRON_ONBOARDING_CHAT_COOKIE_SECRET) BEFORE boot(). The docblock at open/server.ts:39-45 explicitly admits boot() re-reads process.env independently for db/slug/host/port and a divergent env arg "would silently desync" (Codex r1 P2). Exactly as described.
- boot() re-reads env directly: resolveDbPath() gateway/index.ts:118-122 reads process.env['NEUTRON_DB_PATH']; resolveOwnerSlug() gateway/index.ts:147-157; resolveListenPort(process.argv, process.env, ...) at gateway/index.ts:272; hostname process.env.NEUTRON_HOST at gateway/index.ts:308.
- Only the port is validated (gateway/boot-helpers.ts:224-256 assertPort throws on NaN/out-of-range). No other var is schema-validated.
- Census: I counted 119 distinct NEUTRON_* tokens in .ts (112 outside tests), 159 across ts/sh/md; 72 files read NEUTRON_ (42 non-test). "~150 vars / 71 files" is loose but directionally honest once non-NEUTRON vars (SINK_TOKEN, CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, OPENAI_API_KEY, OLLAMA_BASE_URL, TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID) are added.
- .env.example documents ~12 NEUTRON_ + ~6 others (~18 total); NEUTRON_APP_WS_BYPASS is NOT in it. Confirmed.

DIVERGENT ENTRYPOINT DB DEFAULTS (real latent footgun):
- gateway/index.ts:118-122 resolveDbPath() default = ~/.local/share/neutron/owner.db.
- open/owner-identity.ts:40-65 resolveOpenDbPath() default = <NEUTRON_HOME>/project.db (NEUTRON_HOME defaults to ~/neutron). With the shipped .env.example (NEUTRON_HOME=$HOME/neutron/data, NEUTRON_DB_PATH commented), `bun start` → ~/neutron/data/project.db but `bun start:gateway` (package.json:49) → ~/.local/share/neutron/owner.db, and gateway boot with no composer serves ONLY /healthz (gateway/index.ts:589-599 loadGraphComposerFromEnv→undefined→boot({})→defaultHealthzHandler). Divergence + healthz-only confirmed. Caveat: start:gateway is not a supported Open path (Managed sets NEUTRON_DB_PATH via systemd), so this only bites a bare misinvocation — real but low-blast-radius.

SECURITY — WS Origin hijack (the sharpest, genuinely real point):
- gateway/http/app-ws-surface.ts:204-266: the /ws/app/chat upgrade reads ONLY url.searchParams token; NO Origin/Referer check anywhere before server.upgrade (grep confirms no origin handling). 
- open/composer.ts:1977-1993 hardcodes bypass:true then wraps the resolver to REJECT any user_id !== OWNER_USER_ID. So it is NOT "any dev:X" — only the constant `dev:owner` is accepted. But OWNER_USER_ID = 'owner' (open/owner-identity.ts:26) is a compile-time constant, and the served client defaults its token to `dev:${userId}` = `dev:owner` (landing/chat-react/config.ts:217). So on the DEFAULT loopback install, any web page the owner visits can JS-open ws://127.0.0.1:<port>/ws/app/chat?platform=web&token=dev:owner and drive the agent — no Origin gate, guessable constant token. This works on default loopback, not only on NEUTRON_HOST=0.0.0.0, which makes it arguably worse than the critic frames it. Confirmed.
- No bind/auth coupling: NEUTRON_ALLOW_INSECURE_BIND does not exist (grep only hits the proposal). Nothing refuses a 0.0.0.0 bind under dev-bypass. gateway/index.ts:303-308 just defaults hostname to 127.0.0.1 with a comment. Confirmed.
- dev-bypass resolver channels/adapters/app-ws/auth.ts:73-124 accepts raw `dev:<id>`; the owner-only narrowing lives only in the composer wrapper. Confirmed.

SINK_TOKEN (process-wide shared secret):
- persistent-repl-substrate.ts:965 ReplSink.token = randomBytes(24) is a MODULE SINGLETON (comment line 960 "shared across every per-turn substrate instance"), injected into every child at :1620 and :1636, checked at :1003-1004. tools-bridge.ts:52 reads process.env['SINK_TOKEN'], sent at :173. So it is per-boot-random (not compile-time) but shared across ALL warm REPL sessions in the process — the critic's "process-wide dev-channel SINK_TOKEN shared across all warm REPL sessions" is accurate; "compile-time constant" was said of dev:owner, not this. Confirmed.

FOUR SECRETS MODELS coexist: (1) SecretsStore AES-256-GCM keyfile (auth/secrets-store.ts, shared .neutron-aes-key), (2) api-key-store over SecretsStore (auth/api-key-store.ts), (3) project-credentials/store.ts reusing the same envelope, (4) the Anthropic env cred CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN/ANTHROPIC_API_KEY scrubbed into the child (runtime/adapters/claude-code/index.ts:19,73-74; persistent-repl-substrate.ts:1529), plus codex OAuth in auth/. Multiple models with no single documented convention — confirmed.

SEVERITY: P1 is honest. The Origin-less loopback WS hijack alone is a legitimate default-config vulnerability; the config sprawl is real maintainability debt with a concrete desync footgun the code itself documents.

PROPOSAL / behavior-preservation risk: The riskiest transform is unifying the DB-path/slug resolvers "verbatim" — but the two entrypoints GENUINELY diverge today (owner.db vs project.db), so a single resolver cannot preserve both unless it keys on entrypoint; the critic frames the divergence as a bug to fix, which conflicts with a strict no-functionality-change mandate. This must be called out explicitly, not silently unified. Existing tests give partial cover: open/__tests__/open-boot-shell.test.ts, migrations/runner.test.ts (resolveOpenDbPath), gateway/listener.test.ts (resolveListenPort) — but NO test pins the gateway-vs-open DB-default divergence, so a naive unification would pass CI while changing where a bare start:gateway writes. The security items are correctly flagged as behavior CHANGES (not silent refactor). Simpler safer alternative the critic under-weights: the WS Origin allowlist + per-boot random token is independently shippable and higher-value than the full zod config leaf, and could land first as a small security patch decoupled from the L-effort config consolidation.
verification.corrected_diagnosis: Directionally accurate; three precision corrections. (1) The app-ws auth is NOT "unconditional bypass:true accepting anyone" — open/composer.ts:1977-1993 wraps the bypass resolver to reject every user_id except the constant OWNER_USER_ID='owner'. The real hole is that `owner` is a public compile-time constant and the served client defaults its token to `dev:owner`, so only that one guessable token is accepted (the critic's own `Bearer dev:owner` phrasing is right; the "unconditional" adjective is not). (2) The Origin-less WS hijack works on the DEFAULT loopback install (any visited web page can open ws to 127.0.0.1 with token=dev:owner), not only under NEUTRON_HOST=0.0.0.0 — this is more severe than the finding's framing. (3) SINK_TOKEN is a per-boot randomBytes(24) module singleton shared across all warm sessions (persistent-repl-substrate.ts:965), not a compile-time constant; the shared-across-sessions concern is valid, the "constant" implication (if any) is not. Census numbers (~150 vars/71 files) are loose but honest.
verification.corrected_proposal: Keep the plan but split it and correct one internal contradiction. (a) Ship the security hardening FIRST as a small standalone patch — WS Origin allowlist on the /ws/app/chat upgrade (gateway/http/app-ws-surface.ts:261, accept null for native), a per-boot random app-ws token replacing the guessable `dev:owner` injected into the served HTML, and a refuse-non-loopback-bind-under-bypass guard — because these are independent, high-value behavior changes that should NOT wait on the L-effort config leaf. (b) For the config consolidation, do NOT claim to preserve the DB-path precedence "verbatim" in a single resolver: gateway/index.ts:118-122 and open/owner-identity.ts:61-65 genuinely diverge (owner.db vs project.db), so either preserve BOTH behind an entrypoint-keyed resolver under characterization tests, or land the unification as an explicit, separately-reviewed behavior FIX (with a note that bare `bun start:gateway` is not a supported Open path). Add a characterization test that pins each entrypoint's current default DB path BEFORE touching either resolver — none exists today.

findings.title: Persistence discipline: ProjectDb's serialization is unenforceable (no read API, ~70 raw() escapes), no table has an owner, 10 sidecars open with divergent pragmas, and trident's LLM-executed checkpoint writes can silently vanish
findings.diagnosis: ProjectDb's mutex exists so nothing lands inside a foreign open BEGIN, but the API has no read methods — every read in all 44 stores is unserialized, ~20 files run async transaction callbacks holding BEGIN across event-loop yields, and at least two production writes bypass mutex+busy-retry. No one-owning-store-per-table rule: projects is touched by 12 modules (including a second project-creation writer), onboarding_state's phase_state_json is written in two codecs (JS shallow-merge vs SQL json_remove). Ten sidecar DBs each open with a different pragma cocktail (some no WAL, some no busy_timeout, none busy-retry); 61 *_json columns have 16 hand-rolled parsers with three corrupt-data policies. Trident's harvest persists checkpoints by prompting an agent to run sqlite3 CLI at busy_timeout=0 under a must-not-fail contract — a silently lost 'argus-approved' checkpoint blocks the provenance-gated merge. The entity page format is triplicated (scribe + GBrainSyncHook hand-mirror entity-writer's private renderer/parsers that guard data-loss invariants).
findings.proposal: Widen ProjectDb (typed get/all, runSync returning {changes,lastInsertRowid}, prefer sync transaction callbacks), migrate raw() sites mechanically, lint-restrict raw() to migrations, and add the missing regression test (raw write inside a foreign async transaction; busy-retry sleeps stay ASYNC — sync sleeps starve the systemd watchdog). Adopt one-owning-module-per-table enforced by a conformance test keyed off expected-schema.txt. Add openSidecar(path,opts) applying canonical pragmas + busy-retry; extract 3-4 shared store helpers (JSON codec with explicit per-column corrupt policy — do NOT unify policies; ulid/now seams per the persistence-kit) — deliberately not an ORM. Quick win now: PRAGMA busy_timeout=5000 on trident's two sqlite3 invocations (idempotent UPDATEs, two lines). Extract runtime/entity-format.ts (extractCompiledTruth + frontmatter render/parse + KIND_TO_DIR), delete both mirrors behind a byte-stable golden round-trip test.
findings.evidence: persistence/db.ts:85-124,153-159; build-synthesis-import-runner.ts:163-174 (sync raw write); ~70 .raw() sites/30 files; 03-project-shells.ts:304-463 vs project-create.ts:86-92; resume-cron.ts:266-276 vs sqlite-state-store.ts:98-102; comment-store.ts:885 (no pragmas) vs db.ts:15-22; trident/inner-workflow.mjs:403-447 + orchestrator.ts:336-380; scribe/write-to-gbrain.ts:440-538

verification.notes: Checked every cited site at HEAD (d30280c). CORE CLAIMS ALL VERIFIED: (1) ProjectDb has no serialized read API — persistence/db.ts:119-127 documents prepare() as "NOT mutex-serialized"; the mutex (db.ts:54,216-226) covers only run/exec/transaction; raw() escape at db.ts:92-94. (2) raw() count: 74 sites in 31 non-test files (critic said ~70/30 — accurate). (3) Async transaction callbacks holding BEGIN across yields: 21 files (critic ~20 — accurate). (4) Writes bypassing mutex+busy-retry: confirmed at gateway/realmode-composer/build-synthesis-import-runner.ts:163-174 (sync raw write, deliberately — sync onProgress callback, per its own comment) and cores/free/research/src/research-orchestrator.ts:270,277,398 (raw DELETEs). (5) projects table touched by 12 modules: exact — grep finds 12 files; second project-creation writer confirmed: onboarding/wow-moment/actions/03-project-shells.ts:408-465 (reconcileProject + its own resolveBindTarget at 334-353) vs gateway/realmode-composer/project-create.ts:83-99,113+ (ensureProjectRow + duplicated resolveBindTarget). (6) onboarding_state dual codec confirmed: JS shallow-merge at onboarding/interview/sqlite-state-store.ts:98-102 vs raw SQL json_remove at onboarding/interview/resume-cron.ts:266-276. (7) TRIDENT CLAIM CONFIRMED EXACTLY and is the sharpest sub-finding: trident/inner-workflow.mjs:394-408 (checkpoint) and 428-448 (writeTerminalResult) prompt an LLM agent to run `sqlite3 "$dbPath" "UPDATE code_trident_runs ..."` with no busy_timeout (sqlite3 CLI default 0) under a "must NOT fail the build" instruction; code_trident_runs lives in the MAIN instance DB (migrations/expected-schema.txt:72) that the gateway writes constantly, so a SQLITE_BUSY collision silently drops the write; trident/orchestrator.ts:333-380 then fails an APPROVE run lacking inner_checkpoint='argus-approved' (provenance gate at :338,366-380). (8) Entity-format mirrors confirmed by the mirrors' own comments: scribe/write-to-gbrain.ts:525-526 "Mirror of entity-writer.ts:extractCompiledTruth (not exported there)" + frontmatter parse at :440-456; gbrain-memory/GBrainSyncHook.ts:43 "Mirrors KIND_TO_DIR in runtime/entity-writer.ts". CORRECTIONS (secondary counts inflated): sidecar DB opens outside persistence/migrations = 7, not 10 (research store-resolver.ts:209, calendar cache.ts:165, calendar pre-meeting-brief-queue-store.ts:324-326 [busy_timeout=100 only], email cache.ts:334-335 [FK only], code-gen sidecar/store.ts:414-415 [FK only], comment-store.ts:885, binary-store.ts:726-728 [WAL+FK]). comment-store.ts:885 "(no pragmas)" is overstated: applyProjectScopedMigrations→applyMigrations sets foreign_keys=ON on every open (migrations/runner.ts:64-68) and migration 0001's preamble sets persistent WAL on first apply (migrations/comments/0001:16-18); the real gap is busy_timeout=0 and synchronous defaulting to FULL on reopens. "*_json columns": 26 unique names / 33 occurrences in migrations/expected-schema.txt + 5 in sidecar migrations ≈ 31-38, not 61. "16 hand-rolled parsers" ≈ 15 files JSON.parsing row columns (close). "44 stores": 50 *store*.ts files — plausible. BEHAVIOR-PRESERVATION CHECK: persistence/persistence.test.ts:365-430 already tests mutex isolation for run/exec vs open transactions but nothing covers raw()/prepare().run() bypass — the proposed regression test is genuinely missing. Riskiest refactor step is mechanical raw() migration: (a) tx.raw().run inside transactions (persistence/app-chat-*.ts:115-174, connect/member-join.ts:167,184) exists to get changes/lastInsertRowid, which ProjectDb.run discards — the proposed runSync returning {changes,lastInsertRowid} covers this; (b) build-synthesis-import-runner.ts:163 MUST stay sync (sync onProgress callback; the file's own comment explains the unawaited-race hazard) — runSync also covers this, and the critic correctly warns busy-retry sleeps must stay async (canonical BUSY_TIMEOUT_MS=100, WRITE_MAX_RETRIES=15 at persistence/retry.ts:35-39). The trident quick win (inline PRAGMA busy_timeout in the two command strings, or `sqlite3 -cmd '.timeout 5000'`) is feasible, idempotent, and behavior-safe; note it edits an LLM prompt string, so trident/inner-workflow.test.ts should assert the command text. One-owning-module-per-table for `projects` is the riskiest consolidation (03-project-shells.ts:416-417 mixes TEXT-ISO projects timestamps with REAL epoch-seconds topics timestamps; INSERT OR IGNORE + soft-delete skip semantics are load-bearing per comments at :421-436) — the proposal wisely phrases it as a rule + conformance test rather than an immediate merge. Simpler alternative considered: moving trident checkpoint writes into the gateway process would eliminate the LLM-executed-SQL class entirely, but that is the rejected trident-v2 Option A direction (gateway-orchestrated dispatch); within the kept Workflow-inner-loop architecture the busy_timeout fix is the right move.
verification.corrected_diagnosis: As original, with four count corrections: (1) sidecar DBs opened outside persistence/db.ts and the migration runners number SEVEN, not ten (research, calendar cache, calendar brief-queue, email cache, code-gen, comments, binary-store) — the divergent-pragma claim holds (three open with foreign_keys only, one with busy_timeout=100 only, one WAL+FK, comment-store with none inline); (2) comment-store is not fully pragma-less — it gets foreign_keys=ON per-connection via applyProjectScopedMigrations and persistent WAL from migration 0001's preamble on first apply; its real gaps are busy_timeout=0 and synchronous reverting to FULL on every reopen; (3) *_json columns number ~31-38 across instance + sidecar schemas (26 unique names in expected-schema.txt), not 61; (4) the entity page format is not fully triplicated — entity-writer is canonical, scribe/write-to-gbrain.ts mirrors extractCompiledTruth + frontmatter parsing (self-documented as a mirror of an unexported function), and GBrainSyncHook mirrors only KIND_TO_DIR. Everything else checks out at the cited lines, including the trident LLM-executed sqlite3 checkpoint writes at busy_timeout=0 against the gateway's hot main DB under a must-not-fail contract, gated by orchestrator.ts's provenance check that fails an APPROVE without the recorded argus-approved checkpoint.
verification.corrected_proposal: Unchanged in substance; two refinements. First, when migrating tx.raw().run sites, note they exist specifically to read .changes/.lastInsertRowid (app-chat-* stores, connect/member-join) — runSync's return shape must land before those migrate. Second, the trident quick win should be paired with an assertion in trident/inner-workflow.test.ts on the generated command text (the fix edits an LLM prompt string, not code, so nothing else pins it); alternatively use `sqlite3 -cmd '.timeout 5000'` to keep the SQL argument untouched. Do not attempt to consolidate the two project-creation writers (03-project-shells reconcileProject vs project-create ensureProjectRow) in the same pass as the table-ownership rule — their duplicated resolveBindTarget + INSERT OR IGNORE + soft-delete-skip semantics are load-bearing (duplicate-shell and never-resurrect invariants documented inline); unify them behind one function only under a dedicated test capturing all three outcomes ('created'/'existing'/'skipped').
verification.behavior_risk: Highest risk: mechanical raw()-to-wrapper migration silently changing (a) the sync-write semantics of build-synthesis-import-runner.ts:163-174 (must remain synchronous — the onProgress callback is sync and the file's comment documents the unawaited-race hazard; converting to async ProjectDb.run would reintroduce it) and (b) the fail-fast semantics of writes that currently have NO busy-retry — adding retry to a formerly single-shot write changes latency and failure behavior under contention, and a sync sleep in a new runSync would starve the gateway watchdog (persistence/retry.ts is async-yield by design, tested at persistence.test.ts:249). Second risk: applying canonical pragmas to sidecars changes on-disk journal mode (delete→WAL) and synchronous level for stores that may implicitly depend on fail-fast busy_timeout=100 (pre-meeting-brief-queue). Third: any consolidation of the projects-table writers risks the duplicate-shell/never-resurrect invariants and the TEXT-ISO vs REAL-epoch timestamp split (03-project-shells.ts:416-417). Existing tests cover ProjectDb mutex isolation and busy-retry (persistence.test.ts:365-430,199-282) but NOT raw()/prepare().run() bypass, sidecar pragma state, or the trident checkpoint command text — the trident fix in particular has zero current test coverage of the generated sqlite3 invocation's pragma behavior.

findings.title: Error message strings are load-bearing API: typed substrate metadata is destroyed at the central seam and re-derived by regex (3 documented incidents), while 14+ deliberate propagation invariants exist only as comments
findings.diagnosis: The substrate error Event carries only message/retryable/retry_after_ms with no machine-readable class; credential-pool health decisions regex adapter prose (parseHttpStatusFromMessage, detectBinaryNotFound, detectChannelWedged, detectTurnTimeout — duplicated across two files), and the classifier docstrings record three production incidents where a missing regex laundered failures into 'all credentials in cooldown'. collectTokensToString flattens everything into `new Error('cc-llm-call: '+msg)`, discarding both typed fields; 429 detectors are duplicated sync-by-comment; the drain-to-text loop is re-implemented ~8-11x with divergent load-bearing semantics. At least 14 spots REQUIRE what looks like bad hygiene (sender registry MUST propagate throws or reconnect recovery dies; drains MUST NOT break on completion or adapter teardown breaks; was_new:true on persist failure) — exactly what a tidy-errors pass rewrites first, and several have no pinning tests. Rewording one adapter message silently reverts a P0 cooldown fix.
findings.proposal: FIRST commit the invariants inventory (INVARIANTS.md per subsystem) plus the ~4 missing pinning tests (drain-continues-past-completion, isFreezeTimeout per producer message, classifier outcomes per code, auth-claim polarity), and make 'invariants consulted' part of definition-of-done. Then: additive code? field (binary_not_found | channel_wedged | turn_timeout | http_status | ...) stamped at adapter emit sites; SubstrateCallError{code,retryable,retry_after_ms} thrown by collectTokensToString with today's EXACT message text; classifiers switch on code with regexes retained as fallback until parity is proven on captured production messages; collapse the duplicated classifier copies; extract runtime/substrate-text.ts drainToText(handle,{errorMode,onAbortCheck,timeout}) + ONE 429/exhaustion classifier, migrating one caller per commit (email's throw-by-design stub, scribe/reflection abort checks, no-break-on-completion each become explicit options).
findings.evidence: runtime/events.ts:55-56; build-llm-call-substrate.ts:437-541 (taxonomy) vs :823-826 (flatten); build-import-substrate.ts:409-416; job-runner.ts:2072-2083 + substrate-callers.ts:402-406 (dup regexes, 'kept in sync'); build-live-agent-turn.ts:1445-1447; chat-bridge.ts:202-219; app-ws/adapter.ts:184-196; 14-item inventory (errors report §8); drain census: 11 files

verification.notes: Checked every cited location at HEAD. CORE DIAGNOSIS ACCURATE: (1) runtime/events.ts:55 — error Event carries only {message, retryable, retry_after_ms}, no machine-readable class; header (events.ts:4-6) declares the union VERBATIM per spec "do not deviate". (2) gateway/realmode-composer/build-llm-call-substrate.ts:508-570 — credential-pool health decisions branch on regex classifiers over adapter prose; docstrings record exactly three production incidents: 2026-06-17 import-blocker (binary-not-found, :515-521, :663-665), 2026-06-26 dev-channel wedge (channel-wedged, :526-539, test :523), 2026-06-26 chat-blocker (turn timeout, :544-559). (3) collectTokensToString flatten confirmed at :823-825 — `throw new Error('cc-llm-call: '+ev.message)` discards retryable/retry_after_ms. (4) Downstream re-derivation confirmed: build-live-agent-turn.ts:1445-1447 isFreezeTimeout regexes the doubly-prefixed prose ('cc-llm-call: persistent-repl: turn timeout'). (5) 429 detector duplication confirmed: job-runner.ts:2072-2083 is429RetryableError vs substrate-callers.ts:402-406 is429ErrorMessage, sync-by-comment (:397-400 "Same regexes as job-runner.is429RetryableError... stay in sync"). (6) Drain census: 13 files contain `for await (const ev of` (9 are consumer drains) with genuinely divergent load-bearing semantics — email substrate-llm.ts:101-116 continues past completion and THROWS on missing completion, while collectTokensToString:840-845 RETURNS buf on missing completion; scribe/extract.ts:141-156 adds watchdog-abort checks. (7) Invariant samples check out: chat-bridge.ts:202-219 sender MUST propagate throws (comment-only rationale), app-ws/adapter.ts:184-196 persist-failure fail-soft. TWO FACTUAL ERRORS: (A) The four named classifiers are NOT "duplicated across two files" — they live once in build-llm-call-substrate.ts:611-744 and build-import-substrate.ts imports them (lines 62-65; header :32-36 says the dedup already happened). What IS duplicated is the ~45-line classification branch inside each builder's event loop, and it has a real semantic divergence the critic missed: build-import-substrate.ts:395-440 lacks the detectChannelWedged and detectTurnTimeout fast-paths, so on the import path those retryable no-status errors still fall through mapStatusForPoolCooldown(null,true)→429→reportFailure — the exact laundering the two P0 fixes eliminated on the llm-call path. (B) "several have no pinning tests" is overstated: classifier outcomes ARE pin-tested with exact producer message fixtures at the seam (build-llm-call-substrate.test.ts:497-617 asserts no reportFailure + re-emit per class), the freeze-timeout retry path is tested (build-live-agent-turn-timeout-retry.test.ts:39 pins 'cc-llm-call: persistent-repl: turn timeout'), and producer tests pin /turn timeout/ (persistent-repl-substrate.test.ts:398,482,579,848,863,885). The GENUINE gap is narrower and subtler: pinning is per-side, not cross-boundary — producer tests pin only the substring "turn timeout" while detectTurnTimeout (:742-744) requires /persistent-repl:\s*turn timeout/, so a reword to e.g. "turn timeout (persistent-repl)" passes every existing test yet silently reverts the P0 cooldown fix. That preserves the finding's headline claim in corrected form. SEVERITY: P1 honest for a behavior-preserving refactor audit — this is precisely the class of coupling a tidy-errors pass breaks silently, and it has caused three P0-class incidents already. RISKIEST PART OF PROPOSAL: "collapse the duplicated classifier copies" would silently CHANGE import-path cooldown behavior (the missing two fast-paths) unless the divergence is explicitly preserved or its removal approved as a bug fix; and drainToText unification flips opposite missing-completion defaults (email throws, collectTokensToString returns) and must preserve each caller's exact error prefix ('cc-llm-call: ' is itself matched by isFreezeTimeout's /\baborted\b/ path). Existing tests would catch prefix changes at the llm-call seam but not the import-path divergence (no test covers it).
verification.corrected_diagnosis: The substrate error Event (runtime/events.ts:55) carries only message/retryable/retry_after_ms with no machine-readable class, so error message strings are load-bearing API across three layers: the persistent-REPL adapter emits prose ('persistent-repl: turn timeout', 'spawn failed (channel-wedged; ...)'), buildLlmCallSubstrate classifies credential-pool health by regexing that prose (detectBinaryNotFound/detectChannelWedged/detectTurnTimeout/parseHttpStatusFromMessage, build-llm-call-substrate.ts:611-744, applied :508-570), collectTokensToString then flattens everything into `new Error('cc-llm-call: '+msg)` destroying the typed fields (:823-825), and build-live-agent-turn.ts:1445-1447 re-derives freeze-vs-fault by regexing the flattened string. Classifier docstrings record three production incidents (2026-06-17 binary-not-found import-blocker; 2026-06-26 dev-channel wedge; 2026-06-26 turn-timeout chat-blocker) where a missing regex laundered failures into "all credentials in cooldown". CORRECTIONS: the four classifiers are NOT duplicated across files — build-import-substrate.ts imports them (:62-65) — but the ~45-line classification branch IS re-implemented in both builders' event loops WITH a live divergence: the import path (build-import-substrate.ts:395-440) lacks the channel-wedged and turn-timeout fast-paths, so those failures still launder into 429 cooldowns during imports. The genuinely duplicated-by-comment pair is the 429 detectors (job-runner.ts:2072-2083 vs substrate-callers.ts:402-406). The pinning-test gap is cross-boundary, not absent: classifier outcomes and the freeze-timeout path are pin-tested with exact message fixtures (build-llm-call-substrate.test.ts:497-617; build-live-agent-turn-timeout-retry.test.ts:39), and producer tests pin /turn timeout/ (persistent-repl-substrate.test.ts:398 etc.), but producer and classifier are tested against independently-written strings — a reword keeping the substring "turn timeout" but breaking the /persistent-repl:\s*turn timeout/ shape passes every test and silently reverts the P0 fix. The drain-to-text loop is re-implemented ~9x with divergent load-bearing semantics (email throws on missing completion, collectTokensToString returns partial buffer; scribe adds abort checks), and comment-only propagation invariants (chat-bridge.ts:202-219 throw-propagate; app-ws/adapter.ts:184-196 fail-soft persist) verified as claimed.
verification.corrected_proposal: Keep the ordering (inventory + tests first, then additive typed codes), with four corrections. (1) The missing tests are cross-boundary, not per-side: add a test that drives the REAL adapter error text through buildLlmCallSubstrate's classifier (or better, extract the producer message strings/prefixes into shared exported constants — e.g. TURN_TIMEOUT_MESSAGE in one module referenced by both persistent-repl-substrate.ts and detectTurnTimeout — making the string contract a compile-time link instead of a regex convention; this is smaller and safer than the code? field and can ship first). (2) The additive `code?` field on the error Event requires updating the spec docs it is declared VERBATIM against (events.ts:4-6 cites docs/engineering-plan.md §B.P1 and substrate-adapter spec §2.2) in the same commit. (3) Do NOT blindly "collapse the duplicated classifier copies": build-import-substrate.ts:395-440 deliberately-or-accidentally lacks the channel-wedged and turn-timeout fast-paths; collapsing changes import-path cooldown behavior. First surface this divergence as an explicit decision (latent bug fix vs preserved behavior) — it is likely an unintended gap and the strongest single piece of evidence in this finding. (4) Scope down the drainToText extraction or defer it: it is the riskiest, lowest-payoff part — the ~9 drains have opposite missing-completion defaults (email throws, collectTokensToString returns partial buffer) and downstream-matched error prefixes ('cc-llm-call: aborted' feeds isFreezeTimeout's /\baborted\b/); if pursued, each caller's exact prefix and terminal-event semantics become explicit options and migrate one caller per commit as proposed. The SubstrateCallError{code,retryable,retry_after_ms} with today's exact message text is sound (all downstream consumers regex .message or use instanceof Error, both preserved).
verification.behavior_risk: Highest: collapsing the two classification-branch copies silently adds channel-wedged/turn-timeout fast-paths to the import path (or removes them from the llm-call path), changing credential-cooldown behavior during imports — no existing test covers the import-path divergence. Second: drainToText unification flipping email's throw-on-missing-completion into collectTokensToString's return-partial-buffer default (or vice versa), and altering error-prefix strings that downstream regexes (isFreezeTimeout) depend on — seam tests pin fixtures per-file, so a unified helper with subtly different defaults can pass each file's tests while changing cross-file behavior.

findings.title: persistent-repl-substrate.ts: 17 clusters over 13 module-global singletons make the production substrate unsplittable, with its enabling seams and contract tests missing
findings.diagnosis: The 4,009-line file holds the entire warm-REPL runtime; substrate instances are stateless facades over 13 ambient module globals (pool, childByKey, sink, watchdog families, respawn gates...), so any split either cycles imports or threads globals ad hoc, and the 48-file fake-PtyHost suite resets state only by afterEach convention. Contract gaps compound the risk: the CC adapter violates the locked SessionHandle contract (iterator.return() does not cancel — consumers compensate via unwritten no-break-drain conventions) with zero tests pinning either side; isAlive is an undocumented duck-typed superset consumed via structural casts; /tool-call authorizes on the process-wide token only, untested; the only real PTY-bind proof (the 2026-06-26 P0's guard) is describe.skipIf'd and has never run in CI; and AgentSpec's 'locked' shape cites docs/engineering-plan.md which does not exist in the repo, having drifted into an undeclared per-adapter dialect (turn_timeout_ms CC-only, spec.session dead, ZERO_USAGE vs real usage).
findings.proposal: Sequence: (1) reify one PoolRuntime object owning all 13 structures, constructed once at module scope and threaded explicitly; land createTestPoolRuntime() and re-point existing suites BEFORE splitting (do not fork the sink). (2) Mechanical split along the file's own section banners into ~8 modules behind a re-export barrel; invariant comments (sink.register before spawn, identity-guarded eviction, one-owner pendingChildKills, watchdog scoping) travel verbatim. (3) Contract tests: iterator.return semantics (pin the deviation or the fix, explicitly — adding finally→cancel is a deliberate change), /tool-call from a non-bridge session, and a nightly NEUTRON_PTY_E2E lane on a credential-bearing runner. (4) Re-home AgentSpec authority in-repo with a per-adapter dialect table pinned in an extended adapter-equivalence suite.
findings.evidence: persistent-repl-substrate.ts:934-1101,1384-1404,1576-2314,3053-3160; pool-key-namespace.test.ts:31-33 (convention reset); runtime/session-handle.ts:8-15 vs event-channel.ts:45-59 (0 test hits); persistent-repl-substrate.ts:1036-1062 (token-only auth); dev-channel-pty-bind.e2e.test.ts:17-19; runtime/substrate.ts:4-6 (absent spec file)

verification.notes: Checked every cited claim at HEAD. VERIFIED EXACTLY: (1) File is 4,009 lines (wc -l). (2) Exactly 13 mutable module-global singletons: replToolBridge :934, sink :1094, pool :1384, childByKey :1391, ephemeralSessions :1398, pendingChildKills :1404, activeWatchdogs :3053, activeModelWatchdogs :3058, supervisedBySessionKey :3115, respawnGates :3151, wedgeAlertState :3153, cwdDriftRespawnState :3157, cwdDriftAlertState :3160 (all in runtime/adapters/claude-code/persistent/persistent-repl-substrate.ts). (3) 48 test files in persistent/__tests__/, reset only by per-file convention — pool-key-namespace.test.ts:31-33 afterEach(shutdownAllPersistentRepls). (4) SessionHandle contract deviation is real: runtime/session-handle.ts:13-15 mandates "adapters MUST also cancel from inside the events iterator's finally so iterator.return() is enough", but the handle exposes the bare EventChannel (persistent-repl-substrate.ts:3005 `events: channel`) and event-channel.ts:45-59 has no finally/cancel hookup; consumers compensate with explicit handle.cancel() (agent-dispatch/substrate-turn.ts:81-98). No test pins iterator.return semantics. (5) isAlive superset confirmed (persistent-repl-substrate.ts:2998-3019) consumed via structural cast (onboarding/synthesis/synthesis-session.ts:574-576) — though it IS comment-documented on both sides and the consumer drain behavior IS tested (synthesis-session.test.ts:568-575); only the producer side and the interface are unpinned. (6) /tool-call authorizes on the single process-wide X-Sink-Token (:1003-1005) and is routed BEFORE session lookup against the process-global bridge (:1017-1063). (7) dev-channel-pty-bind.e2e.test.ts:31,44 is describe.skipIf(!NEUTRON_PTY_E2E) — the 2026-06-26 P0 regression guard never runs in CI. (8) docs/engineering-plan.md does not exist anywhere in the repo (find confirms), yet substrate.ts:4-6 and session-handle.ts:4 cite it as the locked authority. (9) turn_timeout_ms/turn_absolute_ceiling_ms consumed only by the CC persistent adapter (grep: no hits in gpt-5-5-api/ or gpt-5-5-codex-cli/); ZERO_USAGE at :186 emitted on every completion (:1322). (10) adapter-equivalence.test.ts exists (tests/integration/) and is mocked-transport only, so "extend" is the right verb. FACTUAL ERRORS FOUND (see corrected_diagnosis): "spec.session dead" is wrong — its PRESENCE is load-bearing (ephemeral-vs-pooled gate, :2628-2642, fail-fast invariant tested in ephemeral-oneshot-isolation.test.ts:220-238); only .id is unused for resume (registry-resume instead, documented at substrate.ts:26-31). "/tool-call ... untested" overclaims — tool-bridge.test.ts:223-243 tests dispatch/error-shaping/503/400; what's untested is the AUTH boundary (no bad-token 401 test; grep confirms every test posts the valid token). "Holds the entire warm-REPL runtime"/"unsplittable" is overstated: ~40 sibling modules are ALREADY extracted (wedge-detector, respawn-strategy, heartbeat-watchdog, repl-registry, pty-host, pending-respawns-queue, etc.); the 4,009 lines are the globals-entangled orchestration core, and the extraction pattern is proven in-repo. "17 clusters" is unverifiable as stated (the 13-global count is what matters). SEVERITY: P1 is honest — this is the production substrate core of a no-behavior-change refactor, its contract authority is a dead link, and its only real-PTY proof never runs. BEHAVIOR RISK of the proposal is real but the critic pre-flags the two worst traps correctly (sink forking; finally→cancel would trigger the ABANDON-POISON path :3021-3041, poisoning warm sessions when consumers break early — substrate-turn.ts early-returns on completion inside the for-await). One risk the critic missed: a barrel split changes module-init order; sink lazy-start and the late-bound replToolBridge setter (wired by composeProductionGraph after the substrate is built, per :914-925 banner) must stay order-insensitive.
