verification.corrected_diagnosis: The 4,009-line persistent-repl-substrate.ts is the globals-entangled orchestration core of the warm-REPL runtime (much of the runtime is ALREADY extracted into ~40 sibling modules — the proven pattern; what remains is the part no one could split). Substrate instances are stateless facades over exactly 13 ambient module globals (replToolBridge :934, sink :1094, pool :1384, childByKey :1391, ephemeralSessions :1398, pendingChildKills :1404, plus 7 watchdog/supervision/alert maps at :3053-3160), and the 48-file fake-PtyHost suite resets them only by per-file afterEach(shutdownAllPersistentRepls) convention (pool-key-namespace.test.ts:31-33). Contract gaps compound the risk: the handle's events iterator violates session-handle.ts:13-15 (bare EventChannel at :3005, no finally→cancel in event-channel.ts:45-59) — consumers compensate with explicit handle.cancel() calls (agent-dispatch/substrate-turn.ts:81-98) and zero tests pin either side; isAlive is a duck-typed superset consumed via structural cast (synthesis-session.ts:574-576) — comment-documented and consumer-tested, but unpinned on the producer side and absent from the interface; /tool-call's dispatch IS tested (tool-bridge.test.ts:223-243) but its authorization boundary is not — it accepts the single process-wide X-Sink-Token (:1003-1005) with no per-session scoping and no rejected-token test; the only real-PTY bind proof (the 2026-06-26 P0 guard, dev-channel-pty-bind.e2e.test.ts:44) is describe.skipIf'd behind NEUTRON_PTY_E2E and never runs in CI; and the "locked" AgentSpec/SessionHandle shapes cite docs/engineering-plan.md (substrate.ts:4-6) which does not exist in the repo, while the spec has drifted into an undeclared per-adapter dialect (turn_timeout_ms/turn_absolute_ceiling_ms CC-only; usage always ZERO_USAGE :186/:1322). NOT part of the dialect drift: spec.session is NOT dead — its presence gates ephemeral-vs-pooled dispatch with a fail-fast invariant (:2628-2642, tested in ephemeral-oneshot-isolation.test.ts); only spec.session.id is unused for resume, which substrate.ts:26-31 already documents.
verification.corrected_proposal: Keep the critic's sequence but downgrade step (1)'s mechanism: instead of reifying-and-threading a PoolRuntime object (a wider, riskier change than the split needs), first extract the 13 globals verbatim into one pool-state.ts module that the split modules import — identical object identities and lifetimes by construction, no import cycles (the state module imports no stateful siblings), no call-site threading. Preserve replToolBridge as a mutable late-bound slot (it is wired by composeProductionGraph AFTER the substrate is built — :914-925 — so it can never become a constructor parameter) and keep sink lazy-start order-insensitive, since a barrel split changes module-init order. createTestPoolRuntime()/DI threading can follow as a separate, optional testability step once the split is proven behavior-identical. Steps (2)-(4) stand as written: mechanical split along the ~9 existing banner sections with invariant comments traveling verbatim (sink.register-before-spawn, identity-guarded unregisterIf :992-998, one-owner pendingChildKills, ABANDON-POISON :3021-3041); contract tests for iterator.return semantics (pinning today's no-cancel behavior first — adding finally→cancel is a deliberate change that would poison warm sessions for early-breaking consumers) plus a rejected-token /tool-call auth test (the dispatch path already has coverage); a nightly NEUTRON_PTY_E2E lane; and re-homing AgentSpec authority in-repo with a per-adapter dialect table — corrected to record spec.session presence-is-load-bearing/.id-unused rather than "dead" — pinned in an extended tests/integration/adapter-equivalence.test.ts.
verification.behavior_risk: Riskiest behavior changes a refactor could silently introduce: (1) forking the ReplSink or pool map across split modules — two sinks means spawned children POST replies to the wrong port/token, turns never settle, and the inactivity watchdog respawns healthy sessions; the 48-file suite would likely catch gross breakage since fakes round-trip through the real sink, but a subtle double-instantiation under Bun module resolution might not fail deterministically. (2) "Fixing" the SessionHandle contract by adding finally→cancel to the events iterator — any consumer that breaks out of for-await mid-turn (substrate-turn.ts returns from inside the loop) would then trigger cancel()'s ABANDON-POISON path (:3021-3041), marking warm sessions poisoned and forcing respawns; NO existing test pins current iterator.return behavior, so this would ship silently. (3) Module-init/order effects from the barrel: replToolBridge is late-bound by composeProductionGraph and a turn before wiring fail-softly sees no tools (:914-925) — reordering could turn fail-soft into a hard 503 at boot. (4) Real-PTY spawn/bind regressions are invisible in CI (the only guard is skipIf'd), so any split touching build-repl-argv/post-spawn-assertion interplay is unverified until a credentialed runner executes NEUTRON_PTY_E2E=1.

findings.title: Dual durable transcript: every agent reply is persisted twice at different fidelity, WS resume drops all agent metadata (buttons render as plain text), and no hydration-parity test exists
findings.diagnosis: One live reply persists into button_prompts ([[OPTIONS]]-stripped body, options in options_json, user turns as empty-body pre-resolved prompts — schema abuse) AND into app_chat_messages (body only). Hydration splits by transport: HTTP history reads button_prompts (buttons present) while WS resume replays app_chat rows whose envelope reconstruction carries no options/prompt_id/citations/doc_refs — a reconnecting client permanently sees button prompts as plain text. HTTP history, resume, sidebar rail, reflection, and message-search each read a different store; PR #122 needed 5 synchronized copy sites for 4 new fields and missed one (caught only by external review); consumers must remember latestPromptByTopic-not-body (already a Codex P1). The app_chat side is itself four copy-pasted stores (932 LOC). This is the largest data-model duplication in the instance DB and the invariant most likely to silently break during refactor.
findings.proposal: FIRST land the hydration-parity golden — seed one transcript, render via live emit, HTTP history, and WS resume, assert field-level equivalence with today's divergences explicitly marked known-lossy (it currently fails; that is the contract). THEN, staged and LAST in the extraction order: make app_chat_messages the single durable transcript (nullable options/prompt_id/citations/doc_refs JSON columns, write-through at emit), point readers over one at a time, shrink button_prompts to prompt lifecycle (emit/resolve/expire/idempotency) with resolutions writing through; fold the four app-chat stores into one generic per-topic event-log behind the existing interfaces; pair with the shared frame-schema leaf (finding on wire-types) so a new field is one edit. Pagination tiebreaks (rowid-DESC vs composite-tuple), the expired-row emit matrix, was_delivered re-render, and pickAgentMeta incoming-wins are load-bearing — pinned by the parity test + existing button-store suites before any schema move.
findings.evidence: build-live-agent-turn.ts:970-1016; channels/adapters/app-ws/adapter.ts:174-199,806-841 (lossy resume); channels/button-store.ts:289-368,697-815; chat-history-surface.ts:182; chat-core/sync-engine.ts:87-93 (missed copy site, a133b38); persistence/app-chat-{store,receipts,reactions,edits}.ts (932 LOC)

verification.notes: Checked every cited location at HEAD d30280c. (1) Dual durable write CONFIRMED: a live composer reply persists into button_prompts via buttonStore.emit (gateway/realmode-composer/build-live-agent-turn.ts:979-996, [[OPTIONS]]-stripped body per lines 965-974) AND into app_chat_messages via buildAppWsSendReply -> adapter.send -> chat_log.append (open/composer.ts:2701-2731; channels/adapters/app-ws/adapter.ts:174-196 — body only). (2) Lossy WS resume CONFIRMED: appChatRowToEnvelope agent branch (adapter.ts:831-840) rebuilds only body/message_id/ts/seq/project_id; AppChatRow has no meta columns at all (persistence/app-chat-store.ts:20-32), so the loss is server-durable, unrecoverable by any client. (3) Schema abuse CONFIRMED: persistInertUserTurn writes user turns as empty-body pre-resolved prompts with text in resolution_freeform_text (channels/button-store.ts:334-368); persistInertAgentTurn at 289-311. (4) HTTP history reads button_prompts (gateway/http/chat-history-surface.ts:182 -> listHistoryByTopic) but note it only accepts web:<user> topics (lines 162-178). (5) Missed-copy-site claim CONFIRMED: PR #122 (a133b38) added image_urls/citations/doc_refs/deep_link and needed an in-PR follow-up commit 'fix(chat): address Codex review — propagate rich metadata through SyncEngine' — the sync-engine copy site (chat-core/sync-engine.ts:87-94) was missed and caught by external review. (6) 932 LOC exact: app-chat-{store,receipts,reactions,edits}.ts = 222+211+244+255. (7) Load-bearing subtleties real: listHistoryByTopic paginates by (created_at DESC, prompt_id DESC) (button-store.ts:697-714) while latestTurnByTopic/latestPromptByTopic deliberately use rowid DESC with a documented reflection bug rationale (736-751, 773, 810); ghost filter resolved_at IS NOT NULL OR expires_at > now on all three; one-reply dedupe gates live re-send on button_prompts idempotency was_new (composer.ts:2776-2804). (8) No hydration-parity test CONFIRMED: resume-envelope.test.ts is decode-only (34 lines); nothing asserts a replayed agent_message carries options — button-store.test.ts covers only the button_prompts side. Two corrections found: (a) 'a reconnecting client permanently sees button prompts as plain text' is overstated for the common same-device case — pickAgentMeta (chat-core/store.ts:151-175) falls back to existing local meta, and the web client uses OPFS when available (chat-core/stores/opfs-store.ts:198-204 snapshots full ChatMessage JSON incl. meta), so a same-device reconnect replays only the gap and keeps buttons; the loss is real for any store-cold hydration: second device, cleared site data, OPFS-unavailable browsers (in-memory fallback -> every reload), and the PR #128 stale-store auto-reset. (b) 'each read a different store' is wrong as phrased: sidebar rail (button-store.ts:817+ / chat-topics-surface), reflection (latestTurnByTopic), message-search (gateway/composition/message-search-wiring.ts:84), and HTTP history ALL read button_prompts; only WS resume reads app_chat_messages. It is 5+ readers split across 2 divergent transcripts (plus the client-side third copy in chat-core stores), not 5 stores. Severity P1 is honest for a refactor-risk audit: this is the core chat transcript, the divergence is server-durable, and the copy-site fan-out failure mode is empirically demonstrated (PR #122). Proposal is feasible and correctly staged (parity golden first); riskiest behavior changes if readers move off button_prompts: the expired-unresolved ghost filter (app_chat rows never expire — history composition would change), created_at+rowid ordering vs per-topic seq, the inert user-turn interleave that build-live-agent-turn.ts:1402 and reflection depend on for prior-context assembly, and the was_new-gated one-reply dedupe. Existing suites pin the button_prompts side only; nothing today would catch a resume-fidelity regression or cross-store divergence — the proposed parity golden is precisely the missing guard.
verification.corrected_diagnosis: As critic wrote, with two precision fixes: (1) The resume metadata loss is not 'permanent for a reconnecting client' in the common case — chat-core's pickAgentMeta (store.ts:151-175) preserves locally-held options on a metadata-less replay, and OPFS/device-SQLite stores persist meta across reloads. The loss is server-durable and bites every store-cold hydration: a second device, cleared site data, browsers without OPFS (createWebStore silently falls back to in-memory, so every page reload replays the whole transcript lossy), and the PR #128 stale-store reset. app_chat_messages has no meta columns at all (AppChatRow, app-chat-store.ts:20-32), so no client can ever recover buttons/citations/doc_refs for history it did not witness live. (2) The reader split is 5+ readers over TWO divergent durable transcripts, not five stores: HTTP history, sidebar rail, reflection, prior-turn context (build-live-agent-turn.ts:1402), and server message-search all read button_prompts; WS resume (plus receipts/reactions/edits replay) reads app_chat_messages; the client keeps a third copy in chat-core stores with its own search. Everything else in the diagnosis checks out exactly, including the 932-LOC four-store duplication and the PR #122 missed-copy-site incident.
verification.corrected_proposal: The staged plan is sound and the parity-golden-first ordering is right. One addition: stage 0 is separable and cheap — an S-sized fidelity fix (add a nullable meta JSON column to app_chat_messages, stamp it in adapter.send where the envelope already carries options/prompt_id/citations/doc_refs, extend appChatRowToEnvelope) closes the only user-visible hole without touching button_prompts, its readers, or any ordering/expiry semantics. Land that with the parity golden immediately; the full unification (shrink button_prompts to prompt lifecycle, fold the four app-chat stores) then becomes a lower-risk cleanup whose residual urgency is P2 and can stay last in the extraction order as proposed. When moving readers off button_prompts, the parity golden must additionally pin: the expired-unresolved ghost filter (app_chat rows never expire — history composition changes otherwise), the created_at+rowid vs per-topic-seq ordering mismatch, the inert user-turn interleave that prior-context assembly and reflection depend on, and the was_new-gated live re-send dedupe in onboardingMsgHolder.emit (composer.ts:2776-2804).
verification.behavior_risk: Highest-risk behavior for the refactor to silently break: (1) history composition when readers move to app_chat — button_prompts' ghost filter (resolved_at IS NOT NULL OR expires_at > now, button-store.ts:702/712) drops expired unresolved prompts from hydrated history while app_chat rows never expire, so a naive reader swap resurrects timeout/proactive ghosts; (2) the dual recency semantics — pagination by (created_at DESC, prompt_id DESC) vs last-inserted by rowid DESC (button-store.ts:697-714 vs 753-815) — collapsing them reintroduces the documented same-millisecond reflection bug; (3) the one-reply-per-turn dedupe: duplicate finalize suppression rides on button_prompts idempotency was_new (composer.ts:2781-2804), so moving the durable write without keeping that key double-posts closings/openings; (4) prior-turn context and button-backed answers read [[OPTIONS]]-stripped bodies + options_json via latestPromptByTopic (build-live-agent-turn.ts:1402, button-store.ts:790-815) — re-parsing body instead is a known Codex P1 trap. No existing test would catch (1) or a resume-fidelity regression; only the button-store suite pins (2)-(4) on the button_prompts side.

findings.title: Chat delivery architecture: chat-bridge god file (~510 dead lines), two sender registries with opposite failure semantics, an adapterless ChannelRouter wired as trident's delivery sink, and persisted channel-kind vocabulary forks
findings.diagnosis: gateway/http/chat-bridge.ts (3.1k) stacks 9 clusters including a verifiably dead slug-picker engine hook and a second full inbound dispatch engine, with scribe fan-out pasted 4x. Two live sender registries exist for two topic grammars (web: vs app:) — admitted mirrors with OPPOSITE load-bearing failure semantics (chat-bridge must propagate sender throws for crash recovery; app-ws must evict and continue) — so timer/cron producers must guess: the PR#105 deliver-to-nobody bug was patched per-producer twice. The documented ChannelAdapter/ChannelRouter architecture has zero registered production adapters, yet the module graph wires the router as trident's terminal-delivery sink where send can only throw (swallowed into a log) — silent delivery loss by construction the moment a run carries a chat_id. Channel-kind vocabulary forks in PERSISTED rows ('app_socket' vs 'app-socket', 'webhook' as a system sentinel, no 'web' kind) and topic_id spans two keyspaces under one name.
findings.proposal: PR1: delete the slug-picker block after the Managed-ABI check. PR2: type moves per the contracts pass (registry type to leaf, kill the sideways ChatCommandFilter import and the http↔wiring cycle). PR3: split chat-bridge along the partitions its 10 test files already use, keeping the 4-entry closure whole. Then: ONE subscription registry keyed by parseAnyTopicId behind deliver(topic, envelope) owning durable-row-first + push-best-effort, with an explicit per-consumer failure policy (propagate vs evict — mandatory, or a merge silently kills one guarantee) and a two-stack delivery-conformance test; one shared chat-policy module (filter execution + scribe fan-out) for both stacks. Decide the router seam once: register real adapters + a composition test that every ChannelKind a run row can carry has an adapter, OR delete the router-as-sink wiring and declare direct composition the architecture. Channel-kind unification type-first (branded TopicUuid vs ChannelTopicId, merged union with read-side normalizers — never rewrite persisted rows).
findings.evidence: chat-bridge.ts:162-301,1052-2006,2027-2511 (dead), 2511-3043, scribe at :1748-1949; session-registry.ts:5-9; channels/router.ts:60,143-151; build-core-modules.ts:238-245,341-342; trident/tick.ts:169-179; channels/types.ts:12 vs button-primitive.ts:57; open/composer.ts:1905-1911 (PR#105 reroute)

verification.notes:
Verified every major claim by reading the code at HEAD.

1) God file + dead slug-picker block — CONFIRMED. gateway/http/chat-bridge.ts is 3113 lines. The slug-picker engine hook (ProcessSlugPickerReplyFn at chat-bridge.ts:2027, buildSlugPickerEngineHook at :2177) has ZERO importers repo-wide — grep for both symbols hits only chat-bridge.ts itself (tests build their own stub SlugPickerEngineHook in tests/integration/personality-name-slug-projects-flow.open.test.ts:223). gateway/index.ts (the boot shell) never wires it. The dead block is ~2012-2483 (~470 lines, critic said ~510 for 2027-2511 — right ballpark). Critical caveat the critic already anticipated: the doc comments (chat-bridge.ts:2013-2018, 2060-2062) say the MANAGED boot shell injects processSlugPickerReply — Managed is a separate repo, so the "Managed-ABI check" in PR1 is mandatory, not optional.

2) Second inbound dispatch engine — CONFIRMED. handleProjectTopicInbound (chat-bridge.ts:2664→~3005) is a full parallel dispatch path for project topics (own live-agent eligibility gate, own agent-turn runner, own persistence via persistProjectStubTurn/persistProjectUserTurnOnly at :2545/:2601, engagement-mode + delegate hooks), called from the primary engine at :1779.

3) Scribe fan-out pasted 4x — CONFIRMED at chat-bridge.ts:1748, 1811, 1896, 1949 (identical try/catch + scribe_hook_threw log). There is a FIFTH copy in open/composer.ts:~3168-3186 (app-ws receiver side), which strengthens the shared chat-policy-module proposal.

4) Two registries, opposite load-bearing failure semantics — CONFIRMED, with a path correction: the app registry is channels/adapters/app-ws/session-registry.ts (NOT gateway/http/session-registry.ts). Its header (:5-9) literally says it "Mirrors the WebChatSenderRegistry shape in gateway/http/chat-bridge.ts … so a future consolidation can fold both into one". App-ws send() evicts throwing senders and continues (:127-148, "must NOT abort the fan-out"); WebChatSenderRegistry.send() deliberately PROPAGATES throws (chat-bridge.ts:202-218, T10 comment: catching would silently downgrade closed-socket failures; propagation drives InterviewError('send_failed') → 4001 teardown → delivered_at stays NULL → reconnect re-emit). Both semantics are documented as review-mandated (Codex r1 P1 / PR#142 P2).

5) PR#105-class per-producer patching — CONFIRMED, and it is actually THREE instances, not two: reminders rerouted to app: registry (open/composer.ts:1860-1890), proactive brief rerouted (:1904-1916, "same live-delivery bug as reminders, now fixed for both"), AND the proactive sink override inside build-core-modules.ts:873-876 ("Override sink … wins; else the core router … would drop a timer-fired web post"). Trident delivery got NO such override — proving the guess-the-registry hazard is still live.

6) Adapterless router as trident sink — CONFIRMED. Zero production registerAdapter call sites in this repo (grep: only channels/router.ts:60 definition + tests). open/composer.ts never sets CompositionInput.channel_router (zero refs), so build-core-modules.ts:243-245 constructs a bare `new ChannelRouter(...)`; :341-342 wires it as buildTridentDelivery's sink; ChannelRouter.send throws "no channel adapter registered" for EVERY kind (router.ts:143-151); tick.ts:169-179 swallows it into console.error. trident/delivery.ts:145 only no-ops when chat_id is null, so any run carrying a chat_id loses its terminal chat message by construction. Two nuances: (a) the loss is partially masked — the board-reconcile observer (build-core-modules.ts:350-360) still flips the Work Board lane, so the UI shows completion even though the chat message dies; (b) the router seam appears to be live in the external Managed repo (build-telegram-webhook.ts:58 expects composition.channel_router; the production AppWsAdapter at open/composer.ts:3206 is composed directly, never router-registered), so the "delete the router" branch of the proposal also needs the Managed check.

7) Vocabulary forks — CONFIRMED. channels/types.ts:12 ChannelKind = 'telegram'|'app_socket'|'webhook'|'cli' vs button-primitive.ts:57 ChannelKindForButton = 'telegram'|'app-socket'|'webhook'; '__system__'/'webhook' sentinel persisted at button-store.ts:307,950; no 'web' kind despite the web: topic grammar. topic_id two-keyspace confirmed: trident/delivery.ts:150-153 stuffs channel_topic_id into Topic.topic_id while router.ts:179 puts the topics-row UUID there.

8) Sideways import + cycle — CONFIRMED. chat-bridge.ts:1009,1711 type-import ChatCommandFilter from './app-ws-surface.ts'; chat-bridge.ts:116 VALUE-imports ONBOARDING_HANDOFF_SKIP_FOR_NOW_VALUE from ../realmode-composer/build-onboarding-handoff.ts while realmode-composer value-imports back (build-wow-dispatcher.ts:90 renderButtonPromptForWeb) — a real http↔wiring cycle. Note one prior cycle was already broken via web-topic-id.ts (:8-11), showing the pattern of leaf extraction works here.

Minor factual corrections: session-registry path (above); test-file count is 8 chat-bridge-named files in gateway/http/__tests__ (31 test files import chat-bridge repo-wide), not 10; dead block ~470 lines not ~510. None change the substance. Severity P1 is honest: the dual-registry seam has caused ≥3 shipped per-producer patches, and the adapterless-router sink is a live silent-delivery defect today, not hypothetical.
verification.corrected_diagnosis: As stated, with three corrections/sharpenings: (1) the app-ws registry lives at channels/adapters/app-ws/session-registry.ts (not gateway/http/session-registry.ts); (2) the deliver-to-nobody class was patched per-producer THREE times, not twice — reminders (open/composer.ts:1860-1890), proactive brief (:1904-1916), and the proactive router-sink override in build-core-modules.ts:873-876 — while trident's sink was never overridden and is dead-by-construction in the Open composition (zero production registerAdapter calls; open/composer.ts never supplies channel_router); (3) the trident delivery loss is partially masked in practice because the board-reconcile observer (build-core-modules.ts:350-360) still updates the Work Board lane on terminal runs — only the chat completion message vanishes. Also scope note: the router seam is plausibly live in the external Managed repo (build-telegram-webhook.ts:58 takes the composer's pre-built router), so "zero registered production adapters" is a statement about this repo's compositions.
verification.corrected_proposal: As stated, with two additions: (1) the router-seam decision (register-real-adapters vs delete-router-as-sink) must be checked against the external Managed repo, not just neutron-open — build-telegram-webhook.ts:58 implies Managed threads composition.channel_router, and the Managed-ABI grep required for the slug-picker deletion should cover the router and the chat-bridge re-exports (webTopicId, renderButtonPromptForWeb, WebChatSenderRegistry) in the same pass; (2) under the NO-FUNCTIONALITY-CHANGE mandate, registering adapters would CHANGE behavior (trident completion messages would start arriving in chat where today they are silently dropped) — so split it: file the adapterless trident sink as a standalone P1 functional bug (the minimal fix mirrors the existing proactive pattern: pass a durable buttonStore+appWsAgentPushRegistry sink into buildTridentDelivery, exactly like build-core-modules.ts:876 / composer.ts:1913), and keep the refactor PRs behavior-preserving. The shared chat-policy module should also absorb the FIFTH scribe fan-out copy in open/composer.ts (~:3168-3186), not just chat-bridge's four.
verification.behavior_risk: Riskiest: merging the two sender registries. WebChatSenderRegistry.send's throw-propagation is load-bearing for onboarding crash recovery (chat-bridge.ts:202-218 — a merged registry that evicts-and-continues would report delivery success on a dead socket, InterviewEngine.start would markDelivered, delivered_at would be stamped, and reconnect re-emit would stop recovering the user); app-ws eviction is load-bearing for multi-device fan-out (session-registry.ts:127-148 — a propagate-throw merge would let one dead laptop socket abort the phone's delivery). Neither invariant appears pinned by a dedicated conformance test today (the semantics live in comments citing Codex/Argus reviews; the 8 chat-bridge test files + app-ws tests exercise happy paths), so the critic's mandatory per-consumer failure policy + two-stack delivery-conformance test is the correct precondition, not gold-plating. Second risk: deleting the slug-picker block or the router wiring without checking the external Managed repo breaks an out-of-repo consumer this repo's tests cannot see. Third: "fixing" the adapterless router by registering adapters is itself a behavior change (chat messages start arriving) and must be shipped as a flagged bug fix, not silently inside the refactor.

findings.title: Supervision is decorative and loop lifecycle is unmanaged: both watchdog systems dead in production, tested loops never composed, tick rejections escape with no process handler, cancellation never reaches the observer chain
findings.diagnosis: The watchdog/ package can never detect or notify (no-op notifier, always-fresh heartbeats, ProcessRegistry with zero production writers, 3 of 6 detectors never registered); the agent-aware subagent watchdog that service.ts CLAIMS supervises dispatch is never scheduled — a wedged Atlas/Sentinel run is never reaped. cron_state errors have zero live readers. Built-and-tested loops are silently dormant: the 6-hourly project-backup scheduler's only importer is its own test; the comments AgentWatcher has zero production constructors — and no boot-time inventory of running loops exists, so the set of running loops is not derivable from anywhere. The five hand-rolled tick loops let store-level throws escape as unhandled rejections (no process-level handler exists) and stop() never awaits in-flight ticks; trident's built drain() is destructured away. Watcher re-arming is event-coupled (owner reconnect), never recomputed from durable state at boot. Cancellation writes phase:'stopped' directly through the store, bypassing the terminal-observer chain (delivery → board reconcile → skill-forge audit), while the detached workflow keeps building to completion unobserved.
findings.proposal: One decision per system instead of drift: delete the decorative watchdog/ package (after the ABI check; keep migration tables) and genuinely schedule runLifecycleTick notify-only first; LoopRegistry (name, cadence, last_tick, last_error) + one boot log line + a production-composer test pinning the Open loop inventory (the ISSUE #32 pattern, one level up), with an explicit wire-or-relocate decision per dormant loop — wiring backups is a deliberate feature PR, never silent. One SupervisedLoop primitive (single-flight, per-tick catch-all + consecutive-failure escalation, awaited stop()) adopted by trident/reminders/backup/sweeper with existing loop suites as the lock; wire trident drain() into shutdown; install one process-level unhandledRejection logger plus a fireAndForget(name, promise) wrapper for the 28 bare void sites. Add idempotent rearmFromDurableState() at composition time (import watcher, wow re-fire) and route all terminal writes through store-level terminate(run, phase, reason) that runs the observer chain — actually killing detached work is a separate flagged change.
findings.evidence: open/composer.ts:3434-3440; tools/process-registry.ts:6; runtime/subagent/watchdog.ts:148 + lifecycle.ts:52 (zero callers); agent-dispatch/service.ts:28-30 (false header); project-backup-scheduler.ts:85 (sole importer = own test); agent-watcher.ts:265; trident/tick.ts:129-154; cron/scheduler.ts:339-360; orchestrator.ts:188 vs build-core-modules.ts:415 (drain dropped); work-board-surface.ts:286-305; open/composer.ts:2479-2528,3236-3272

verification.notes: Verified at HEAD d30280c. CONFIRMED CLAIMS: (1) Watchdog decorative in Open — open/composer.ts:3436-3439 wires `watchdog_notifier: { notify: async () => undefined }` and `heartbeat_tracker: { lastHeartbeatAt: () => Date.now() }` (always fresh); only 3 of 6 detectors registered (Heartbeat/StuckAgent/CrashedAgent at gateway/composition/build-core-modules.ts:470-487; the comment at 488-495 admits OverrunCron/DbLock/SubstrateCooldown are deferred to "sprints S5/S6"); ProcessRegistry is instantiated (build-core-modules.ts:229) but has ZERO production register()/touch() call sites — the two agent detectors read an always-empty registry, so the supervisor.start() at :496 runs but can never observe or notify anything. (2) Subagent watchdog never scheduled — runLifecycleTick (runtime/subagent/lifecycle.ts:52) and runAgentWatchdog (watchdog.ts:148) have zero non-test callers; agent-dispatch/service.ts:28-30 header falsely claims "The SAME registry is supervised by the already-ported agent-aware watchdog"; buildDispatchWatchdogNotifier (agent-dispatch/watchdog-report.ts:23) has no production consumer. (3) Dormant loops — ProjectBackupScheduler: zero production `new`, only importer is its own test (gateway/__tests__/project-backup-scheduler.test.ts; other hits are comments in project-backup-store.ts:1392 and chunked-upload-sweeper.ts:20); comments AgentWatcher (gateway/comments/agent-watcher.ts:265): constructed only in tests, and even buildAgentWatcherLlmCall has no non-test consumer. (4) No process-level unhandledRejection/uncaughtException handler anywhere in the repo (only SIGTERM/SIGINT in the two MCP helper processes, tools-bridge.ts:190, dev-channel.ts:338). (5) drain() built and dropped — trident/orchestrator.ts:188,490-496 returns { step, drain }; build-core-modules.ts:416 destructures `const { step } = ...`; shutdown only calls loop.stop() (tick.ts:137-141 clears the interval without awaiting an in-flight runOnce). Zero production .drain() callers. (6) Event-coupled rearm — the import watcher is an in-memory setTimeout chain (open/composer.ts:2479-2528) re-armed ONLY in on_session_open (composer.ts:3236-3272); the code comment itself documents that absent a reconnect "Onboarding wedges PERMANENTLY" after a restart. No boot-time rearm exists. (7) Cancellation bypass — gateway/http/work-board-surface.ts:294-303 writes `trident_runs.update(runId, { phase: 'stopped' })` directly; the tick loop's on_terminal (delivery → skill-forge, wired at composer.ts:3534) fires only on step-driven terminal transitions (tick.ts:169-179), so stopped runs skip the chain and the detached inner Workflow keeps running (this matches PR #174's documented design). CORRECTIONS/OVERSTATEMENTS: (a) "a wedged Atlas/Sentinel run is never reaped" is wrong in the common case — DispatchService has a 30-min default per-turn wall-clock timeout (agent-dispatch/service.ts:243,391) and buildCancellableDispatchTurn cancels the subprocess on timeout (substrate-turn.ts:74-79), driving the run terminal; trident separately has its own 25-min no-advance hang watchdog (orchestrator.ts NO_ADVANCE_HANG_MS). The missing watchdog is lost defense-in-depth (never-settling turn promise, pid-dead detection), not the sole reaper. (b) "store-level throws escape" is scoped too broadly for trident: tick.ts:181-185 catches per-run step+save throws and :173-178 catches delivery; only store.listNonTerminal escapes the outer try/finally into `void this.runOnce()` (tick.ts:129-131). For cron it IS real: handler errors are caught but `await this.state.record(...)` (scheduler.ts:351) sits OUTSIDE the try, so a DB failure escapes via `void this.fireOnce()` (scheduler.ts:155,265). Also note Bun's default merely logs unhandled rejections without exiting, so today's failure mode is silent stderr noise, not a crash — real but milder than implied. (c) "28 bare void sites" is an undercount — I count ~64 `void fn(...)` statement sites in production dirs. (d) cron_state readers: get/list exist (cron/state.ts:25,64); the only error-consuming reader would be the never-registered OverrunCronDetector; I found no HTTP/admin consumer, though I did not exhaustively audit every tabs/ surface. SEVERITY: P1 is defensible for an architecture audit (supervision claims in headers are false, loop inventory underivable), but no live user-visible loss is proven — operationally this is latent risk + dead code, P2-ish. BEHAVIOR RISKS IN PROPOSAL: routing ALL terminal writes through a terminate() that runs the observer chain would NEWLY fire delivery + skill-forge audit on user cancels — a functionality change violating the no-functionality-change mandate unless stopped-phase writes keep skipping the chain (or the chain no-ops for 'stopped'); genuinely scheduling the agent watchdog even notify-only is safe, but any reap-enabled mode risks killing legitimate long dispatches (thresholds); folding CronScheduler into a generic SupervisedLoop risks its load-bearing calendar semantics (Persistent=true catch-up, >24.8-day timer chunking, overlap-skip at scheduler.ts:230-268,320-326) — adopt the primitive only for the simple interval loops; wiring drain() into shutdown extends SIGTERM latency by up to an in-flight fire turn and must be time-bounded; installing an unhandledRejection handler in Bun suppresses the default report, so it must re-log. Existing suites (trident/tick tests, reminders tests, cron scheduler tests, watchdog-six-modes integration test) lock loop-internal behavior but nothing pins the COMPOSED loop inventory — the proposed production-composer inventory test is the right lock and doesn't exist today.
verification.corrected_diagnosis: The watchdog/ package is decorative in the Open composition: no-op notifier + always-fresh heartbeat (open/composer.ts:3436-3439), a ProcessRegistry with zero production writers feeding the two agent detectors (build-core-modules.ts:229 vs no register()/touch() call sites), and 3 of 6 detectors never registered (build-core-modules.ts:488-495 defers them to unshipped "S5/S6"). The agent-aware subagent watchdog that agent-dispatch/service.ts:28-30 claims supervises dispatches is never scheduled (runLifecycleTick/runAgentWatchdog: zero production callers; buildDispatchWatchdogNotifier unconsumed) — however, a wedged dispatch IS still bounded by the 30-min per-turn wall-clock timeout (service.ts:243,391; substrate-turn.ts cancels the subprocess), and trident has its own 25-min no-advance reaper, so the loss is defense-in-depth plus false documentation, not an unreaped-run production incident. Built-and-tested loops are silently dormant with no boot-time inventory: ProjectBackupScheduler's only importer is its own test; the comments AgentWatcher (and its buildAgentWatcherLlmCall) has zero production constructors. Rejection hygiene is real but narrower than stated: trident's per-run step/save/delivery ARE caught (tick.ts:173-185) — only listNonTerminal escapes; cron's handler errors are caught but the post-run `state.record` (scheduler.ts:351) sits outside the try and escapes via `void fireOnce` (scheduler.ts:155,265); no process-level unhandledRejection handler exists anywhere, though Bun's default logs-without-exit makes this noise, not crashes (~64 bare `void fn()` sites, not 28). stop() never awaits an in-flight tick and trident's built drain() (orchestrator.ts:490-496) is destructured away at build-core-modules.ts:416. The import watcher is re-armed only on owner reconnect (composer.ts:3236-3272 — the comment itself documents the restart wedge), never from durable state at boot. Work-board delete cancels a run by writing phase:'stopped' directly through the store (work-board-surface.ts:294-303), deliberately (PR #174) bypassing the tick loop's terminal-observer chain (delivery → skill-forge audit) while the detached inner Workflow builds to completion unobserved.
verification.corrected_proposal: Keep the shape of the plan but re-scope three pieces to stay behavior-preserving. (1) Watchdog: the delete-vs-finish-wiring choice is a product decision — the code comments show deliberate incremental S5/S6 intent, so surface it to Ryan as delete OR wire, not default-delete; either way keep the alert-store migrations, and if the subagent watchdog is scheduled it must launch notify-only with thresholds above the existing 30-min dispatch turn timeout so it never reaps a dispatch the timeout would have handled. (2) LoopRegistry + one boot log line + a production-composer test pinning the Open loop inventory: adopt as-is (nothing pins composition today); every dormant loop (backup scheduler, AgentWatcher) gets an explicit wire-or-delete decision, and wiring backups is a feature PR. (3) SupervisedLoop primitive: adopt only for the simple interval loops (trident tick, reminders, sweeper — their existing suites are the lock); do NOT fold CronScheduler in — its calendar catch-up/chunking/overlap-skip semantics are load-bearing; instead fix the one concrete cron gap by moving `state.record` inside the try (or catching it) so a DB hiccup can't escape. Wire trident drain() into module shutdown with a hard time bound (SIGTERM latency). Install one unhandledRejection logger (it must re-log, since a Bun handler suppresses the default report) and sweep the ~64 bare void sites through fireAndForget(name, promise) mechanically. (4) rearmFromDurableState() at composition time for the import watcher is a bug FIX (it changes restart behavior — today onboarding wedges until reconnect); land it as a separately-reviewed change, not inside the refactor. (5) Do NOT route existing 'stopped' writes through an observer-running terminate(): that would newly fire delivery/skill-forge on user cancels, a functionality change. Introduce terminate(run, phase, reason) as the single chokepoint but with the observer chain explicitly skipped for 'stopped' to preserve current semantics; enabling observers-on-cancel and actually killing detached work are separate flagged product changes.
verification.behavior_risk: Highest-risk elements if applied naively: (a) terminate() running the observer chain on cancel would newly deliver terminal announcements and trigger skill-forge audits for user-stopped runs — no existing test pins the absence of that behavior; (b) scheduling the subagent watchdog in reap mode could kill legitimate long-running dispatches that today are protected only by the 30-min turn timeout; (c) folding CronScheduler into a generic loop primitive could silently break calendar catch-up (Persistent=true), >24.8-day timer chunking, and overlap-skip — cron/scheduler tests cover some but the timing seams are subtle; (d) awaiting drain() in shutdown lengthens SIGTERM handling by an in-flight fire turn unless bounded.

findings.title: Observability program: no logging framework (468 raw console.* in 4+ conventions), 582 silent catch{} losing user-facing failures, an unreachable degradation-notification tier, and no diagnostics surface for a self-hosted product
findings.diagnosis: Every subsystem logs its own way with no levels or verbosity control and 34 divergent per-module log?: seams. The dominant degrade idiom is `catch { return null/[]/false }` with no log — verified losses include the React web client's Bun.build failure returning an unlogged 404 with the compile errors discarded, prewarm failures leaving no trace, and DB errors rendering an empty sidebar. The substrate's carefully built degradation tier (size alerts, rate-limit banners, dead-turn notices, recovered replies) has all five DI sinks unwired in Open and kind:'status' events dropped by every consumer — a usage-capped session looks like 'Neutron stopped answering'. The owner cannot ask the product why something degraded: healthz is status+uptime, the systemd watchdog is an unconditional tick, doctor covers only GBrain, and the gbrain latch/credential cooldown/wedged REPLs/failing cron/orphaned imports are all invisible although the data already exists in stores. The 500 catch-all is context-free text/plain breaking the JSON contract; fail-open vs fail-closed polarity is per-site folklore that a normalization sweep could silently flip.
findings.proposal: One observability/ leaf: createLogger(subsystem) with levels, NEUTRON_LOG_LEVEL, and once()/rateLimited() replacing the three hand-rolled latches; back the 34 existing seams with logger defaults (zero call-site churn), then sweep console sites and require `// silent-ok:` annotations for bare catch{} via lint — never changing the returned degrade VALUES. Author the one-page boundary-policy ADR (auth=fail-closed, delivery=fail-open, memory=fail-soft+journal) with machine-greppable polarity annotations FIRST — it is the prerequisite for every other error change. Add read-only GET /api/app/admin/diagnostics composing existing state (gbrain latch + deferred-edge count, credential pool probes, repl-registry.json sessions, cron_state, import jobs) + extend neutron doctor; boot-time mounted-surface manifest line + request context/id on the catch-all, 500 as {ok:false,code:'internal'} after client-grep. Wiring the notice sinks and recovered-reply drain is a FLAGGED functional PR after the no-change phase.
findings.evidence: counts: 468 console.*, 582 bare catch{}, 34 log?: seams; landing/server.ts:1243-1262 (discarded Bun.build logs); open/composer.ts:3661-3684,1544-1553; runtime/adapters/claude-code/index.ts:133-135,226-231 (unwired hooks); build-llm-call-substrate.ts:823-826 (status dropped); gateway/index.ts:309-331,477-496; bin/neutron:87-95; GBrainSyncHook.ts:138-148; app-admin-surface.ts:391-408; wow-push-emitter.ts:105-171 vs calendar-wiring.ts:256 (polarity divergence)

verification.notes: Verified at HEAD d30280c. COUNTS (excluding tests, node_modules, .claude/worktrees): 457 console.log/error/warn/info/debug sites (critic: 468 — within noise); 587 bindingless `catch {` blocks (critic: 582 — matches); 42 `log?:` occurrences across ~33 files (critic: 34 seams — close). CITED SITES ALL CHECK OUT: landing/server.ts:1246-1263 Bun.build failure → return null with result.logs (compile errors) discarded, catch{} → null, served as bare 404 'chat-react.js unavailable' at server.ts:1377-1379; open/composer.ts:3673-3682 prewarm `catch { /* best-effort */ }` with zero trace; open/composer.ts:1551-1553 sidebar project query `catch { return [] }`. DEGRADATION TIER UNREACHABLE — confirmed STRONGER than stated: (a) BuildLlmCallSubstrateInput carries only onRecoveredReply (build-llm-call-substrate.ts:296); onDeadTurnNotice/onSizeAlert/onRateLimitBanner/onModelUpdate cannot even be threaded through the composition helper; (b) no caller anywhere passes onRecoveredReply (only the forward at :484); makeRecoveredReplySink (recovered-reply-store.ts:185) is never called; the chat-bridge drain (chat-bridge.ts:1450,1512) is gated on opts.recoveredReplyStore which open/composer.ts:1237's buildLandingStack call never supplies — a fully built + unit-tested (gateway/http/__tests__/replay-redelivery.test.ts) redelivery pipeline that is dead at Open's composition root; (c) grep confirms ZERO consumers in the repo branch on kind === 'status', while dispatchRateLimitBannerNotice (persistent-repl-substrate.ts:585-617) delivers the '🚧 Claude usage limit reached' message ONLY as a status event (dropped), the unwired hook, and stderr — so 'usage-capped session looks like Neutron stopped answering' is accurate for the end user (operator does get journald stderr); collectTokensToString (build-llm-call-substrate.ts:812-827), the main conversational drain via build-live-agent-turn.ts:889, explicitly ignores status. healthz = status+uptime only (gateway/index.ts:477-495; Open leaves the default per open/composer.ts:30-31); doctor = GBrain-only (bin/neutron:87-108); 500 catch-all = text/plain 'Internal Server Error' though it DOES console.error the trace (gateway/index.ts:324-330) — the gap is response shape + request id, not server-side silence. Polarity divergence confirmed: wow-push-emitter.ts:111-119,167-174 fail-closed (documented Codex P2 on pushAll leak) vs calendar-wiring.ts:256 instance-wide pushAll for briefs. Diagnostics data pre-exists as claimed: GBrainSyncHook.ts:148 latch + :159 deferredEdgeCount getter; credential-pool.ts:59,114-137 cooldown/probe; repl-registry.json path at runtime/adapters/claude-code/index.ts:185-195; cron/state.ts:69 listAll. MINOR OVERSTATEMENTS: 'bare catch{}' is 587 bindingless-catch blocks, not literally-empty catches (exactly 1 of those); many carry deliberate documented fail-soft comments — the defect is un-inventoried polarity, not 582 raw bugs. The chat-react 404 is a packaging-error/mid-life-rebuild path (the boot shell requires the entry), slightly narrower than implied. Severity P1 is honest: the unreachable notice tier is a genuine shipped-but-dead user-facing feature (PR #67 built the seams expressly so the gateway could wire delivery; Open never did), and this is a pre-release self-hosted product whose stated mission is robustness.
verification.corrected_proposal: Keep the proposal, with four safety additions the critic missed. (1) The 'zero call-site churn' claim holds only for the 34 log?: seams — at least 5 test files spyOn(console.warn/info/error) directly (e.g. gateway/__tests__/resolve-registry-db-path.test.ts:48,72,97; gbrain-memory/__tests__/embedder-config.test.ts:19; gateway/http/__tests__/chat-bridge-seed-reemit-race.test.ts:132), so the console sweep must either route through console.* under the hood or update those tests in the same PR. (2) Exempt CLI-stdout-as-UI from the sweep: gbrain-doctor.ts and bin/* print user-facing output via console/printf — leveling that behind NEUTRON_LOG_LEVEL would break `neutron doctor`. (3) Do NOT fold the behavior-latches into once()/rateLimited(): GBrainSyncHook.gbrainUnavailable (GBrainSyncHook.ts:148) gates early-return of every subsequent onEntityWrite, and the rate-limit-banner detector latch gates user-notification cadence — these are state machines, not log dedup; only log-side dedup may migrate. (4) The logger must be throw-safe and non-blocking: it will be called inside the PTY scan tick (persistent-repl-substrate.ts:614-616 documents 'a bad notice hook must never crash the scan tick') and adjacent to the 100ms heartbeat tick (heartbeat-watchdog.ts) — no sync I/O amplification in hot paths, and rateLimited() must ship in phase 1, not as follow-up, or newly-logged former-silent catches in scan loops become journald storms. Also note the recovered-reply wiring is smaller than 'wire five sinks': the store, sink builder, drain, and tests all exist (recovered-reply-store.ts, chat-bridge.ts:1450/1512, replay-redelivery.test.ts) — the flagged functional PR for that one is roughly 'construct the store in open/composer.ts and pass it to buildLandingStack + buildLlmCallSubstrate'; the other four sinks additionally need input-type plumbing through BuildLlmCallSubstrateInput (build-llm-call-substrate.ts:296).
verification.behavior_risk: Riskiest silent behavior changes: (a) replacing the gbrainUnavailable or banner-detector latches with generic logger dedup would change actual control flow (skip-writes / notification cadence), not just logs; (b) a logger that throws or does sync I/O inside the PTY output-scan tick or heartbeat path could crash scan ticks or trip the event-loop-block detector (heartbeat-watchdog.ts:113) — both paths are documented as must-never-crash; (c) changing the 500 body/content-type (gateway/index.ts:329) and the 404 'chat-react.js unavailable' text before grepping app/Expo + chat-react clients for status/text matching; (d) tests that spy on console.warn/info will fail or, worse, be weakened to pass. Existing tests would catch (d) loudly, partially catch (a) (GBrainSyncHook has tests; banner-latch cadence coverage is thinner), and would NOT catch (b) — no test exercises logging under a live PTY scan tick.

findings.title: Workspace manifests are fiction: 87% of cross-module imports bypass package boundaries, four cross-consumed source dirs have no package.json, and package naming drift is coupled to installed data
findings.diagnosis: 795 of 910 cross-module imports are relative paths that bypass every manifest: gateway declares 12 deps but imports 39 modules; runtime declares one dep while importing core-sdk/jwt-validator/onboarding; reminders (a data package) imports gateway/landing/chat-bridge while declaring cron+persistence; migrations imports the product surface for its CLI block. open/ (the production composition root, 96 outgoing cross-imports), work-board/, tabs/, and project-credentials/ are not workspaces at all. No tool can compute a real build order and extraction is unplannable. Naming drift compounds it: @neutron/chat-core is the sole odd scope (metro-config-coupled), core-sdk vs cores/sdk sit one letter apart with rival validators, email ships as 'email-managed-core' (Managed vocabulary as a product name), agent-settings alone lacks -core — and packageNameToSlug keys core_installations rows, sidecar filenames, and table prefixes, so core renames are pre-first-release-or-never.
findings.proposal: (1) Add package.json + workspace entries for open/, work-board/, tabs/, project-credentials/. (2) Mechanically declare every actual dep from the measured per-file edge list. (3) Adopt import/no-relative-packages at root, rolled out module-by-module with the full runner — app-adjacent packages last with an Expo bundle build (chat-core raw-TS/metro constraint). (4) Move resolveOpenDbPath to a paths leaf and lift reminders/outbound delivery into gateway composition (preserving persist-before-send and the app:-registry target). (5) Naming series while pre-release: @neutron/chat-core → @neutronai/chat-core; email-managed-core → email-core and agent-settings → agent-settings-core WITH slug-alias migrations for installed data tested against a seeded install (or an explicit documented decision to freeze names forever); collapse the core-sdk/cores-sdk pair per the cores-platform finding.
findings.evidence: declared-vs-actual delta table (layering report §3-4); gateway/package.json vs 39 measured targets; reminders/dispatcher.ts:28 + outbound.ts:24-25 vs its package.json; root package.json:5-46 workspaces; chat-core/package.json:2 + app/metro.config.js; cores/runtime/loader.ts:61-81 (slug keying); cores/free/email/package.json

verification.notes: Verified every cited artifact at HEAD. (1) Root package.json workspaces (lines 4-45) omit open/, work-board/, tabs/, project-credentials/ and none has a package.json (ls confirms); all four are cross-consumed — tabs by gateway, work-board by agent-dispatch/gateway/open/trident, project-credentials by gateway/open/trident (measured import graph). (2) gateway/package.json declares 12 workspace deps + jose; measured 35 distinct relative-import target modules (union with pkg-name targets ~39). (3) runtime/package.json declares only @modelcontextprotocol/sdk while relatively importing core-sdk, jwt-validator, mcp, onboarding, scribe, tools. (4) reminders/dispatcher.ts:28 value-imports ../gateway/realmode-composer/build-llm-call-substrate.ts; reminders/outbound.ts:22-25 imports channels (value) + landing/server.ts and gateway/http/chat-bridge.ts (type-only, erased at runtime — a nuance the critic missed but the gateway value edge is real); package.json declares only cron+persistence. (5) migrations/runner.ts:6 statically imports ../open/owner-identity.ts — unconditional top-level import, used at :180 inside the import.meta.main block at :174, so it is actually WORSE than 'for its CLI block': every consumer of runner.ts loads open/. (6) chat-core/package.json:2 = @neutron/chat-core, coupled at app/package.json:16, app/metro.config.js:3-8, app/tsconfig.json:5. (7) cores/runtime/loader.ts:61-81 packageNameToSlug with docstring :53-59 confirming table-prefix/sidecar/core_installations-PK keying; cores/free/email/package.json name @neutronai/email-managed-core with hardcoded capability strings read:email_managed_core.messages; cores/free/agent-settings = @neutronai/agent-settings (no -core). (8) Rival validators confirmed: core-sdk/validator.ts (hand-written stub) vs cores/sdk/manifest.ts (zod), with cores/sdk/manifest.ts:17-25 documenting a manual 'MUST stay shape-compatible' burden; loader uses @neutronai/cores-sdk (loader.ts:24-27). Numbers: my scan gives 729 relative vs 88 package-name cross-module imports in production code (89.2% bypass; 2044/2262 = 90.4% with tests, excluding .claude), same magnitude as the claimed 87% though the critic's absolute 795/910 is not reproducible as stated; open/ has 219 outgoing relative cross-import statements (critic said 96 — likely distinct-file counting). Severity P1 is honest for a refactor-mission audit: manifests actively mislead and four cross-consumed dirs have no boundary at all. Tests relevant to the riskiest moves exist: reminders/dispatcher.test.ts + dispatcher.integration.test.ts cover fire-time composition; persist-before-send lives inside buildButtonStoreReminderOutbound (outbound.ts:17, 63-88), wired at open/composer.ts:1881, so it survives a wiring move as long as the builder is reused.
verification.corrected_diagnosis: Substantively as stated, with corrections: (a) ~89-90% (not exactly 87%) of cross-module imports in production TS are relative and bypass manifests — 729 relative vs 88 package-name statements prod-only; open/ has 219 outgoing relative cross-imports, not 96; gateway's measured distinct dependency modules is 35 relative-target modules (~39 with package-name-only targets) vs 12 declared. (b) The reminders→landing and reminders→chat-bridge edges (outbound.ts:24-25) are TYPE-ONLY imports (no runtime coupling, breakable with local structural types); the genuinely load-bearing runtime edge is dispatcher.ts:28 → gateway/realmode-composer. (c) migrations→open is worse than 'for its CLI block': runner.ts:6 is an unconditional top-level import, so every programmatic consumer of applyMigrations loads open/owner-identity.ts. (d) 'No tool can compute a real build order / extraction is unplannable' is overstated: there is no build step at all (every manifest is main:./index.ts raw TS run by Bun), and dependency-cruiser is ALREADY a root devDependency (package.json:57) sitting unconfigured — the true import graph is computable from source today; the actual harm is that manifests mislead humans and workspace boundaries constrain nothing. (e) Rename blast radius is larger than 'slug keys': the email manifest hardcodes capability strings containing the slug (read:email_managed_core.messages), so a rename also touches granted-capability data, and a live dogfood install already exists — 'pre-first-release-or-never' is effectively already 'never without a tested data migration'.
verification.corrected_proposal: Keep steps 1-2 (add the four missing package.json + workspace entries; mechanically declare measured deps — near-zero runtime risk since Bun workspaces are symlinks and nothing is compiled). Before step 3, configure the already-installed dependency-cruiser with layering/no-new-edge rules as the enforcement mechanism: it gives boundary enforcement over the REAL graph with zero import rewriting and zero behavior risk, making the eslint import/no-relative-packages rewrite an incremental cleanup rather than the load-bearing step. When rewriting imports, note packages WITH exports maps (cores/sdk, cores/free/email) reject deep subpaths that relative imports currently reach — extend exports or route through index; keep the critic's app-last Expo-bundle gate (Metro symlink handling). Re-aim step 4: the reminders→gateway runtime edge is collectTokensToString (dispatcher.ts:28) — extract that helper to a leaf (runtime/ or shared util) and replace outbound.ts's type-only landing/gateway imports with local structural interfaces; do NOT move outbound construction out of open/composer.ts:1881 — persist-before-send lives inside buildButtonStoreReminderOutbound and survives untouched if the builder is reused; also break migrations→open by moving resolveOpenDbPath to a paths leaf (as proposed). Downgrade step 5: take the explicit freeze-names decision for core packages (email-managed-core, agent-settings) rather than slug-alias migrations — the slug also appears in capability strings and installed-data rows and a live dogfood install exists, so the rename buys aesthetics at data-migration cost; document @neutron/chat-core as a deliberate exception (or rename it only, since chat-core has no installed-data slug coupling — its cost is app/package.json:16 + metro/tsconfig + import sites + an Expo bundle verification). Collapse core-sdk/cores-sdk per the cores-platform finding — the code itself documents the manual shape-compatibility burden (cores/sdk/manifest.ts:17-25).
verification.behavior_risk: Riskiest: (1) core package renames — packageNameToSlug (cores/runtime/loader.ts:61-81) keys core_installations PK, per-core table prefixes, and sidecar filenames, AND the slug is embedded in manifest capability strings (read:email_managed_core.messages), so a rename against the existing live dogfood install silently orphans installed core data and capability grants; no test seeds an installed-data upgrade path, so the suite would NOT catch it. (2) Import-rewrite step: deep-subpath imports into packages with exports maps would fail at module resolution (loud, caught by boot/tests), and Metro symlink resolution for app/chat-core can break the Expo bundle (caught only by the proposed bundle build, not by bun test). (3) Moving reminders outbound wiring (original step 4 as written) risks the persist-before-send ordering and app:-registry live-push target (timer-fired messages on the wrong registry never deliver — known PR#105 failure mode); reminders/dispatcher tests cover composition but not the composer wiring, so the corrected proposal deliberately leaves wiring at open/composer.ts:1881. Steps 1-2 (manifests only) have effectively zero behavior risk.

findings.title: The Cores plugin platform's contract is fiction in both directions: bundled cores import monorepo internals, the gateway consumes cores by duck-typing with silent throw-stub degradation, and the published SDK validates a different schema than production
findings.diagnosis: Every dimension of 'a Core compiles against the published SDK' is violated: four cores relatively import the monorepo migrations runner; research value-imports runtime/models.ts; agent-settings value-imports onboarding config AND dormant connect. Gateway-side, the real entry contract is dynamic import + typeof checks of undeclared barrel exports, backend injection via a hardcoded BACKEND_KEY_BY_SLUG table (with a ghost dtc_analytics entry), and manifest-declared tools with no handler silently becoming throw-stubs while install stays 'ok' — exactly how the notes core shipped four broken tools (ISSUE #330). The published core-sdk's 650-line hand validator and closed NeutronCapability union have ZERO production callers; production validates via cores/sdk's open Zod schema, and every custom capability string dispatches only because install casts through the closed union. The nine cores also maintain a copy-paste template (loadManifest x9, the sidecar resolver x4 where only research kept the path-traversal guard on tool-supplied project_id — duplication forked a security control; install-lifecycle test x6).
findings.proposal: Three injection seams with in-repo precedent: cores-runtime exports a project-scoped sidecar-migrations applier; model accessors injected the way email already gets emailModel (keep constants frozen in the no-change phase — flipping to live thunks is a separate flagged PR); engagement policy to the contracts leaf. Typed defineCore/BundledCoreModule entry point in cores/sdk with the backend key in the manifest; manifest.tools ⊄ built-handlers becomes a surfaced 'degraded' installation state visible via /api/cores (not hard-fail — required-secret cores legitimately install before credentials), plus a conformance test over all nine barrels. Collapse to the one Zod manifest source, generate or delete the JSON schema, delete the hand validator + its 622-line parity test (version the npm package). Add generic ProjectSidecarResolver<H> (traversal guard default-on — flagged security fix for email/code-gen/calendar), loadManifestFromPackageDir, one error module, one shared lifecycle test harness. Then arm the cores-use-sdk-only depcruise rule as the living contract test.
findings.evidence: cores/free/calendar/migrations/runner.ts:19 (+3 siblings → migrations/runner.ts); research/src/backend.ts:45-47 → runtime/models.ts; agent-settings/src/backend.ts:42-47; install-bundled.ts:751-800,886-905,957-964,1020-1035; core-sdk/validator.ts:164 + types.ts:139-161 vs cores/sdk/manifest.ts:17-31; research/store-resolver.ts:186-200 (lone traversal guard) vs email/cache.ts:285-325

verification.notes: Checked every cited claim at HEAD. CONFIRMED: (1) Four cores relatively import the monorepo migrations runner — cores/free/research/src/store-resolver.ts:22, cores/free/calendar/migrations/runner.ts:19, cores/free/email/src/cache.ts:23, cores/free/code-gen/src/sidecar/store.ts:18 all import ../../../../migrations/runner.ts. (2) research value-imports runtime/models.ts (substrate-runtime.ts:27, backend.ts:47, research-orchestrator.ts:17 — SONNET_MODEL/FAST_MODEL, env-derived const values at runtime/models.ts:88,95). (3) agent-settings value-imports onboarding (backend.ts:42 TELEGRAM_BIND_TOKEN_TTL_MS) and dormant connect (backend.ts:43-47 DEFAULT_AGENT_ENGAGEMENT_MODE + isAgentEngagementMode from connect/agent-engagement.ts — value, not type-only; tools.ts:38 is type-only). (4) Duck-typed entry contract: install-bundled.ts:771-802 dynamic import + typeof checks of buildTools/buildExtraTools; silent degradation to throw-stubs at :786,800,828,836,850,894-905,966-987 while the install loop still does installed.set + logs cores.install_ok (:228-242) — degradation is telemetry-only, install record unaffected. (5) ISSUE #330 is documented verbatim at docs/AS_BUILT.md:669-680: notes manifest declared 8 tools, barrel never exported buildExtraTools, 4 tools fell to not_implemented stubs on every install. (6) SDK duality: core-sdk/validator.ts:164 validateNeutronManifest has ZERO production callers (only core-sdk/index.ts:47 re-export + its own 622-line validator.test.ts); production validates via cores/runtime/loader.ts:25-27 parseManifest (Zod, @neutronai/cores-sdk) called from lifecycle.ts:137 loadCoreFromDir; the closed NeutronCapability union (core-sdk/types.ts:139-161) is only satisfied by casts at install-bundled.ts:935,974 — the comment at :926-934 admits the runtime gates on string equality, not the union. (7) Security-control fork is real AND reachable: research alone has safeResolveProjectRoot (store-resolver.ts:128-153, null-byte + strict-subpath checks); email (cache.ts:289-291 bare join), code-gen (store.ts:364-365), and the gateway calendar resolver (gateway/cores/calendar-wiring.ts:159 join + mkdirSync recursive, no guard) don't; tool-supplied project_id flows to the unguarded resolvers at email/tools.ts:276-278 and calendar-wiring.ts:102. (8) Duplication counts: loadManifest x9 (all free cores except notes), install-lifecycle.test.ts x6 (research/tasks/calendar/reminders/email/code-gen), sidecar-migrations importer x4. CORRECTIONS: (a) dtc_analytics in BACKEND_KEY_BY_SLUG (install-bundled.ts:1033) is NOT a ghost — it is the Tier-2 paid-staging core expected under cores/paid-staging/, conditionally exercised by gateway/__tests__/cores-surface.test.ts:34-47 (HAS_PAID_STAGING) and slug-mapped in cores/runtime/loader.ts:39; deleting the entry would break paid-staging installs. (b) 'Published SDK' overstates: core-sdk/package.json and cores/sdk/package.json are both "private": true — no npm publication is evidenced in-repo, which actually LOWERS the risk of deleting the hand validator. (c) The 'cores-use-sdk-only depcruise rule' does not exist to be 'armed': dependency-cruiser@17.4.3 is a devDependency (package.json:57) with no config file anywhere in the repo; the rule exists only as prose in the audit's own docs (docs/research/refactor-audit-2026-07-02/critic-layering.md:394). Severity P1 is honest: the plugin platform is the declared extension seam, its contract is enforced nowhere, and duplication demonstrably forked a security control.
verification.corrected_diagnosis: As stated, with three factual fixes: (1) the dtc_analytics BACKEND_KEY_BY_SLUG entry is not a ghost — it fronts the out-of-repo Tier-2 paid-staging core that gateway/__tests__/cores-surface.test.ts conditionally installs when cores/paid-staging/ is present; it must be kept (the real smell is that the gateway hardcodes per-core backend keys at all). (2) Neither SDK is actually published: both core-sdk and cores/sdk package.json are "private": true, so the dead hand validator is an in-repo dual-contract problem, not an external-consumer schema drift — strictly easier to delete. (3) agent-settings' connect import is a genuine value import (DEFAULT_AGENT_ENGAGEMENT_MODE/isAgentEngagementMode); its tools.ts import is type-only.
verification.corrected_proposal: Directionally sound, with staging fixes: (1) The depcruise rule must be AUTHORED, not armed — no dependency-cruiser config exists in the repo (the tool is an unwired devDependency). (2) Keep the dtc_analytics key when moving backend keys into manifests, or paid-staging installs break; the conformance test can only cover the nine in-repo barrels. (3) Two items in the proposal are functionality changes and must ship as separately flagged PRs outside the no-change refactor, exactly as the critic half-acknowledges: the 'degraded' installation state surfaced via /api/cores (new observable API state; today the only signal is cores.tool_registration_failed telemetry, install-bundled.ts:899-905), and traversal-guard-on-by-default for email/calendar/code-gen (research's guard rejects anything not a strict subpath of <owner_home>/Projects/ after resolvePath canonicalization, store-resolver.ts:143-153 — symlinked project roots or dotted project_ids that work today would start throwing; no existing email/calendar test covers this, so new tests are required). (4) The pure-refactor core that would have caught ISSUE #330 with zero behavior risk is just the conformance test asserting manifest.tools ⊆ built-handlers over all nine barrels — ship that first, before touching registerCoreTools. (5) normalizeBackend replacement must byte-preserve the sniff heuristic's verbatim-passthrough branch (install-bundled.ts:1005-1016: objects with backend|store|client|orchestrator|summarizer keys pass untouched; null/undefined short-circuits to stubs) — notes' factory returns {backend, resolver} and relies on it. (6) Model injection is safe only as frozen values: research imports env-derived constants (SONNET_MODEL/FAST_MODEL read once at module load) while the existing emailModel precedent injects getBestModel (a live function, mount-open-cores.ts:288) — wiring research to the live thunk would change model selection, so the critic's 'constants frozen in the no-change phase' caveat is load-bearing and correct.
verification.behavior_risk: Riskiest: (a) adopting the traversal guard in email/calendar/code-gen changes accepted inputs — any currently-working project_id that resolves outside a canonical <owner_home>/Projects/ subpath (symlinked NEUTRON_HOME, unusual slugs) would begin throwing, and no existing test in those cores exercises path resolution adversarially, so a silent regression window exists; (b) replacing normalizeBackend/BACKEND_KEY_BY_SLUG with manifest-declared keys can misroute a backend for any factory whose return shape relies on the sniff heuristic's passthrough branch — the six install-lifecycle.test.ts copies plus gateway/__tests__/cores-surface.test.ts and cores-composition.test.ts give decent coverage for the nine bundled cores but nothing covers the out-of-repo dtc_analytics path; (c) hardening manifest-tool-without-handler from telemetry-only to a surfaced degraded state alters /api/cores responses and could alter install-ordering side effects if implemented as anything but a post-hoc annotation — the current fail-soft ordering (install row committed, then tools registered, failures never unwind the install, install-bundled.ts:214-242) is load-bearing for required-secret cores that legitimately install before credentials exist.

findings.title: Three-client wire-model and dual-frontend duplication: ~1,300 mirrored LOC across 7 mirror families (most parity-untested), twin fetch clients for every surface, and renderers already drifting user-visibly
findings.diagnosis: The chat wire protocol is defined on the server (envelope.ts, 931 LOC), hand-mirrored in the Expo app, independently re-decoded in chat-core, and re-derived in the web client; five near-identical option shapes with lossy hand mappings; TabDescriptor is triple-declared and AgentEngagementMode mirrored with comment-only lockstep ('byte-for-byte', zero tests). Both frontends duplicate every gateway consumer: ~6 twin fetch-client pairs (docs 867 vs 532 LOC; work-board self-describes as 'the web twin'), 16+8 bespoke error classes, and two DIFFERENT markdown algorithms so the same agent output already renders differently on phone vs browser. ~45% of PR #149's insertions were the second copy; 'registry-driven' tabs still cost 7-8 touch points via client hand-mirror allowlists; one per-project setting fans out across 9+ files. The mirrors exist because node-poisoned packages can't be imported — but chat-core proves node-free raw-TS leaves work in both bundlers.
findings.proposal: Immediately: add the missing parity tests (tabs descriptor, engagement mode) following the existing ws-envelope/doc-links pattern — they are the guardrail for everything else. Then extract node-free @neutronai/wire-types (envelope union, ONE canonical option type with explicit render projections, topic-id derivation, doc-links build/parse, TabDescriptor, engagement mode, message caps): server re-exports first, clients cut over file-by-file, mirrors deleted last with parity tests still green. Then a shared client-core (GatewayHttpClient base with one GatewayClientError{code,status} + injectable fetch, thin per-surface modules, view-models) consumed by both fronts, consolidated against recorded fixtures; derive client tab legality/loading defaults from the resolver payload (still rejecting unknown persisted values); a typed per-project settings registry driving PATCH validation and both UIs. Enforce node-free purity with a depcruise/lint rule (a node import bricks the RN bundle). Renderer unification and the react-native-web-vs-client-core question are explicit, separately-scheduled owner decisions.
findings.evidence: channels/adapters/app-ws/envelope.ts:181,223 vs app/lib/ws-envelope.ts:98-142 vs chat-core/types.ts:429-703; app/lib/tabs-client.ts:15-52 + landing/chat-react/tabs-client.ts:42-58 (no test imports tabs/registry); app/lib/markdown-render.tsx (908) vs chat-react/Markdown.tsx (118); grep -a citations/doc_refs in chat-react = 0; git show ca78517 --stat; 9-file agent_engagement_mode fanout

verification.notes: Opened every cited file. CONFIRMED: envelope.ts=931 LOC with option iface at channels/adapters/app-ws/envelope.ts:181 and options field :223; hand-mirror app/lib/ws-envelope.ts:98-107 (header :2-10 mandates lockstep, parity test app/__tests__/ws-envelope-parity.test.ts exists); chat-core/types.ts:429 is normalizeInbound re-decoding the same wire shapes (ChatMessageOption at :22, parseOptions :548). TabDescriptor triple-declared: tabs/registry.ts:67, app/lib/tabs-client.ts:41-50, landing/chat-react/tabs-client.ts:43-52 — both clients say "byte-for-byte" (:15/:17) and NO test imports tabs/registry from either client. AgentEngagementMode mirrored comment-only: connect/agent-engagement.ts:37 vs app/lib/projects-client.ts:28 ("must stay in lockstep"), zero tests; fanout ~11 non-test files (grep agent_engagement_mode). Five option shapes confirmed incl. channels/button-primitive.ts:59 and landing/server.ts:232; live lossy mapping: gateway/http/chat-bridge.ts:435-437 drops `decoration` while landing/chat-react/ChatApp.tsx:350-352 reads decoration?.style. Twin clients: docs 867 vs 532 LOC, work-board 207 vs 311 (landing work-board-client.ts:4 self-describes "The web twin"), tabs 115 vs 180, project-credentials 150 vs 178; error classes exactly 16 app + 8 web (grep "extends Error"). Markdown: app/lib/markdown-render.tsx:4-18 hand-rolled state machine (908 LOC, "no remark" is a DOCUMENTED bundle-size decision) vs web react-markdown+remark-gfm+rehype-sanitize (118 LOC); citations/doc_refs = 0 refs in chat-react non-test code while mobile ships citation-chip-row.tsx — user-visible drift real. git show ca78517: 3,293 insertions/33 files confirmed. Several client files are grep-binary ("file" reports data), matching the critic's grep -a note. CORRECTIONS: (1) "45% of PR #149's insertions were the second copy" is wrong arithmetic — the second (web) copy is ~690/3,293≈21%; BOTH frontend copies together are 1,480/3,293≈45%, and the 640-line settings.tsx vs 453-line SettingsTab.tsx are platform-native UIs, not recoverable mirrors — only the fetch/type layer consolidates. (2) "duplicate every gateway consumer / ~6 twin pairs" overstates: exactly 4 exact-name twin pairs (docs, work-board, tabs, project-credentials); integrations + codex-credential are web-only; mobile has ~15 clients with no web twin. (3) "most parity-untested" true by family count but false by LOC — the two largest mirrors are tested (ws-envelope-parity.test.ts; runtime/__tests__/doc-links-parity.test.ts covers the 493-line app/lib/doc-links.ts mirror), >55% of mirrored LOC; note app/lib/doc-links.ts:9 cites the parity test at a WRONG path (gateway/__tests__/) — drift evidence in itself. (4) Proposal sub-item "derive client tab legality/loading defaults from the resolver payload" is partially incoherent: pre-fetch defaults are deliberately platform-divergent (app/lib/project-tabs.ts:37-49 legacy PROJECT_TABS loading set vs landing tabs-client.ts:89-105 CHAT_TAB-only with documented anti-flicker rationale) and by definition can't come from the not-yet-fetched payload. FEASIBILITY: ws-envelope-parity.test.ts:18-32 imports server-module values cross-workspace under bun test, proving the import constraint binds only the RN bundle — so step-1 parity tests for tabs/engagement are near-free; chat-core is genuinely node-free and consumed by both bundlers (landing/chat-react/main.tsx:17; app/lib/chat-core/*). Riskiest behavior changes a cutover could introduce: (a) unifying the five option shapes could accidentally start passing `decoration` through the chat-bridge path (currently stripped at :436) or change chat-core's drop-malformed-frame posture (types.ts:422-427) — fixture-recorded decode tests needed; (b) Metro monorepo config for a new shared package is a known gotcha (chat-core precedent) but fails loud at bundle time; (c) flattening the deliberately-different pre-fetch tab defaults would visibly change first-paint behavior. Severity P1 is honest given the mission is extensibility: ca78517 empirically shows the 7-9-file per-feature tax.
verification.corrected_diagnosis: The chat wire protocol is server-defined (channels/adapters/app-ws/envelope.ts, 931 LOC), hand-mirrored in the Expo app (app/lib/ws-envelope.ts, 237 LOC, comment-mandated lockstep), independently re-decoded in chat-core (types.ts:429 normalizeInbound), and re-shaped again in landing/server.ts:232. Five near-identical option shapes exist (button-primitive.ts:59, envelope.ts:181, app mirror :98, chat-core ChatMessageOption :22, landing/server.ts:232) with a live lossy hand-mapping (chat-bridge.ts:435-437 silently drops `decoration` that ChatApp.tsx:350 renders when present). TabDescriptor is triple-declared with comment-only "byte-for-byte" lockstep and no parity test; AgentEngagementMode is mirrored (connect/agent-engagement.ts:37 vs app/lib/projects-client.ts:28) with zero tests and an ~11-file per-setting fanout. However: the two LARGEST mirrors (ws-envelope, and the 493-line doc-links mirror) DO have parity tests — the untested mirrors are the smaller, newer ones (tabs, engagement mode, work-board types), i.e., the parity-test discipline is eroding as surfaces are added, not absent. Twin fetch clients: exactly 4 exact twin pairs (docs 867/532, work-board 207/311 — self-described "web twin", tabs 115/180, project-credentials 150/178) plus 16+8 bespoke error classes; NOT "every gateway consumer" — mobile has ~15 clients with no web counterpart, so the tax applies per surface shipped on both fronts. Two different markdown algorithms confirmed (hand-rolled 908-LOC state machine vs react-markdown+rehype-sanitize 118 LOC) — but the mobile hand-roll is a DOCUMENTED deliberate decision ("no remark" bundle-size guard), and the real user-visible drift is that the web client renders citations/doc_refs nowhere (0 refs) while mobile has citation-chip-row.tsx. PR #149 arithmetic corrected: the second (web) copy was ~21% of insertions; both frontend copies together were ~45%, and roughly 1,100 of those 1,480 lines are platform-native UI that a shared client-core would NOT eliminate — the consolidatable layer is the fetch clients + types (~330 LOC in that PR). The mirrors exist because node-poisoned packages can't enter the RN bundle, but chat-core (node-free, consumed by both bundlers) proves the extraction pattern works, and ws-envelope-parity.test.ts proves cross-workspace imports already work in tests, so parity coverage is near-free today.
