verification.corrected_proposal: Step 1 unchanged and endorsed: add parity tests for TabDescriptor and AgentEngagementMode following the ws-envelope/doc-links pattern — note this is even cheaper than the critic implies, since app/__tests__/ws-envelope-parity.test.ts already imports server modules cross-workspace under bun test (the RN-bundle constraint doesn't apply to tests); also fix the stale parity-test path comment in app/lib/doc-links.ts:9. Step 2 (extract node-free @neutronai/wire-types, server re-exports first, clients cut over file-by-file, mirrors deleted last) is sound, with three added behavior-preservation guards: (a) record decode fixtures BEFORE unification — the canonical option type must preserve chat-bridge.ts:435's deliberate decoration-stripping on the landing path and chat-core's drop-malformed-frame posture (types.ts:422-427), or buttons/frames will render differently; (b) budget for Metro monorepo config (chat-core precedent: dep + allowImportingTsExtensions + metro config) — failure is loud, not silent; (c) keep the deliberately-divergent pre-fetch tab defaults (mobile legacy PROJECT_TABS vs web CHAT_TAB-only) as explicit per-client constants — DROP the sub-item deriving loading defaults from the resolver payload, since pre-fetch defaults by definition precede the payload and their divergence is documented anti-flicker behavior. Step 3 (shared client-core with one GatewayClientError) should be re-scoped honestly: it consolidates the 4 existing twin fetch clients and future ones (~1,000 LOC today), not the platform-native UI components (settings.tsx 640 vs SettingsTab.tsx 453 stay separate) — set expectations that the per-feature tax drops from ~9 files to ~6, not to 2. Renderer unification correctly stays a separate owner decision — flag that it contradicts the documented "no remark on mobile" bundle-size decision, so the owner is choosing between drift and bundle size, and that the cheaper immediate win is porting citations/doc_refs rendering to web (a feature gap, not a refactor). Depcruise node-free purity rule: keep as-is.
verification.behavior_risk: Highest risk is silent wire-semantics drift during option-shape canonicalization: (1) the chat-bridge landing path deliberately strips `decoration` (gateway/http/chat-bridge.ts:435-437) while the app-ws path carries it and ChatApp.tsx:350 renders it — a unified type with naive passthrough changes button styling on one surface; existing per-client tests would NOT catch this (no cross-path fixture test exists). (2) chat-core normalizeInbound's defensive drop-malformed-frame posture (chat-core/types.ts:422-427) vs the server decoder's validation caps (MAX_ATTACHMENTS_PER_MESSAGE etc.) — merging validators can flip a previously-rendered frame to dropped; only fixture-recorded decode tests catch it. (3) Flattening the deliberately-different pre-fetch tab defaults (app/lib/project-tabs.ts:37-49 vs landing tabs-client.ts:89-105) changes visible first-paint behavior; tabs-client tests test each client in isolation and would pass. (4) Metro/Vite bundling of the new shared package fails loud at build time (chat-core precedent required metro monorepo config) — annoying but not silent.

findings.title: Open-vs-Managed security-path forks sync by comment: two auth gates (the tested one dormant, the live one an anonymous closure with an internal duplicate block), two credential resolvers, a forked topics surface, and credential-scope policy smeared across three stores
findings.diagnosis: The tested landing/auth-gate.ts (655 LOC) is consumed via a composition field no production code in this repo sets; Open's real gate is the untested openFetch closure containing TWO verbatim copies of the claim-token+mint-cookie block, with cookie-mint logic additionally duplicated between compose.ts's stitch and a documented fork of landing/session-cookie.ts. resolveOpenLlmPool 'mirrors the Managed resolver's precedence' by comment against a 309-LOC 4-tier twin ('ambient' exists only in one, encoded nowhere as policy) — credentials are the highest-blast-radius config in the system and a tier change on either side silently diverges the other. open/chat-topics-surface forks the gateway surface (same route+wire, different data source). Credential-scope semantics live in three stores plus a hardcoded SERVICE_SCOPE literal table — reclassifying one credential's scope took a 19-file PR.
findings.proposal: Converge: (1) one auth-gate implementation behind the existing compose.ts AuthGate seam — extract openFetch's logic VERBATIM into a named, unit-tested OpenOwnerGate first, unify second; landing exports its own route manifest which compose.ts consumes, deleting LANDING_PATHS behind a generated==literal transition test; bootstrap HTML injection becomes a typed createLandingServer option instead of cross-package string-replace. (2) One precedence-table credential resolver parameterized by mode flags (allowAmbient, allowSharedEnvTier) beside credential-pool.ts, keeping both existing suites plus a table-driven tier×mode case enumeration; the ambient probe stays consulted only on the no-explicit-token branch. (3) Merge the topics surfaces behind an injected listTopics source (preserving Open's projects-table source verbatim — the fork exists because the other source rendered an empty sidebar). (4) One credential-policy module owning service→scope/store as data, never rekeying existing rows. Cookie semantics (append-never-replace, sliding refresh, single-use JTI, Secure-drop on loopback, stale-cookie cold-start) preserved byte-exact under the production-composer test family.
findings.evidence: landing/auth-gate.ts (zero non-test setters); open/composer.ts:1655-1760 (dup blocks :1713-1726,:1738-1749), :287-316, :3624-3632; gateway/http/compose.ts:894-948; realmode-composer/resolve-llm-credentials.ts; open/chat-topics-surface.ts vs gateway/http/chat-topics-surface.ts; git show 3c252c6 --stat (19 files); core-credential-resolver.ts:47-51

verification.notes:
Checked every citation at HEAD d30280c.

ACCURATE claims: (1) landing/auth-gate.ts is 655 LOC with a 38-test suite (landing/__tests__/auth-gate.test.ts), and its production consumption path — compose.ts:895-896 `evaluateAuthGate` when `authGate !== undefined` — is fed only by gateway/composition.ts:254-258 reading `composition.auth_gate`, which NO code in this repo ever sets (grep: only the read site + the type decl at gateway/composition/input/auth-input.ts:14). The tested gate is dormant in-repo; Managed wires it from outside. (2) Open's real gate is the `openFetch` closure at open/composer.ts:1655-1755, mounted as landing_server.fetch at composer.ts:3579-3580, and it does contain two near-verbatim copies of the claim-token→withReactBootstrap→conditional-mint-cookie async block at :1714-1723 (SPA deep-link branch, whose comment even says "identical to the /chat ?start= gate below") and :1738-1747 (/chat branch). (3) formatOwnerSetCookie at composer.ts:3617-3632 is a self-documented fork of landing/session-cookie.ts:formatSetCookie ("Mirrors ... but drops Secure on plain http loopback"). (4) resolveOpenLlmPool (composer.ts:287-316) syncs by comment: :248-252 says "mirroring the Managed resolver's precedence (gateway/realmode-composer/resolve-llm-credentials.ts...)". The Managed twin is exactly 309 LOC with tiers 0/0.5/1/2/3 (file header calls it "four sources" with an inserted 0.5), and 'ambient' appears nowhere in it — it exists only in Open's resolver. Precedence is encoded only in prose on both sides. (5) Topics fork confirmed: open/chat-topics-surface.ts (294 LOC) vs gateway/http/chat-topics-surface.ts (229 LOC), same route GET /api/v1/chat/topics, same wire shape by documented intent (open/chat-topics-surface.ts:24-25), different data source (projects table vs button_prompts) — the fork rationale is in composer.ts:1757-1765. (6) git show 3c252c6 --stat = exactly 19 files, and it IS a credential-scope reclassification (Codex per-project→global). (7) SERVICE_SCOPE hardcoded literal table at gateway/cores/core-credential-resolver.ts:46-50 (critic said 47-51, off-by-one). LANDING_PATHS exists at compose.ts:722 (exported :1401), so proposal step 1's manifest target is real.

INACCURATE claims (corrected): (a) "untested openFetch closure" is FALSE — openFetch's most security-critical behaviors have real integration coverage that boots the full Open composition over live Bun.serve: open/__tests__/open-start-token-single-use.test.ts (3 tests: first ?start= mints, replay of same token serves page but mints NO cookie), open-boot-shell.test.ts (5 tests), open-doc-deeplink-serve.test.ts (3 tests, the SPA mint-bounce branch). What's missing is a named, unit-testable extraction, not coverage. (b) "TWO verbatim copies" is slightly overstated — the blocks are structurally identical but not byte-identical (different Response variable, minted-check order). (c) "cookie-mint logic additionally duplicated between compose.ts's stitch and ... session-cookie.ts" mischaracterizes compose.ts:894-948 — that stitch appends the gate's decision.set_cookie; it does not mint. The real mint duplication is auth-gate/session-cookie formatSetCookie vs composer.ts's formatOwnerSetCookie Secure-drop fork.

SEVERITY: P1 is defensible but rests on divergence risk in security-critical config (proven blast radius: the 19-file scope-reclassification PR, and the original replayable-token bug lived exactly in this gate), NOT on absent test coverage as the diagnosis implies.

BEHAVIOR-PRESERVATION RISK of the proposal: riskiest is step (2), the merged credential resolver — the two resolvers consult genuinely different sources (Managed: DB OAuth source, ApiKeyStore, per-slug env, deployment-mode-gated shared env at resolve-llm-credentials.ts:35-43; Open: env-only + ambient probe), and a unified precedence table could silently change which credential resolves. Both suites exist (27 tests in gateway/realmode-composer/__tests__/resolve-llm-credentials.test.ts, 10 in open/__tests__/resolve-open-llm-pool.test.ts) and would likely catch tier reordering; the proposed tier×mode enumeration adds the cross-product. Second risk the critic did NOT flag: auth-gate.ts and resolve-llm-credentials.ts are production-wired only from the out-of-repo Managed overlay, so any semantic change to them cannot be integration-verified from this repo — only their unit suites protect the Managed deployment. Full gate unification (step 1 second half) is the most questionable piece: Open's gate is partly product flow (resumable-state cold-start at composer.ts:1686-1690, deep-link bounce), not just auth, and Managed's gate does signin-redirect/JWT work Open must never do; converging shared primitives is safer than converging the gates.

SIMPLER ALTERNATIVE (missed): the sharpest 80% is an S-effort in-file fix — extract one local serveWithStartTokenMint helper in composer.ts (kills both dup blocks; open-start-token-single-use + open-doc-deeplink-serve pin behavior), and upstream the Secure-drop into landing/session-cookie.ts:formatSetCookie behind an explicit option (kills the fork). Steps 2-4 stand as written but should be sequenced after that.
verification.corrected_diagnosis: Open-vs-Managed security paths sync by comment rather than by shared code. The tested landing/auth-gate.ts (655 LOC, 38-test suite) is consumed only via composition.auth_gate (gateway/composition.ts:254-258), which no production code in this repo sets — Open's live gate is the openFetch closure (open/composer.ts:1655-1755, mounted at :3579-3580). That closure has real integration coverage of its security-critical paths (open-start-token-single-use, open-doc-deeplink-serve, open-boot-shell boot the full composition over live Bun.serve) but is an unnamed anonymous closure containing two structurally-identical copies of the claim-token+mint-cookie block (:1714-1723, :1738-1747), plus formatOwnerSetCookie (:3617-3632), a documented fork of landing/session-cookie.ts:formatSetCookie that exists only to drop Secure on http loopback. resolveOpenLlmPool (:287-316) "mirrors the Managed resolver's precedence" purely by comment (:248-252) against the 309-LOC resolve-llm-credentials.ts 4-tier twin; 'ambient' exists only in the Open one and precedence is encoded nowhere as data, so a tier change on either side silently diverges the other — and the Managed side is production-wired only from the out-of-repo overlay, so divergence would not surface in this repo's integration tests. open/chat-topics-surface.ts forks gateway/http/chat-topics-surface.ts (same route + wire shape, different data source, deliberately — Managed's button_prompts source rendered an empty sidebar). Credential-scope semantics are smeared across ProjectCredentialStore, OAuthTokenManager, ApiKeyStore/SecretsStore plus the hardcoded SERVICE_SCOPE literal at core-credential-resolver.ts:46-50; reclassifying one credential's scope took a 19-file PR (3c252c6, verified).
verification.corrected_proposal: Sequence smallest-risk first. (0) S-effort in-file convergence that removes the sharpest duplication with near-zero behavior risk: extract one named serveWithStartTokenMint(res, url) helper inside open/composer.ts replacing both dup blocks (pinned by open-start-token-single-use.test.ts's replay-mints-no-cookie assertion and open-doc-deeplink-serve.test.ts), and upstream the Secure-drop-on-loopback into landing/session-cookie.ts:formatSetCookie behind an explicit option, deleting formatOwnerSetCookie. (1) Name and unit-test the extracted OpenOwnerGate; landing exports a route manifest that compose.ts consumes, deleting LANDING_PATHS behind a generated==literal transition test (compose.test.ts + compose-start-route.test.ts already pin routing edges); bootstrap HTML injection becomes a typed createLandingServer option. Do NOT attempt full unification of OpenOwnerGate with landing/auth-gate.ts — Open's gate embeds product flow (resumable-state cold-start, deep-link bounce) and Managed's embeds identity-signin redirects; converge only the shared primitives (start-token claim, cookie mint/verify), which mostly already are shared. (2) One precedence-table credential resolver parameterized by mode flags beside credential-pool.ts, keeping BOTH existing suites (27 + 10 tests) plus a table-driven tier×mode enumeration; ambient probe stays consulted only on the no-explicit-token branch; flag explicitly that the Managed wiring is out-of-repo, so this change ships only with the tier×mode table as its safety net. (3) Merge topics surfaces behind an injected listTopics source, preserving Open's projects-table source verbatim. (4) One credential-policy module owning service→scope/store as data, never rekeying existing rows. Cookie semantics (append-never-replace, sliding refresh, single-use JTI, Secure-drop on loopback, stale-cookie cold-start) preserved byte-exact under the existing production-composer test family.
verification.behavior_risk: Highest: credential-resolver unification silently changing which credential a boot resolves (tier reorder, ambient leaking into managed mode, or shared-env tier becoming reachable outside 'open' mode) — partially caught by the two existing suites, but the Managed production wiring is out-of-repo so only unit tests guard that side. Second: openFetch consolidation breaking an ordering subtlety (replay-serves-page-but-mints-no-cookie, stale-cookie-with-no-resumable-state cold-start, deep-link mint-bounce preserving path+query) — the existing open/__tests__ integration family covers these specific branches. Third: LANDING_PATHS→manifest changing the gated-route set (a route silently entering/leaving the auth gate).

findings.title: The 'reusable' agentic-action stack was forked by its first second consumer; the queued wow-moments work will rationally create a third parallel stack
findings.diagnosis: The wow-moments plan mandated reusing WowActionModule/ActionRunner and generalizing the selector; the shipped kickoff (#151) instead defined its own KickoffMatch/KickoffInput/KickoffResult contract importing NOTHING from the action stack — its own header explains the runner is welded to a button-prompt/cron channel adapter callers cannot supply, and WowActionContext is a ~15-field onboarding-shaped bag (interview, import_result, rituals, gmail_scopes...) no non-onboarding caller can construct. Plan Phase A (extract a pickActions core) was never executed. The contract fails the second-consumer test, and per-action registration currently costs 5 edit sites; the queued recurring per-project dispatcher would fork again.
findings.proposal: Extract the action contract, ActionRunner, and the selector's parse/validate/timeout/fallback core from onboarding/wow-moment into a neutral agentic-actions package with capability-scoped context slices (docs, tasks, reminders, emit) instead of one fat context; re-home the #151 kickoff onto it as the proving consumer; collapse per-action registration into a single module-registry entry. Import-motion only: existing llm-selector/dispatcher tests pass unchanged, 07-first/01-last ordering, the 60s hang timeout, prompt serialization, telemetry event shapes, and the onboarding_opening:<id> dedupe slot preserved byte-identically.
findings.evidence: gateway/realmode-composer/build-project-kickoff.ts:27-29,74-142 (own contract, zero action-stack imports); onboarding/wow-moment/action-types.ts:132-179 (context bag); grep pickActions llm-selector.ts = none; catalogue.ts:16-18 (5 edit sites per action)

verification.notes: Verified at HEAD. (1) Fork confirmed: build-project-kickoff.ts:74-126 defines its own KickoffMatch/KickoffInput/KickoffResult/ProjectKickoff contract; imports (lines 50-64) include zero action-stack modules (only wow-moment/project-materializer.ts types + project-identity.ts namesRelate); header lines 27-29 explicitly document skipping ActionRunner because the plain-emit finalize path has no channel adapter. (2) Context-bag claim confirmed with corrections: WowActionContext is action-types.ts:132-191 (not 132-179) and has ~20 fields (not ~15), including required onboarding-shaped fields a non-onboarding caller cannot supply: channel:WowChannelAdapter (:172), cron_jobs (:166), cron_state (:168), db (:170), reminders (:157), interview (:137), stalled_threads (:154), gmail_scopes (:155). Precision: ActionRunner itself is context-agnostic (action-runner.ts:37-58,103 — takes telemetry+module+ctx); the weld is the required ctx fields, not the runner. (3) grep pickActions over llm-selector.ts = zero hits; pickWowActions (llm-selector.ts:125) hardwires buildUserPayload (:299) + onboarding prompt path (:106); plan Phase A (docs/plans/2026-06-30-agentic-per-project-wow-moments-plan.md:165-167) never executed — confirmed. (4) catalogue.ts:16-18 documents exactly the 5 per-action edit sites — confirmed. (5) Two factual corrections: (a) the kickoff selects actions DETERMINISTICALLY (assessSignal + hasWorkSignal/hasInterestSignal gates, build-project-kickoff.ts:216-260) — it duplicates NO LLM-selector parse/validate/timeout/fallback code, so that part of the diagnosis overstates the duplication; (b) timeline/framing: the plan is dated 2026-06-30 (DESIGN, still untracked in git) and PR #151 merged 2026-07-01 executing a SEPARATE brief whose HARD RULES explicitly forbade the recurring machinery (build-project-kickoff.ts:15-20), so #151 is a documented scoping decision, not a plan violation; and the queued plan itself already mandates the reuse/generalization (§2.1 'Do not fork the action model', §2.5 generalize-don't-clone) — the finding partially restates declared queued design work. The 'third parallel stack' claim is a prediction the plan explicitly plans against, though #151's precedent (fork-with-documented-reason when the context can't be constructed) gives it real weight, and the plan's own B1 hedges on exactly the fat-context question ('extend WowActionContext... OR a project_opening-scoped context'). (6) Behavior risks in the proposal the critic missed: re-homing the kickoff onto ActionRunner collides the kickoff composer's 90s budget (KICKOFF_COMPOSE_TIMEOUT_MS=90_000, build-project-kickoff-composer.ts:34,70) with the runner's 60s hard timeout (DEFAULT_ACTION_TIMEOUT_MS=60_000, action-runner.ts:61) — rich projects whose doc compose takes 60-90s would silently degrade from draft-doc to the deterministic opening; the runner records onboarding.wow_action_fired on EVERY path (action-runner.ts:114,130,155) which the kickoff today never emits (new telemetry rows unless a no-op telemetry is injected); the kickoff's hobby error contract (compose-fail → interest-questions, never null, build-project-kickoff.ts:160-174,198-207) does not map onto the runner's fired/reason model without a wrapper. 'Import-motion only' is false for the context-slices half: fallbackPick invokes mod.triggerCondition(fallback_ctx) with the full context (llm-selector.ts:261-285, esp. :274), so slicing rewrites all 7 action module signatures + dispatcher + tests. Tests exist that would catch selector/runner regressions (onboarding/wow-moment/__tests__/llm-selector.test.ts, action-runner.test.ts, dispatcher.test.ts, catalogue.test.ts; gateway/realmode-composer/__tests__/build-project-kickoff.test.ts) but none would catch the 60s-vs-90s timeout downgrade (it only bites on slow real LLM calls) or the new telemetry rows. Severity: P1 is inflated — the remedy for the core duplication is already designed as Phase A of the queued plan, blast radius today is one well-documented ~460-line module with its own tests, and there is no correctness impact; P2 is honest.
verification.corrected_diagnosis: The onboarding wow-action stack failed its second-consumer test, and the failure point is precisely located: ActionRunner itself is orchestration-agnostic (action-runner.ts:37-58), but WowActionContext (action-types.ts:132-191, ~20 fields) requires onboarding-shaped and channel-shaped members (channel: WowChannelAdapter, cron_jobs, cron_state, db, reminders, interview, stalled_threads, gmail_scopes) that a plain-emit caller cannot construct — so the #151 kickoff (a deliberately descoped one-time brief, not a violation of the wow-moments plan, which is queued DESIGN work dated one day earlier) rationally defined its own KickoffInput/KickoffResult contract with zero action-stack imports (build-project-kickoff.ts:27-29,50-64,74-126), documenting the context weld as the reason. The kickoff does NOT duplicate the LLM selector (its action choice is deterministic gates, build-project-kickoff.ts:216-260); the duplicated concepts are the trigger/run action shape, the never-throw degrade contract, and a sibling doc-composer. Plan Phase A (extract a pickActions core from llm-selector.ts) is unexecuted (no such export exists), per-action registration costs 5 edit sites (catalogue.ts:16-18), and the queued recurring dispatcher's plan hedges on the fat-context problem (Phase B1: 'extend WowActionContext OR a scoped context') — so without deciding the context question first, the third consumer plausibly forks again despite the plan's stated reuse mandate.
verification.corrected_proposal: Split into two motions with different risk profiles. Motion 1 (true import-motion, safe): execute plan Phase A verbatim — extract pickActions(input,deps) (JSON-envelope parse, validatePick, withTimeout, predicate fallback, llm-selector.ts:201-285,329-343) into a neutral module with pickWowActions as a thin onboarding wrapper; existing llm-selector/dispatcher tests must pass unchanged. Collapse the 5 registration edit sites by deriving WowActionId from the registry keys and generating the candidate list, keeping telemetry row values byte-identical. Motion 2 (interface change, do NOT sell as import-motion): introduce capability-scoped context slices (docs, tasks, reminders, emit) as an ADDITIVE composition — define slice interfaces the existing fat WowActionContext structurally satisfies, so current action modules keep compiling against the fat type while new consumers (the queued per-project dispatcher, a re-homed kickoff) take only the slices they need; do not rewrite the 7 existing modules' signatures in the same PR. Do NOT re-home the #151 kickoff onto ActionRunner as-is: it would (a) clamp the kickoff's 90s compose budget (build-project-kickoff-composer.ts:34) to the runner's 60s default timeout (action-runner.ts:61), silently degrading slow-but-successful draft-doc kickoffs to deterministic openings, (b) add onboarding.wow_action_fired telemetry rows the kickoff never emits today, and (c) not represent the hobby compose-fail→questions degrade path. If the kickoff is re-homed, it needs action_timeout_ms>=90s, an injected no-op telemetry (or an explicit product decision to start emitting), and a wrapper preserving the null/questions fallback semantics — each an observable behavior change requiring sign-off, contradicting the no-functionality-change ground rule. Cheapest honest scope: Motion 1 + slice interfaces now; make the queued dispatcher (not the shipped kickoff) the proving second consumer, since it has a channel and matches the runner's semantics.
verification.behavior_risk: Highest-risk behavior change: re-homing the kickoff onto ActionRunner silently converts 60-90s doc composes from success to reason:'timeout' (60s runner default vs 90s composer budget), degrading rich-project kickoffs to the deterministic opening — no existing test catches this (build-project-kickoff.test.ts stubs the composer; the collision only manifests with real LLM latency). Second: new onboarding.wow_action_fired telemetry rows from the runner on kickoff paths that emit nothing today. Third: context-slicing the 7 existing modules risks perturbing trigger predicates evaluated on the selector's deterministic fallback path (llm-selector.ts:274) — the load-bearing fallback that makes actions still fire when the cold CC-spawn picker times out; llm-selector.test.ts/dispatcher.test.ts would catch gross breakage but not subtle field-availability drift. Load-bearing subtleties to preserve byte-identically: 07-first/01-last ordering (catalogue.ts:37,40,83), timeout-never-retries (action-runner.ts:187-201), retry only for 01/02 (action-runner.ts:81-84), kickoff never-throws + hobby-gets-questions-never-null (build-project-kickoff.ts:160-207), and the onboarding_opening:<project_id> dedupe slot (build-onboarding-finalize.ts:424).

findings.title: Identity vocabulary: the real debt is project_slug-meaning-OWNER (6,494 refs) + internal_handle (855 refs) guarded only by comments after a real credential-loss incident — and two live 'tenant' tokens defeat the zero-tolerance leak-gate
findings.diagnosis: The declared '441+ internal_handle/TenantsRegistry' debt is stale — the tenant WORD purge is done. What remains: 'project_slug' means the owner/instance slug across 719 files, ~25 SQL tables, the GraphComposer contract, and the healthz wire shape, colliding head-on with real projects (Task rows carry both columns); internal_handle is the frozen twin whose misuse already caused the documented 2026-05-12 production credential loss, currently protected by prose only. Two live tenant tokens even defeat the CI gate: tasks/history-import-seeder.ts hashes a `tenant:` literal followed by a raw NUL byte (grep's binary heuristic skips the whole file — a naive 'fix' would change every history-import task id and break idempotent re-seed), and a core sidecar migration ships research_tasks_tenant_project_status_idx through a word-boundary + allowlist-scope gap. The NUL bypass generalizes to every grep-based gate in the repo.
findings.proposal: Now (S): fix the leak-gate bypasses — replace the raw NUL with the two-character \x00 escape (byte-identical hash input, pinned by a golden hash test) and LOCK the tenant: literal as an allowlisted hash-seed constant (it is data, not vocabulary); rename the research index via an idempotent sidecar migration; add a gate tripwire failing any tracked source file grep classifies as binary. Now (S): branded OwnerHandle/InternalHandle types at the SecretsStore/ApiKeyStore/ProjectCredentialStore boundaries + a glossary. Then the staged rename (owner_handle for the frozen handle, owner_slug for instance-sense project_slug), sequenced AFTER the kill-list shrinks the sweep: in-repo non-ABI sweep first; ABI surfaces with a dual-accept {owner_handle ?? internal_handle} window coordinated via the composer-ABI snapshot test; TS identifiers only — SQL strings, healthz field, start-token claims, NEUTRON_INSTANCE_SLUG, and .url_slug stay byte-identical, with SQL column renames deferred indefinitely (secrets-store precedent: column frozen, TS honest); leak-gate rule banning new internal_handle.
findings.evidence: gateway/index.ts:184; boot-helpers.ts:362; open/composer.ts:405; tasks/store.ts (Task{project_slug, project_id}); auth/secrets-store.ts:8-27,196; tasks/history-import-seeder.ts:63-64 (NUL at offset 2097); scripts/ci/leak-gate.sh:107-109,145-155; cores/free/research/migrations/0001_research_claims.sql:38; mount-open-cores.ts:183-264 (one value, four names)

verification.notes: Verified at HEAD d30280c. (1) Counts: internal_handle=856 refs (claimed 855), project_slug=6,589 refs/718 files (claimed 6,494/719) — trivial drift; TenantsRegistry=0 refs, so the declared '441+ internal_handle/TenantsRegistry' debt is confirmed half-stale. (2) project_slug-means-owner confirmed at gateway/index.ts:184 (const project_slug = resolveOwnerSlug()), gateway/boot-helpers.ts:362 (GraphComposer contract {db, project_slug}), gateway/index.ts:474-486 (healthz JSON {status:'ok', project_slug, uptime_ms}), tasks/store.ts:116-118 (Task carries BOTH project_slug and project_id), cores/sdk/auth.ts:16-71 (expected_project_slug in the cores JWT-validator contract — an SDK ABI the critic didn't cite). mount-open-cores.ts:183-264 confirmed 'one value, four names': internal_handle (l.183), owner_slug (~215), project_slug (~252), slug_suffix (~264), all = input.project_slug. (3) 2026-05-12 credential-loss incident documented at auth/secrets-store.ts:10-26; contract is prose-only — SELECT at lines 195-201 binds input.internal_handle to the frozen project_slug column; grep found NO branded types anywhere. (4) NUL bypass REPRODUCED: leak-gate.sh:107-108 uses grep -EnHI (-I skips binary); tasks/history-import-seeder.ts:63-64 has raw NULs at byte offsets 2097 and 2140 inside the hash-seed template literals; running the full gate on a clean `git archive HEAD` export returns SILENT/0 findings despite the tenant: literal — the gate's own comment (leak-gate.sh:145-155, 'the public tree carries ZERO tenant anywhere in code') is false at HEAD. (5) Word-boundary gap confirmed: rule '(^|[^a-z0-9_])tenant' (l.155) cannot match research_tasks_tenant_project_status_idx (cores/free/research/migrations/0001_research_claims.sql:38, preceded by '_'), and allowlist glob migrations/* (scripts/ci/leak-gate-allowlist.txt) does not cover cores/free/research/migrations/. (6) CRITIC UNDERCOUNTED: a THIRD live token at runtime/adapters/claude-code/persistent/__tests__/wedge-detector.test.ts:124 ('/srv/neutron/tenants/foo bar/home') — it WOULD match the word-bounded rule but the file carries 4 NUL bytes (garbled-terminal fixtures, first at offset 4707) so it is also binary-skipped: a second independent instance proving the 'NUL bypass generalizes' claim. (7) Test gap confirmed: tasks/__tests__/history-import-seeder.test.ts:61-90 asserts only determinism + /^hi_[a-f0-9]{24}$/ format, no pinned golden hex — a botched escape edit (e.g. \\x00 double-escape) would pass tests while changing every hi_* task id. (8) Proposal sloppiness: no 'composer-ABI snapshot test' exists (zero hits for word 'abi' in open/ + gateway/); the real anchors are the many gateway/__tests__/*-production-composer.test.ts wiring tests. Severity P1/effort XL is honest for a refactor-mission audit: largest naming debt in the tree, one documented production incident of exactly the class a sweep could re-trigger, and the enforcement backstop demonstrably bypassed by 3 live tokens.
verification.corrected_diagnosis: As the critic states, with one material correction: there are THREE live tenant tokens defeating the zero-tolerance gate, not two. Besides (a) tasks/history-import-seeder.ts:63-64 (tenant: hash-seed literal hidden by raw NULs at offsets 2097/2140 → grep -I binary skip) and (b) cores/free/research/migrations/0001_research_claims.sql:38 (research_tasks_tenant_project_status_idx evading the left word-boundary '[^a-z0-9_]' via the preceding underscore, outside the migrations/* allowlist scope), there is (c) runtime/adapters/claude-code/persistent/__tests__/wedge-detector.test.ts:124 — a '/srv/neutron/tenants/foo bar/home' fixture path that WOULD trip the word-bounded rule but is hidden because the file's garbled-terminal fixtures contain 4 raw NUL bytes, making it a second independent instance of the binary-skip bypass. Verified end-to-end: the full gate run on a clean git-archive of HEAD is SILENT while all three tokens are present, directly contradicting the gate's own 'ZERO tenant anywhere in code' claim at leak-gate.sh:145-155. All other cited facts check out (counts within ~1.5% drift at HEAD; secrets-store prose-only contract with frozen project_slug column bound to input.internal_handle at auth/secrets-store.ts:195-201; healthz wire shape gateway/index.ts:474-486; GraphComposer contract boot-helpers.ts:362; one-value-four-names in mount-open-cores.ts:183-264; plus cores/sdk/auth.ts:16-71 expected_project_slug — a published-SDK ABI surface the critic did not cite that further constrains the rename).
verification.corrected_proposal: Keep the critic's plan with four corrections. (1) The binary tripwire will immediately flag wedge-detector.test.ts as well — escape its 4 fixture NULs to \x00 string escapes (runtime-byte-identical) and decide the '/srv/neutron/tenants/' fixture path (rename to a neutral path or allowlist by exact file; it is a test fixture, renaming is behavior-safe). (2) The seeder fix must escape BOTH NULs (offsets 2097 AND 2140 — the project: separator too), and the golden-hash test must be committed in the SAME change as the escape edit, pinning the exact current hi_* hex for a fixed input, because the existing test (tasks/__tests__/history-import-seeder.test.ts:61-90) only checks determinism and would pass a hash-changing botch. (3) The research-index sidecar migration (DROP INDEX IF EXISTS old + CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS new — SQLite has no ALTER INDEX; indexes are semantics-free so this is behavior-preserving) does NOT remove the old token from the immutable 0001 file text; pair it with either an allowlist entry scoped to cores/*/migrations/* or acceptance that the current left-boundary regex never sees it — decide explicitly rather than silently. (4) There is no 'composer-ABI snapshot test' — anchor the dual-accept rename window to the existing gateway/__tests__/*-production-composer.test.ts wiring-test suite (and add a dedicated contract test if none pins the {owner_handle ?? internal_handle} seam). Also worth stating as an explicit decision point: the branded OwnerHandle/InternalHandle types + glossary + gate fixes (all S) deliver most of the incident-prevention value; the 6.5k-ref mechanical rename (XL) can be deferred indefinitely behind them without losing safety — the critic's sequencing already permits this but should name it as a sanctioned stopping point.
verification.behavior_risk: Riskiest: any byte change to historyImportTaskHash's seed string (the tenant:/project: literals or their NUL separators) silently changes every hi_* task id and breaks idempotent history-import re-seed — duplicate task rows on re-import — and the existing test suite would NOT catch it (no golden hash pinned). Second: renaming project_slug on wire/persisted surfaces (healthz JSON gateway/index.ts:486, cores/sdk/auth.ts expected_project_slug JWT contract, start-token claims, SQL columns) would break live clients/cores/sessions mid-rollout — the proposal correctly freezes these byte-identical. Third: any slug-vs-handle mixup during the internal_handle sweep at the SecretsStore/ApiKeyStore boundary re-creates the documented 2026-05-12 credential-invisibility incident (silent null reads, chat drops to gate page) — this is exactly what the branded-types step mitigates, and no current test guards it.

findings.title: Agent-facing sources of truth are wrong: the Forge/Argus contract exists in four copies (the disk files agents edit are NOT the live one), a LIVE prompt instructs agents to run nonexistent scripts, three AS-BUILT changelogs, stale plans/AGENTS.md/README diagram
findings.diagnosis: In a self-building repo, stale orientation text is bad model input. Verified truth table: editing prompts/forge.md silently does nothing — the LIVE v2 contract is a separate inline copy in inner-workflow.mjs, with a diverging production-dead fork in code-gen and dead render/parse halves in trident/prompts.ts despite its 'single-source guarantee' claim; the LIVE reminder-patterns.md tells fire-time agents to post via tg-post.sh and fetch weather.sh — Vajra scripts absent from this repo (~18 ghost scripts/** references tree-wide, including an operator-facing boot message). THREE AS-BUILT changelogs (10,728 lines) exist because two writer prompts hardcode different filenames. Both 'queued' plans still read PLAN-ONLY after substantially shipping (inviting double-builds by the very agents this audit serves); README's five-layer diagram omits 12 modules, lists landing twice, and is violated by half the measured edges; AGENTS.md files describe empty P0 skeletons for 71k-LOC directories. The /code grammar and board-binding rules are also duplicated with dead halves.
findings.proposal: Single-source per prompt role: inner-workflow.mjs reads forge.md/argus.md at fire time via plain fs (byte-diff the rendered text before/after; keep the fail-soft fallback), delete the code-gen fork with its retired pipeline and the three dead prompt files + KNOWN_PROMPTS entries + parity-test rows in one PR, pinned by prompts-disk-source-style tests per role. Converge changelogs on docs/AS_BUILT.md in strict order: writer prompts first (or the next Ralph run recreates the root file), archive root content, move the leak-gate allowlist entries with the rename. Rewrite reminder-patterns.md delivery instructions to the substrate-native path (behavior-adjacent — verify one real fire) and fix the ghost-script operator message. Replace the README diagram with the measured 5-band model as a GENERATED artifact (depcruise archi) so it cannot rot; stamp plan-status headers (pending | building | shipped(#PR) | superseded-by) and rewrite the three lying AGENTS.md files post-refactor. Keep one parseCodeCommand and extract shared enforceBoardBinding(); add the trident inner-workflow execution shim + nightly fire→harvest smoke so the 771-line build-loop centerpiece is no longer verified only by source-string assertions.
findings.evidence: trident/prompts.ts:80-98,89,275; inner-workflow.mjs:281,330,361; cores/free/code-gen/src/prompts/forge-system.ts:7,24; prompts/reminder-patterns.md:50-217 + absent scripts/{tg-post,weather}.sh; boot-helpers.ts:205; wc -l AS-BUILT trio = 7441/1469/1818; docs/plans/*-plan.md:5 (PLAN-ONLY after shipping); README.md:241-266; inner-workflow.test.ts:1-11 (119 toContain, 0 execution)

verification.notes: Verified every load-bearing claim at HEAD. (1) Four-copy contract: live Forge/Argus contract is inline in trident/inner-workflow.mjs:314-326 (forgeBuildContract) + :332 (ARGUS_RUBRIC); trident/prompts.ts:83 claims a "single-source guarantee" and does read prompts/forge.md fresh (prompts.ts:89, :275), but that render/parse half is production-dead: gateway/composition/build-core-modules.ts:415-418 wires the tick loop with stubAdvanceDeps() (classify always 'running') and only the orchestrator fires inner-workflow.mjs, so session.ts:209/232 parse calls never run live. Editing forge.md silently does nothing to live fires — confirmed. code-gen fork confirmed diverging (cores/free/code-gen/src/prompts/forge-system.ts:25 contract: always draft gh PR) and effectively dead: gateway/boot-helpers.ts:1128-1149 says codegen_core only backs four legacy codegen_* MCP tools via a "SKELETON runner" with no real orchestrator. (2) Ghost scripts: 17 refs across prompts/{reminder-patterns,reminder-agent-base,topic-agent-base}.md vs scripts/ containing only ci/lib/run-tests.sh; boot-helpers.ts:205 operator warn cites nonexistent scripts/install/regenerate-owner-slug-dropin.sh. reminder-patterns.md IS live model input: gateway/boot-helpers.ts:728,948 reads it raw, smart-wrap Shape C persists the pattern body verbatim into the stored message (cores/free/reminders/src/smart-wrap.ts:166-199), and reminders/prompt.ts:87-88 embeds shape.block into the fire-time prompt. Nuance: the live prompt wrapper forces compose-only output ("Scope: you ONLY compose a message... Output the message text only"), so delivery is NOT broken — it is contradictory model input plus a genuinely-dead weather-context step. (3) Changelogs: wc -l = 7441/1469/1818 (10,728 total) confirmed; writer split confirmed (trident/prompts.ts:116,199 + inner-workflow.mjs:361 say root AS-BUILT.md; forge-system.ts:7 references AS_BUILT.md). (4) Plans: both 2026-06-30 plans read PLAN-ONLY/Plan-only (settings plan :5, wow-moments :3) while AS-BUILT.md:337 records "2026-07-01 — Per-project Settings tab + credential system (FOUNDATION)" shipping; note both plan files are UNTRACKED in git. (5) README.md:241-266: landing/ listed twice (lines 243 and 262) and exactly 12 top-level modules omitted (trident, open, agent-dispatch, chat-core, doc-search, jwt-validator, message-search, project-credentials, reflection, skill-forge, tabs, work-board) — confirmed by dir enumeration; the "half the measured edges violated" claim I could not verify. (6) AGENTS.md: gateway/AGENTS.md (7 lines) says "P0 ships only the empty-but-correct skeleton" over ~128k LOC (incl. tests); cores/AGENTS.md and onboarding/AGENTS.md similar; the "71k-LOC" figure is off but order-of-magnitude right. (7) Tests: inner-workflow.test.ts has 115 toContain (critic said 119) and 0 executions; header (lines 1-11) documents the Workflow-runtime-globals constraint. prompts-disk-source.test.ts already exists as the style the critic proposes. (8) /code grammar duplicated: trident/code-command.ts:57 (live via gateway/boot-helpers.ts:633) vs cores/free/code-gen/src/chat-commands.ts:109 (dead half). (9) Three dead prompt files confirmed: loadPrompt is only ever called in production with forge/argus (trident/prompts.ts) and atlas/sentinel (trident/agent-prompts.ts:117); topic-agent-base.md, reminder-agent-base.md, scribe.md sit in KNOWN_PROMPTS (prompts/template.ts:139-148) but are only "ported inline" as comments (reminders/prompt.ts:4, scribe/extract.ts:10). Note a same-day refactor plan already exists covering parts of this (docs/plans/2026-07-02-world-class-refactor-plan.md:318-323).
verification.corrected_diagnosis: Diagnosis stands with three precision fixes. (a) The reminder ghost-script claim should read: the live fire path embeds pattern bodies verbatim (boot-helpers.ts:728/948 → smart-wrap Shape C → reminders/prompt.ts:88), but the fire-time wrapper is compose-only and delivery is dispatcher-side, so the tg-post.sh/weather.sh text is contradictory model input and a dead weather-context capability — user-visible delivery is NOT broken. (b) The code-gen fork is not fully production-dead: it is reachable via four legacy codegen_* MCP tools, but they dispatch into a skeleton runner with no real orchestrator (boot-helpers.ts:1128-1149), so it is behaviorally inert. (c) Minor: 115 toContain not 119; AGENTS.md directories are ~128k LOC (gateway incl. tests) not 71k; both stale plan files are untracked in git, and the wow-moments plan's kickoff portion shipped via PR #151. The dead-copy topology is otherwise exactly as described, and the root AS-BUILT.md / docs/AS_BUILT.md writer split is real.
verification.corrected_proposal: Keep everything except the centerpiece step, which should be INVERTED. Making inner-workflow.mjs read forge.md/argus.md at fire time is not the mechanical redirect the critic implies: (1) the live inline contracts are heavily parameterized (isPr/local-mode branching, reenter/resume notes, FORGE_SCHEMA structured reporting, the planner/executor Ralph split at inner-workflow.mjs:355-365, argus synthesis) and differ materially from today's forge.md/argus.md bodies, so "byte-diff before/after" first requires rewriting the md files into multi-variant templates — a content migration inside the one component with zero execution tests; (2) it is unverified that the CC Dynamic Workflow runtime permits node:fs imports in the workflow script, and the prompts/ dir path would have to be threaded via args since the workflow runs in a per-run worktree cwd. Safer single-source-per-role: declare inner-workflow.mjs the canonical build-loop contract; delete the dead prompts.ts render/parse half, session.ts state-machine consumers behind stubAdvanceDeps, the code-gen prompt fork + skeleton-runner MCP-tool path, and the dead chat-commands.ts parseCodeCommand; reduce prompts/forge.md+argus.md to pointer stubs (or delete + KNOWN_PROMPTS entries) so agents can no longer edit a file that does nothing — zero live-path changes. Changelog convergence: correct as sequenced, but note inner-workflow.mjs:361's AS-BUILT instruction is a TARGET-REPO Ralph convention (trident builds arbitrary Projects/<slug>/code repos), so either keep root AS-BUILT.md as the generic target-repo convention and converge only neutron-open's own three files, or make the path repo-relative — do not blindly point the writer prompt at docs/AS_BUILT.md. Reminder fix: strip delivery/context-fetch steps from pattern bodies rather than "rewrite to substrate-native delivery" (there is no agent-side delivery in Open's reminder path), preserving the PATTERN: headers and FILL: slot markers that message-shape.ts:51 and smart-wrap slot-filling depend on; existing stored Shape-C reminders keep old bodies (persisted at create time), so the change is forward-only. Build the fire→harvest smoke BEFORE any prompt-text consolidation, not after.
verification.behavior_risk: Riskiest change: any consolidation that alters the rendered Forge/Argus prompt text — the harvest path is parser-locked on terminal lines (PR_NUMBER=/BRANCH=/WORKTREE=, APPROVE/REQUEST CHANGES) and on FORGE_SCHEMA structured fields, and the only tests over inner-workflow.mjs are 115 source-string toContain assertions with zero execution (inner-workflow.test.ts:1-11), which pin the .mjs source, not fire-time rendered text — a disk-read redirect that renders slightly different text would pass every existing test and break harvest silently (runs wedge at inner_checkpoint, no PR delivered). Second risk: changing the AS-BUILT writer instruction changes agent behavior in every trident-built target repo, not just neutron-open. Third: rewriting reminder-patterns.md bodies without preserving PATTERN:/FILL: markers breaks Shape-C creation (UnknownReminderPatternError path) — partially covered by smart-wrap/message-shape unit tests, which use stub loaders and would NOT catch a malformed on-disk file; a template.test.ts sync check exists for KNOWN_PROMPTS deletions.
